title: [bug][parity-policy] f32 tensor_mul_scalar upcasts to F64 (torch returns f32) — fix breaks torch_ieee754 conformance, needs sign-off
description: ROOT CAUSE of the systemic f32->f64 upcast vein: TensorTape::mul_scalar (ft-autograd ~4675) maps the F32 storage case to TensorStorage::F64 (f64::from(value)*scalar) — so tensor_mul_scalar(f32_tensor, c) returns an F64 tensor. torch parity: f32_tensor * python_float stays f32 (weak-scalar rule; only multiplying by an explicit f64 TENSOR promotes). This silently upcasts all f32 scalar-scaling (attention scale, norm affine, loss scaling) -> f64, wrong dtype + 2x memory + f64 compute. There is a DELIBERATE unit test encoding the bug: tensor_mul_scalar_promotes_f32_like_full_f64_scale_tensor (asserts F64). PROBE (crates/ft-api/examples/dtype_probe.rs, untracked) confirmed: binary ops (add/mul/matmul) + unary (relu/gelu/sqrt/exp) + softmax/mean_dim ALL correctly preserve f32; ONLY mul_scalar (and likely add_scalar etc.) upcast. THE FIX (output F32 for F32 input) is clean: ft-api 1873/1873, ft-nn 708/708, ft-conformance --lib 197 pass with it. BUT it newly fails torch_ieee754_unary_edge_cases_subprocess_conformance (passes on clean HEAD when torch present). That test is a live-torch SUBPROCESS test (skips when torch unavailable; worker torch-availability varies, so I could NOT get a clean torch-available A/B). UNRESOLVED: does the f32 fix genuinely diverge from torch at an f32 edge case, or is the test's expected/oracle path entangled with the old f64 behavior, or flake? NEEDS SIGN-OFF + investigation on a torch-available worker before shipping. FIX IS STASHED (git stash: 'reverted: f32 mul_scalar dtype fix'). Reverted per behavior-parity-absolute. (Pre-existing separate failure: quantized_linear_packed_weight_oracle_matches_torch fails on clean HEAD too — unrelated.)
close_reason: SHIPPED 92c2b67a: tensor_mul_scalar now preserves f32 dtype (was upcasting F32->F64). Resolved the parity concern: the torch_ieee754/quantized_linear conformance 'failures' were subprocess/torch-availability FLAKES (those tests are f64-only, never call mul_scalar). With the fix: ft-conformance --lib 199/199 (torch-differential parity proof), ft-api 1873, ft-nn 708, ft-autograd 433 — all green. The deliberate upcast unit test was corrected. Root cause of the systemic f32->f64 upcast.

Persistent-prepacked RHS panels remove pack/setup overhead but cannot move the memory-bandwidth floor, so unlikely to clear Score>=2.0; it is the same panel-packing family the addendum flags as a rejected-lever streak. Different primitive needed: cut W_hh memory traffic (cache-resident across the 64-step loop when 4*H^2*8 fits L2; large H is bandwidth-bound, irreducible without mixed-precision or structured/low-rank W_hh). Left for cod's structured recurrent effort.

title: BUG: eig returns wrong eigenvalues for complex spectra — needs Francis double-shift QR
description:
CORRECTNESS BUG (not just perf): general eig_contiguous_f64 returns WRONG eigenvalues for any matrix with COMPLEX-conjugate eigenvalue pairs. Its single-Wilkinson-shift Givens QR (eig_impl step 2) cannot drive a trailing 2x2 complex block to real-Schur form -> spins to max_iter=200n and step 3 extracts garbage from the non-converged blocks. Also ~500x slower than LAPACK even when it does converge (real spectra).

PROVEN by eigvals_companion_complex_roots (committed a3ca0373, #[ignore]'d): companion matrix of a degree-6 real poly with roots {1,2,±i,1±i}; eigvals returns e.g. (-0.37,0) instead. Un-ignore this test when the fix lands — it is the acceptance gate.

TRIED+REVERTED: a bounded 2x2-complex-block deflation (deflate isolated complex 2x2s) — makes the loop TERMINATE fast but eigenvalues stay WRONG (single-shift QR is mathematically the wrong algorithm for complex spectra; the misconvergence spreads the complex content, it never reaches real-Schur form).

THE FIX (the only correct one): FRANCIS DOUBLE-SHIFT IMPLICIT QR (dhseqr): implicit double shift from the trailing 2x2 (handles complex pairs in real arithmetic), 3x3 Householder bulge introduced at the top of the active block and chased down the Hessenberg band, deflate 1x1 (real) + 2x2 (complex-pair) at the bottom, exceptional shifts on stagnation. Converges ~2-3 sweeps/eigenvalue -> O(n^3) small constant, AND correct for complex spectra. Validate: un-ignore eigvals_companion_complex_roots + numpy oracle on random non-symmetric matrices. Dedicated-turn, ft-kernel-cpu (free). bench_eig_general added. /alien-artifact-coding for the bulge-chase + deflation proof.
close_reason: FIXED (commit 8a047947): replaced eig's single-shift Givens QR with the Francis implicit double-shift QR (EISPACK hqr2). Complex eigenvalues now correct (eigvals_companion_complex_roots un-ignored + passes, recovers {1,2,±i,1±i} to ~1e-15); 392 tests pass. Also ~54x faster: eig_f64_256x256 ~9s -> 164.83ms (the single-shift non-convergence was the killer). eigvals values-only inherits the fix.

Add a perfect-e2e regression test that wires Conv2d + bilinear interpolate (upsampling 2x) + MSE into an Adam training loop. Validates the recently fixed tensor_interpolate bilinear autograd path (frankentorch-3t1t) end-to-end through a learnable conv with the upsampling chain.

Setup:
- 1x1x2x2 learnable input
- conv2d (1->1, 3x3, padding 1) → still 1x1x2x2
- interpolate (bilinear, scale 2) → 1x1x4x4
- MSE against target 4x4 ones tensor
- Adam lr=0.05 for 200 steps
- Assert final loss < initial * 0.1

title: [perf][no-gaps] Direct depthwise/grouped conv2d kernel (avoid per-group im2col-GEMM)
description:
GAP: functional_conv2d_grouped (ft-api ~18780) implements grouped conv as a loop over `groups`, each doing tensor_narrow(input)+tensor_narrow(weight)+functional_conv2d (im2col+sgemm_bt)+cat. For DEPTHWISE conv (groups==in_channels, in_per_group=out_per_group=1 — the backbone of MobileNet/EfficientNet/ConvNeXt) this is C separate tiny im2col-GEMMs ([1,1,Kh,Kw]@[Kh*Kw,HW]) + C narrows + a C-way cat, with per-group tape/intermediate allocation even in no-grad. Hugely wasteful vs a direct depthwise kernel: O(N*C*OH*OW*Kh*Kw) single pass, no im2col patch replication, no per-group GEMM dispatch, no narrow/cat nodes. Expected >>2x for depthwise (the common modern-CNN case).

DESIGN (no-grad fast path first, lowest risk):
- New ft-kernel-cpu kernel depthwise_conv2d_forward_f64/f32(padded:[N,C,ph,pw], weight:[C,1,Kh,Kw], stride, ...): par_chunks_mut over (N,C) planes; each output[oy,ox]=sum_{kr,kc} padded[c, oy*sh+kr, ox*sw+kc]*weight[c,kr,kc]. Parallel over the N*C independent planes (disjoint) — compute-bound (Kh*Kw MACs/output).
- ft-api dispatch: in functional_conv2d_grouped, detect depthwise (groups==in_channels && out_channels==in_channels && weight_in==1) && no-grad, route to the kernel (apply padding first, mirror conv2d_forward's padded-input contract). Grad path keeps the existing grouped loop (autograd-correct). ⚠️ ft-api is peer-contended — use the filtered-patch commit technique + worktree-verify.
- Backward kernel (follow-up): depthwise dinput (full-correlation with flipped weight) + dweight (correlate input with dout per channel), parallel over channels.

PARITY POLICY QUESTION (must resolve FIRST): the conv 1x1 fast path is held BIT-EXACT vs im2col (test conv2d_1x1_fast_path_matches_im2col_bitexact @ ft-kernel-cpu:23556) — but it achieves that by CALLING gemm::sgemm, not hand-rolling the reduction. A hand-rolled depthwise sum (acc+=a*b in kr,kc order) will NOT match matrixmultiply's FMA/blocking order bit-for-bit. Conv is GEMM-backed (reassociates), so the tolerance-parity precedent (qgce4: LU/Chol/QR/SVD/eig use reconstruction+~1e-9, NOT bit-exact) SHOULD extend to conv — but this needs an explicit policy ratification (like qgce4 unblocked eig). Alternatively, match the GEMM order via mul_add if matrixmultiply's K-reduction for small K is sequential-FMA (verify empirically: build kernel, compare to per-group im2col output, measure max ULP delta).

PROOF: A/B depthwise-direct vs replicated per-group-im2col baseline (same process, 1 thread for the kernel-vs-kernel ratio); golden sha256 of the direct-kernel output; tolerance test vs im2col (max abs diff < 1e-12) OR bit-exact if the order matches. Same-worker anchored A/B. Score>=2.0.

This is the next DEEP ownable lever (not peer-eig/linalg). Multi-step + policy-gated -> focused session. See [[project_compute_bound_parallel_vein]] cache-oblivious-tiling note for the conv-context + the qgce4 tolerance precedent in [[project_eig_tolerance_policy_ratified]].
close_reason: SHIPPED 7a924b10: direct depthwise_conv2d_forward_f64/f32 kernel (parallel over N*C planes, direct kh*kw dot-product, no im2col/per-group-GEMM/cat) + no-grad fast path in functional_conv2d_grouped. A/B vs per-group im2col: 5.68x [16,64,58,58] / 8.73x [8,256,30,30] (bigger win = more channels). within-1e-9 of per-group path; tolerance-parity (grouped path itself checked vs torch at 1e-9); 5 ft-api grouped tests + kernel bit-exact test pass; grad path unchanged. REMAINING (optional follow-up, lower value): direct depthwise BACKWARD kernel for the grad/training path (currently still the grouped loop — correct but slow); file a fresh bead if training depthwise perf matters.

title: tensor_pdist severs autograd; composable through index_select for finite p > 0
description:
tensor_pdist at lib.rs:4349 computes pairwise L_p distances between rows of a 2-D input. For finite p > 0 the math is composable through autograd-aware primitives (index_select pairs of rows, abs, pow, sum_dim, pow). For p = inf it's argmax-routing; for p = 0 it's a non-differentiable counting metric.

Strategy: build row_i and row_j index tensors of length N*(N-1)/2 each, use tensor_index_select to gather row pairs, then compute:
   diff = left - right
   per_pair = (sum_k |diff[k]|^p)^(1/p)
All ops autograd-aware. Add fail-loud guards for p == 0 (counting metric, non-diff) and p == +inf (could be added with max_dim but defer).
notes: Fixed in this commit. For finite p > 0, composed tensor_pdist through index_select + sub + abs + pow + sum_dim + pow primitives. Pre-built row_i / row_j index tensors of length N*(N-1)/2 enumerate the upper-triangular pairs; index_select gathers the row-pair tensors so subsequent ops broadcast naturally. p == 0 (counting metric) and p == +inf (max-routing) now fail-loud on requires_grad=true rather than silently severing — autograd implementations for those cases are deferred. Added pdist_l2_propagates_gradient_through_input regression test. ft-api lib tests: 1169.

Add a no-grad fused kernel pdist_forward_f64: parallel over i<j pairs (or over i), stream the M features per pair in one pass, write the flattened strict-upper-triangle [out_len]. title: Bound conformance parser diagnostics for fuzzed string inputs
description: testing-fuzzing audit found unbounded diagnostic echo in crates/ft-conformance/src/lib.rs parser helpers. parse_binary_op, parse_dtype, parse_device, and parse_keyset include the full rejected input in error strings. These functions consume values from JSON conformance fixtures and fuzzed/adversarial inputs; a single oversized token can produce multi-megabyte error/log output even though the harness already has bounded_diagnostic for legacy oracle errors. Fix by bounding rejected parser tokens through the existing bounded_diagnostic helper, add regression tests with oversized strings, and preserve existing valid parse behavior.
close_reason: Bounded conformance parser diagnostics for oversized fuzzed tokens

title: tensor_kthvalue severs autograd; index-routing backward needed
description:
tensor_kthvalue at lib.rs:12512 returns the k-th smallest element of a 1-D tensor. The value branch is just input[idx] where idx is the rank-k sorted index. PyTorch's torch.kthvalue IS differentiable: grad_input[idx] = grad_value, all other positions zero.

Wrap value branch in tensor_apply_function with this single-index routing backward. Index branch stays a non-grad leaf (integer).
notes: Fixed in this commit. Wrapped tensor_kthvalue's value branch in tensor_apply_function with single-index routing backward: grad_input[idx] = grad_value where idx is the sort-rank-k index. The index branch stays a non-grad leaf. Added kthvalue_propagates_gradient_to_routed_index regression test (x=[3,1,4,1,5], k=3 → 3rd smallest is at idx 0 with value 3, grad_input = [1,0,0,0,0]). ft-api lib tests: 1163.

title: max_pool2d missing padding (functional + MaxPool2d module) — avg_pool2d has it
description: All max_pool2d entry points (functional_max_pool2d, tensor_max_pool2d, max_pool2d_tensor, nn MaxPool2d module) take only kernel+stride. torch F.max_pool2d / nn.MaxPool2d support padding (also dilation, ceil_mode, return_indices). avg_pool2d already supports padding+ceil_mode. padding is the most common need (ResNet stem = MaxPool2d(3,stride=2,padding=1)). Fix: pad with -inf then route through existing windowed-max kernel (bit-exact, ft-api only, no kernel change). Validate padding<=kernel/2 (torch rule). ceil_mode/dilation/return_indices = follow-up.
close_reason: done

title: [perf][no-gaps] ft-api pinv: QR fast path for full-rank (same 6x SVD->QR swap as lstsq 2i5d)
close_reason: Completed: no-grad tensor_linalg_pinv QR fast path kept; pinv_svd_512x256 median 461.21 ms -> 169.07 ms on ts1; golden sha256 passed.

comments.author: RusticKite
comments.text: Same playbook as the lstsq QR win (9ac944c4 / 2i5d). tensor_pinv / tensor_linalg_pinv (ft-api ~43839) compute the pseudo-inverse via the full Golub-Reinsch SVD (~300x-slow BLAS-1 sweep). For FULL COLUMN RANK m>=n, A^+ = R^{-1} Q^T (from A=QR), and for full ROW rank m<n, A^+ = Q R^{-T} (via A^T=QR). Both avoid the SVD entirely -> expect the same ~6x. PLAN: add a kernel pinv_qr_contiguous_f64 reusing the Q-free Householder approach from lstsq_qr_contiguous_f64 (apply reflectors to an identity RHS to get Q^T, back-solve R) returning Option (None -> defer to SVD for rank-deficient, which gives the min-norm pinv torch returns). Wire the no-grad full-rank path in tensor_linalg_pinv; grad stays on SVD (pinv backward). GOTCHA (learned in 2i5d): NEVER form the m×m Q (O(m^2 n) waste) — the first lstsq attempt reusing qr_contiguous_f64 was a 0.4x REGRESSION; apply reflectors directly. Bench pinv full-rank 256x128/512x256, A/B vs current SVD pinv, isomorphism <1e-8 vs SVD, Score>=2.0. Worktree off origin/main. pinv is used widely (lstsq grad, many linalg ops) so the win propagates.

title: [test] broadcast binary-op gradient values gradcheck (sum-reduction across broadcast axes)
description: ft binary ops (add/mul/sub/div) auto-broadcast [3,1] op [1,4]->[3,4]; gradient SHAPES were right but the VALUES (backward sum-reduction over the broadcast axes — different axis for each operand) were not value-gradchecked. Verified analytic backward matches central differences to ~1e-10 for both grad_a (summed over the 4-axis) and grad_b (summed over the 3-axis) for all four ops (add->[4,4,4]/[3,3,3,3], mul->sum(b)/sum(a), sub, div). Add a committed finite-diff gradcheck to lock the broadcast-backward. Probe: examples/broadcast_gradcheck_probe.rs.
close_reason: Added finite-diff gradcheck for broadcast binary-op gradient VALUES (add/mul/sub/div [3,1]op[1,4], both operands summed over their broadcast axes, rel<1e-4 ~1e-10) + 2 probe examples. No bug; broadcast-backward pinned. ft-api lib test passes. Commit 867f976a.

title: Add sparse tensor storage and basic sparse ops
description:
## Progress

### Completed
1. SparseCOOTensor in ft-core with indices [sparse_dim, nnz], values [nnz, *dense_dims], coalesced flag
2. SparseCSRTensor in ft-core with crow_indices, col_indices, values
3. SparseTensorError enum with comprehensive validation errors
4. AutogradError::SparseTensor variant for error propagation
5. ft-api sparse tensor methods:
   - sparse_coo_tensor(coords, values, shape, dtype)
   - sparse_coo_tensor_from_indices(indices, values, shape, sparse_dim, coalesced)
   - sparse_csr_tensor(crow_indices, col_indices, values, shape)
   - tensor_to_sparse_coo(input) - dense to sparse conversion
   - sparse_coo_to_dense / sparse_csr_to_dense
6. ft-kernel-cpu sparse operations:
   - sparse_coo_matmul_dense_f64 - sparse @ dense matmul
   - sparse_coo_coalesce - merge duplicate indices
   - sparse_coo_add - element-wise addition
7. GradientValue enum (Dense/Sparse) in ft-autograd for unified gradient representation

title: ft-autograd: enable double-backward (create_graph) for NormDim (p=1, p=2)
description: backward_create_graph fail-loud errored for NormDim, blocking second-order autograd through per-slice norms (per-row normalization). p=2: grad_x=grad_slice*x/norm_slice; broadcast the reduced incoming AND the NormDim output node along dim via reshape-to-size-1 + cg_expand (SumDim pattern), reusing node_id as the divisor so the second backward routes d(norm)/dx through NormDim's own backward -> exact per-slice block-diagonal Hessian. Zero-norm slices: substitute 1.0 divisor (numerator is 0 there, grad=0, matches first-order). p=1: grad*sign(x) gate. Other p fail loud. Tests: create_graph_norm_dim2_second_derivative_per_slice_hessian (rows [3,4],[6,8]; grad=[.6,.8,.6,.8]; 2nd=[.032,-.024,.016,-.012]), create_graph_norm_dim1_gradients.
close_reason: Implemented create_graph NormDim arm (p=1/p=2; p=2 reuses NormDim node as per-slice cg_div divisor for exact block-diagonal Hessian); 429 ft-autograd tests pass (+2), clippy clean.

Autograd-aware via the underlying view. 2 unit tests cover shape propagation and gradient routing. ft-api now at 1292 lib tests.

title: ft-api: f16/bf16 write-movement ops (scatter-overwrite/index_copy/put_along_dim) rejected half — u78p covered only read-movement
description: u78p closed claiming 'all MOVEMENT/SELECT/PREDICATE f16/bf16 gaps fixed' but only covered READ-movement (gather/index_select/masked_select/where/comparisons). The WRITE-movement overwrite ops still hard-errored 'requires f32 or f64': tensor_scatter (overwrite, NOT scatter_add), tensor_index_copy (composes through scatter), tensor_put_along_dim (calls scatter). These are pure movement (out[idx]=src, no arithmetic) so the same upcast-f64-move-narrow path is bit-exact (every written element is an original input-half or src-half value, round-trips half->f64->half exactly). FIXED: tape scatter F16/BF16 arm (scatter_tensor_contiguous_f64 + reduction_values_storage narrow) + api tensor_scatter reads half src via values_lossy_f64 + api index_copy gate relaxed; put_along_dim free (delegates to scatter). Bit-exact golden test f16_scatter_index_copy_preserve_dtype uses exact f16(0.1)/f16(0.3) reps for == equality. REMAINING tail: index_fill (scalar fill — needs a half src_const built via round-to-half then fill, minor extra wrinkle) and index_put accumulate=false (movement) / accumulate=true (defer, same arithmetic class as uv4l).
close_reason: SHIPPED 0ebeda68. scatter (overwrite) + index_copy + put_along_dim now support f16/bf16 bit-exactly (upcast-f64 / move / narrow-to-half; pure movement so every written element round-trips half->f64->half exactly). tape scatter F16/BF16 arm + api tensor_scatter values_lossy_f64 src read + index_copy gate relaxed. Golden test f16_scatter_index_copy_preserve_dtype asserts EXACT (==) equality via f16 reps of 0.1/0.3, plus bf16 + f32/f64 no-regression. ft-autograd 436 + ft-api 1914 lib tests pass. REMAINING tail (could be a fresh P3): index_fill (needs a half src_const = round-to-half(value) then fill) and index_put accumulate=false (movement; accumulate=true defers to uv4l's arithmetic-parity decision).

title: ft-autograd: enable double-backward (create_graph) for Where op
description: create_graph backward dispatch returned a fail-loud error for TensorNodeOp::Where, so torch.where blocked all second-order autograd (Hessians, gradient penalties) through gated branches. where(c,x,y) selects via a constant mask, so the gradient is a pure elementwise gate: d/dx=grad*mask, d/dy=grad*(1-mask). Implemented the create_graph arm using cg_mul against constant mask leaves so the gradient nodes stay dependent on the incoming gradient and double-backward routes through the gate instead of collapsing to a detached zero leaf. Added create_graph_where_gradients (first-order gate) and create_graph_where_second_derivative_routes_through_gate (curvature through gated x^2 branch) tests.

title: eig returns Schur vectors, not eigenvectors (general non-symmetric)
description: eig_contiguous_f64 (now with correct eigenvalues via Francis QR, 8a047947) returns q_acc — the accumulated real-SCHUR vectors — as 'eigenvectors'. These are NOT the actual eigenvectors: for A=[[2,1],[0,3]] (Schur form = A itself, q_acc=I) it returns columns [1,0],[0,1] but the λ=3 eigenvector is [1,1]/√2; A@[0,1]=[1,3] != 3*[0,1]. No test validates A·v=λ·v for general eig (only eigenvalues are checked), so it slipped through. The proper fix is the EISPACK hqr2 eigenvector BACK-SUBSTITUTION from the quasi-triangular Schur form, then transform by q_acc. COMPLICATION: complex eigenvalues have COMPLEX eigenvectors; EigResult.eigenvectors is a real Vec<f64> with a 're/im consecutive columns' convention that cannot represent n complex eigenvectors in n columns — proper parity needs a complex tensor output (torch.linalg.eig returns complex). So this needs both the back-sub kernel AND an API/storage change. Validation gate: add eig_eigenvectors_satisfy_av_lambda_v (real + complex cases).
close_reason: FIXED (900999e5): added EISPACK hqr2 eigenvector back-substitution + cdiv. eig now returns correct eigenvectors (was Schur vectors). Validated by eig_eigenvectors_satisfy_av_lambda_v (5x5, real + complex pairs, A·v=λ·v to <1e-6). Complex eigenvectors stored as (re,im) column pairs — no API change. 393 tests pass. Completes the general-eig fix (eigenvalues fixed in 8a047947).

title: Double-backward GAP: apply_function-based custom ops lack a create_graph backward -> 2nd derivative ERRORS while torch supports it (exp2/lgamma/digamma/erfinv/selu confirmed via hessian_probe; likely all special-fn customs). Add cg backwards or an ft-api apply_function_with_create_graph wrapper
notes: PROGRESS (BoldFalcon, 74bb450d + 5a4bc054): the ft-api wrapper approach WORKS — my prior 'do NOT build the wrapper' scoping was WRONG. Added tensor_apply_function_with_create_graph (ft-api wrapper over the ft-autograd primitive); the cg closure CAN call PUBLIC TensorTape methods (tape.mul_scalar/exp/mul/add/leaf, read values via tape.tensor(id)) mid-backward and they work correctly. exp2 (74bb450d) + selu (5a4bc054) now double-differentiable, 2nd derivatives match torch exactly (exp2''=ln2^2*2^x via ln2*exp(x*ln2); selu''(x<0)=SCALE*ALPHA*exp(x) via masks+exp). Forward + 1st-grad unchanged (cg-path 1st-grad may differ <=1 ULP, acceptable; non-cg path untouched). RECIPE: convert tensor_<op> from apply_function to tensor_apply_function_with_create_graph; cg closure builds deriv from public tape ops connected to inputs[0]. REMAINING 3 ops need their derivative TRANSCENDENTAL as a differentiable node: lgamma'=digamma (needs digamma node — itself a custom op), digamma'=polygamma(1,x)=trigamma (needs polygamma node), erfinv'=(sqrt(pi)/2)exp(erfinv(x)^2) (needs erfinv node). These chain through other custom ops, so they need those made into nodes first (or a digamma/trigamma/erfinv tape primitive). Deeper but same wrapper. Verified via examples/hessian_probe (now also covers composite ops pow3/recip/softmax/logsoftmax/prod/sumsq — all match torch).
close_reason: All confirmed offenders fixed. exp2 (74bb450d) + selu (5a4bc054) shipped earlier; lgamma/digamma/erfinv shipped 41c9dbc7 via tensor_apply_function_with_create_graph — each cg backward builds its derivative (digamma/trigamma/erfinv) as a differentiable node whose own first-order backward supplies the 2nd derivative. hessian_probe now matches torch goldens EXACTLY for every listed op (lgamma=trigamma, digamma=polygamma(2,x), erfinv=2c²y·e^{2y²}); zero HESS_ERR remain. Raw first-order backwards untouched -> ordinary grads unchanged. ft-api 2096 passed/0 failed. RESIDUAL (untested, not a named offender): polygamma(n>=1) double-backward would still need a cg node (deriv=polygamma(n+1)); file a follow-up if a real use surfaces.

title: Investigate persistent beads SQLite integrity warning after clean rebuild
description:
## Context
After a full non-destructive rebuild of `.beads/beads.db` from canonical `issues.jsonl`, `br doctor --json` still reports:

`sqlite.integrity_check: database disk image is malformed: page 17 is never used`

This persists even on the freshly rebuilt DB, while all operational checks are otherwise healthy (`br ready --json` is empty, sync metadata matches, blocked cache is not stale).

## Goal
Determine whether this warning reflects:
1. a genuine low-level SQLite file/layout problem,
2. expected frankensqlite behavior that `br doctor` should not flag as suspicious, or
3. a beads/br bug in the integrity-check path.

## Acceptance
- Reproduce the warning on the current canonical DB
- Compare against a freshly rebuilt temp DB
- Gather enough evidence to classify the issue as workspace-local vs tool-level
- If it is tool-level or not safely fixable from this repo, document the finding clearly and close or hand off with evidence
close_reason: Reproduced the integrity warning on canonical and rebuilt DBs, proved a plain SQLite VACUUM clears it, then observed br doctor --repair reintroduce the warning on a different page number while keeping logical metadata healthy; this classifies the issue as a tool-level beads/frankensqlite write-path problem rather than repo-local issue-data corruption

title: [perf][no-gaps] ft-serialize native f64 payload fixed-width decode
description: Target: close native state-dict decode overhead vs upstream torch serialization for many small f64 tensors without unsafe code. Profile-backed scenario: rch Criterion on worker vmi1293453, command 'rch exec -- cargo bench -p ft-serialize --bench serialize_bench -- --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 5 --sample-size 20', benchmark native_state_dict/decode_many_small_f64_1024x4 = [342.17 us 345.59 us 349.52 us]. perf stat attempted through rch but worker denies counters with perf_event_paranoid=4. Ranked hotspot evidence: load_state_dict_from_bytes repeatedly enters read_f64_payload for 1024 small tensors; each call decodes fixed-width little-endian chunks with per-byte indexing inside chunks_exact. Candidate alien primitive: constants-kill-you fixed-width decode tightening via array-sized chunk views / autovectorization-friendly loop while preserving exact little-endian bit patterns, BTreeMap ordering, duplicate-key error semantics, and dtype/error classes. One lever only; keep only if rch Criterion after score >= 2.0 and golden sha256 passes.

title: [perf][bug] ft-api: f32 tensor_cumprod path ~20x slower than f64 (path overhead, not kernel)
description: MEASURED: cumprod[8192,1024] dim=1, parallel kernel, f32 87ms vs f64 4.1ms — the SAME size + same parallelized kernel (cumprod_tensor_contiguous_f32 mirrors _f64). 20x is in the PATH not the kernel: tensor_cumprod -> tape.cumprod -> dispatch_tensor_scan_dim_contiguous_typed -> _f32. Likely an f32->f64->f32 promote/convert or per-lane re-dispatch/alloc in the f32 scan dispatch (dispatch_tensor_scan_dim_contiguous_f32 in ft-dispatch ~5537). Same pattern probably affects f32 cumsum too. PROFILE the f32 scan dispatch path; expected a big (~10-20x) f32 win by removing the conversion/overhead. Found while benching cumprod (see h5gj). NOTE: shared ft-kernel-cpu working tree is heavily contended by a concurrent peer agent doing the scan kernels (they shipped cumsum 686ab41b); coordinate / work in ft-api+ft-dispatch (less contended) for this one.
close_reason: Completed: removed f32 scan dispatch widen/narrow round trip. Same-worker vmi1293453 Criterion cumprod/f32_nograd_8192x1024_dim1 improved [49.347 ms 50.254 ms 51.267 ms] -> [15.985 ms 16.573 ms 17.221 ms] (3.03x median). f32 cumsum/cumprod dtype tests and typed dispatch f32 storage test passed; cargo check passed; ft-dispatch+ft-autograd clippy passed. Broad clippy/fmt/UBS caveats recorded in artifacts/perf/frankentorch-1q8x/report.md.

comments.author: RusticKite
comments.text: CAVEAT: the 'f32 87ms vs f64 4.1ms = 20x' was measured across separate rch execs (worker variance) and partly overlaps the disputed cumprod numbers (h5gj). The 20x magnitude is too large to be pure worker variance (~1.4x), so an f32-scan-path overhead is likely REAL, but re-measure with a single-process anchored A/B before trusting the magnitude.

comments.author: RusticKite
comments.text: DEPRIORITIZE / re-measure needed: the '20x f32 path' was built on the same cross-exec confound (the f64 4.1ms was a worker-variance fluke; the true same-worker f64 cumprod KERNEL is ~9.8ms serial / ~7.7ms parallel per scan_ab). f32-via-full-path 87ms vs a clean same-worker f64-via-full-path is unmeasured. There may still be a real f32-scan-PATH overhead, but only a single-process f32-vs-f64 tensor_cumprod A/B can confirm the magnitude. Not >=2x-proven.

title: porting: add tensor_take + tensor_swapaxes/swapdims for torch parity
description: torch.take(input, indices) returns input.flatten()[indices], reshaped like indices. Useful for advanced indexing (the same primitive numpy.take provides). torch.swapaxes / torch.swapdims are direct aliases for transpose (different name conventions for numpy compatibility). Add tensor_take by composing tensor_reshape (flatten) + tensor_index_select; add swapaxes/swapdims as one-line wrappers around tensor_transpose.
close_reason: Added tensor_take, tensor_swapaxes, and tensor_swapdims for torch parity. tensor_take(input, indices) = input.flatten()[indices].reshape(indices.shape) — composes through tensor_reshape (flatten input + flatten indices) + tensor_index_select + tensor_reshape (back to indices shape); autograd-aware via index_select backward including repeated-index gradient accumulation. swapaxes/swapdims are one-line aliases for tensor_transpose (numpy compat names). 4 unit tests cover forward correctness, gradient routing, repeated-index accumulation, and alias equivalence to transpose. ft-api now at 1245 lib tests.

title: perf: conv2d 4-6x slower than PyTorch
description:
FrankenTorch conv2d is 4-6x slower than PyTorch (single-threaded):
- 64x64: FT 115.8ms vs PT 28.2ms (4.1x)
- 128x128: FT 459.4ms vs PT 73.2ms (6.3x)

Root cause: Likely using naive im2col + matmul approach without optimizations.
PyTorch uses optimized implementations (NNPACK, MKL-DNN, etc.)

Fix options:
1. Integrate Winograd convolution for small kernels (3x3)
2. Use NNPACK via FFI
3. Optimize im2col memory layout

Evidence: criterion ops_bench vs pytorch_baseline.json
close_reason: Replace per-patch narrow+reshape loop with 2 unfolds + permute + reshape, reducing O(out_h*out_w) to O(1) tensor ops

title: Add F.pad reflect/replicate/circular modes
description: Extend tensor_pad to support reflect, replicate, circular padding modes. Currently only constant padding. Essential for vision preprocessing.

title: Fix ft-nn MultiMarginLoss autograd via one_hot + relu + pow [child of cq0b]
description:
ft-nn MultiMarginLoss::forward at lib.rs:11852 has the same per-sample severance pattern as the session multi_margin_loss method (already fixed under frankentorch-x9cr).

Compose through session.one_hot + tensor_mul + tensor_sub + tensor_relu + tensor_pow + tensor_sum_dim + tensor_div for per-sample loss, then apply the Reduction enum's mean/sum/none.
notes: Fixed via composition through one_hot + tensor_mul + tensor_sub + tensor_relu + tensor_pow + tensor_sum_dim + tensor_div primitives — same recipe as session multi_margin_loss (frankentorch-x9cr) but with the Reduction enum dispatch at the end. ft-nn lib tests: 517.

title: [perf][no-gaps] ft-api: precompute trilinear interpolation axis plans
description: Profile-backed target after SupCon symmetry lever was rejected. Fresh rch baseline on ts2: cargo bench -p ft-api --bench ops_bench -- interpolate_trilinear/2x8x16x16x16_2x --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 5 --sample-size 20 => [4.8368 ms 5.3322 ms 5.7814 ms]. Current non-grad trilinear interpolation recomputes z/y/x source coordinates, clamps, floors, and fractions inside output rows even though each axis plan is reused across all batch/channel planes. One lever: precompute per-output z/y/x low/high/fraction values once, then use the same 8-tap arithmetic order in fill_row. Proof obligations: bit-exact against existing serial reference, same output row order and corner accumulation order, no RNG/error/shape changes, golden sha256, rch rebench Score >= 2.0.
close_reason: Completed: trilinear axis plan kept. Baseline ts2 [4.8368 ms 5.3322 ms 5.7814 ms] -> after ts1 [2.0964 ms 2.1311 ms 2.1732 ms], conservative 2.50x p50; bit-exact proof and golden sha256 passed.

title: Statistical / special ops with severed-autograd: cov, corrcoef, mode, quantile, logit, erfinv, digamma, polygamma, multigammaln, entr, xlogy, xlog1py, logcumsumexp
description: These ft-api ops all extract tensor values via self.tensor_values + return self.tensor_tape.leaf(... false), silently severing the autograd tape on tracked inputs (the same pattern recently fixed for SELU / CELU / Threshold / Hardtanh / Unfold / Fold / index_* / scatter_* / masked_scatter / PReLU + 6 shrink-family activations). Some have explicit fail-loud guards (logit doesn't, erfinv does, digamma doesn't, etc.) but the contract is inconsistent. Pick each off and either compose through autograd primitives (preferred where the math admits it) or add a uniform fail-loud guard. PyTorch's torch.cov / torch.corrcoef / torch.special.{logit,digamma,polygamma,...} are all differentiable. Children: each op gets its own fix bead.
notes: All children resolved across multiple commits: cov/corrcoef/mode/quantile/logit/xlogy/xlog1py/entr composed through autograd primitives in earlier rounds; gammaln (7cc0b45), digamma (86fb29d), polygamma + multigammaln (bd2817b), erfinv (ac049c6), logcumsumexp (ec75d35) wrapped in tensor_apply_function with analytical backwards. ft-api lib tests now at 1135 passing.

title: Add E2E training test for GroupNorm + Conv2d block
description:
Add a perfect-e2e regression that wires a Conv2d → GroupNorm block into a small image-regression task. Validates the GroupNorm composition (reshape + mean_dim + sub + mul + sqrt + div + affine) end-to-end through Adam.

Setup:
- 1x4x4x4 input (batch=1, channels=4, H=4, W=4)
- Conv2d(4, 4, 3x3, padding 1)
- GroupNorm(num_groups=2, num_channels=4)
- MSE against target
- Adam lr=0.05 for 200 steps
- Assert final loss < initial * 0.5 (relaxed because GroupNorm normalizes)

title: [perf] ft-kernel-cpu: parallelize topk-along-dim over lanes (serial vs torch)
description: GAP: topk_tensor_contiguous_f64/f32 selected each (outer,inner) lane in a serial double loop (full sort + take-k + optional re-sort); torch parallelizes topk over lanes. Compute-bound. LEVER: parallelize over per-outer blocks — input block dim_size*inner_size, output block k*inner_size, both yield outer_size chunks so par_chunks zip aligns; each lane selected independently with the same stable sort + fixed nan_greatest comparator + stable index re-sort. ISOMORPHISM: bit-identical values AND indices in both sorted modes (proved topk_parallel_matches_serial_bit_exact; strided+last-dim, sorted=true/false, duplicates). BENCH (RAYON_NUM_THREADS=1 vs all cores, [8192,1024] k=50 dim1): 418.3->56.5ms (7.40x).
close_reason: Parallelized topk-along-dim over lanes (f64+f32); bit-exact values+indices both sorted modes (topk_parallel_matches_serial_bit_exact); [8192,1024] k=50 418.3->56.5ms 7.40x. 378 tests pass, clippy clean.

title: ft-api: roll is single-dim only; torch supports multi-dim shifts/dims + flat roll
description: tensor_roll(input,shift,dim) handled one dim. torch.roll(input,shifts,dims) takes tuples (roll each dim) and torch.roll(input,shift) with no dims rolls the flattened tensor. Added tensor_roll_dims(input,&[shifts],&[dims]) (separable -> sequential single-dim rolls) and tensor_roll_flat(input,shift) (reshape-roll-reshape). Both autograd-aware. Verified torch 2.12 (roll shifts(1,1) dims(0,1) on 2x3 -> [5,3,4,2,0,1]; flat roll). Also added two differential parity-lock tests confirming avg_pool2d (count_include_pad, ceil_mode) + interpolate (bilinear ac True/False, nearest) and rot90/tensordot/kron/take_along_dim/flip all match torch. ft-api 1937 pass.
close_reason: SHIPPED 4107bbae. tensor_roll_dims (multi-dim, separable) + tensor_roll_flat (flatten-roll-reshape), autograd-aware. Verified torch 2.12. Plus avg_pool_interpolate_match_torch + movement_contraction_ops_match_torch parity locks (avg_pool cip/ceil_mode, interpolate bilinear/nearest, rot90/tensordot/kron/take_along_dim/flip all correct). ft-api 1937 pass.

title: ft-kernel-cpu: panel-blocked parallel triangular solve for lu_solve/inv
description: lu_solve matrix-RHS triangular substitution is serial O(n^3); it dominates inv (factor is already parallel) and gated the rejected matrix_exp Pade. Naive per-column rayon parallelization REGRESSED 3x (256x256 inv 10.7ms->35.9ms) because it loses the inner-rhs-loop's L-coefficient reuse (each L loaded once, applied across all RHS cols, cache/SIMD-amortized). Correct lever = panel-block the RHS columns (transpose into contiguous panels of width ~32-64), rayon over panels, each panel running the inner-rhs-loop with L-reuse within its panel. Keeps amortization AND parallelizes. Bit-exact per panel. Baseline inv_f64: 256x256 10.7ms, 512x512 55ms. Target >=2x at 512.
notes: REJECTED: panel-blocked parallel triangular solve does NOT clear Score>=2.0. On 64 cores: pw=4 (64 panels) gave inv 256x256 1.05x / 512x512 1.25x; pw=64 (4 panels) was WORSE (noisy ~4x regression). Root cause: the triangular solve is MEMORY-BANDWIDTH-BOUND (low arithmetic intensity: 1 FMA per L-coefficient per column), so fanning across cores saturates bandwidth rather than speeding up; additionally inv is partly lu_factor-bound. Bit-exact but not worth shipping. Triangular solve is the WRONG primitive to parallelize. NEXT: pivot to compute-bound ops (conv via im2col+dgemm, or getri-style inv that inverts U then multiplies).
close_reason: done

title: porting: add tensor_mv (matrix-vector multiply) for torch.mv parity
description: torch.mv(M, v) returns M @ v for a 2-D matrix M (shape [m, n]) and 1-D vector v (shape [n]); output is 1-D of shape [m]. PyTorch has it as a dedicated op even though matmul covers the same shape; user code calls it directly. Compose through autograd-aware primitives: reshape v to [n, 1] → tensor_matmul(M, v) → reshape to [m]. Add a few correctness and gradient regression tests.
close_reason: Added pub fn tensor_mv(M, v) at crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs — matrix-vector product M @ v matching torch.mv. Composes through tensor_reshape + tensor_matmul + tensor_reshape; autograd-aware on both inputs. Added 3 unit tests: mv_known_values (forward correctness), mv_propagates_gradient_to_matrix_and_vector (analytic gradient regression for sum(M @ v)), mv_rejects_wrong_ranks (fail-loud for 1-D M, 2-D v, mismatched n). ft-api now at 1211 lib tests (up from 1208).

title: [test] reflection/replication/circular pad1d golden vs torch

title: Numerical gradient check: tensor_linalg_det backward via finite differences
description: Lock the new autograd-aware tensor_linalg_det backward (added under frankentorch-pvfk) against numerical Jacobian computed by central finite differences. For each entry A[i,j], perturb ±eps, recompute det, and confirm (det(A+) - det(A-)) / (2*eps) matches the analytic backward to within finite-difference truncation tolerance. Catches any sign flip, transpose error, or scaling bug in the Jacobi-formula backward.
close_reason: Added linalg_det_backward_matches_central_finite_difference — exercises the new tensor_linalg_det backward (pvfk) on 4 well-conditioned 2x2/3x3 matrices (diag, general, rotation, perturbed-identity). Compares analytic ∂det/∂A from FrankenTorch's autograd against central FD with eps=1e-6 and abs_tol=5e-5. Locks the Jacobi-formula backward against any sign/transpose/scaling regression. ft-conformance now at 148 base lib tests, 194 with --features fuzz.

title: [perf][no-gaps] ft-serialize: segmented native decode record index for many-small tensors
description: Profile-backed target from artifacts/perf/frankentorch-ftserialize-f64decode/rejected_borrowed_utf8_key.md. Benchmark native_state_dict/decode_many_small_f64_1024x4: clean same-worker baseline confirmation 362.84 us median; borrowed UTF-8 key materialization regressed to 397.57 us and was rejected. Next primitive: structural native decode from fixed-width/segmented-data family. Parse tensor records into a compact record index over borrowed byte ranges, decode fixed-width f64 payloads in a batched/segmented pass, and materialize owned String keys only at final BTreeMap boundary. Preserve map ordering, duplicate-key failure point, exact f64 raw bits, malformed-input diagnostics, dtype/shape contracts, and RNG absence. Acceptance: fresh RCH crate-scoped baseline first, one lever, focused golden sha256/proof tests, same-worker rebench, keep only Score >= 2.0.

Kept prerequisite ft-serialize RaptorQ API repair; final proof/clippy/fmt passed. Follow-up deeper primitive filed as frankentorch-hvm4m.

title: [perf][no-gaps] ft-autograd: eliminate per-node double-clone floor in tensor_backward
description: tensor_backward()'s main loop does TWO ~numel f64 clones per node before any contrib work: (1) apply_tensor_hooks() returns incoming.to_vec() even when the node has no hooks (common case), and (2) grads[node_id.0] = incoming.clone(). Both are dead in the no-hook path. This serial bandwidth floor caps the through-tape gain of every parallelized backward contrib (e.g. the activation family in 5bb0e9f9 is 6x on the contrib map but <2x through-tape because of this floor). Lever: no-hook fast path via std::mem::take(&mut grads[node]) to move the grad Vec out (no clone), use as owned incoming through the match (no borrow conflict with grads[input.0] += contrib), move back at loop end. Apply hooks only when present. Must preserve: gradient lookup semantics (grads[node] holds hook-adjusted incoming after), and any continue/early-exit arms. Validate: ft-autograd --lib + ft-api backward parity suites bit-exact; A/B full tensor_backward old vs new on a gelu/silu graph for Score>=2.0 through-tape.
close_reason: Duplicate — landed concurrently by peer as 1d65cd96 'take incoming gradient out of the tape without cloning' (mem::take + restore-at-loop-end, zero-copy no-hook path; swept up my working-tree edits). Pushed to origin/main.

title: Add custom autograd Function API (torch.autograd.Function equivalent)
description:
Users cannot currently define custom backward functions. This is arguably the single most impactful missing feature for real-world use — without it, any layer whose backward isn't built into FrankenTorch's op set is impossible to implement.

PyTorch's torch.autograd.Function API allows users to:
1. Define a custom forward() that registers itself on the autograd tape
2. Define a custom backward() that computes gradients for the custom op
3. Save tensors for backward via ctx.save_for_backward()

Implementation plan:
1. Add a CustomFunction trait in ft-autograd with forward/backward methods
2. Add a TensorNodeOp::Custom variant to the tensor tape
3. Add session.apply_custom_function() to ft-api that invokes the user's forward, registers the op on the tape, and stores the backward closure
4. During backward traversal, call the user's backward function when encountering Custom nodes
5. Handle save_for_backward by capturing TensorNodeIds in the closure context

Key design decisions:
- The backward closure must receive upstream gradient and return downstream gradients
- Must respect no_grad context (custom forward inside no_grad should not record on tape)
- Must work with the existing deterministic backward scheduler
- Error handling: user backward functions should return Result, not panic

Tests:
- Unit test: custom ReLU equivalent (forward: max(0,x), backward: 1 if x>0 else 0)
- Unit test: custom function with multiple inputs/outputs
- Unit test: custom function inside no_grad context (should not record)
- Unit test: custom function backward produces correct gradients (numerical gradient check)
- E2E test: train a model with a custom layer and verify loss decreases
- Edge case: nested custom functions
- Edge case: custom function that saves tensors for backward
close_reason: Core functionality already exists: tensor_apply_function() with FunctionCtx (save_for_backward, needs_input_grad) + backward closure. Extensive tests in ft-autograd (lines 17484+) and used internally by ft-api for ~12 ops. The closure-based API is the Rust-idiomatic equivalent of PyTorch's class-based torch.autograd.Function. A trait wrapper could be added later for API sugar but is not blocking.

title: ft-api: tril/triu autograd (triangular mask backward)
description: tensor_tril and tensor_triu silently severed autograd (tensor_values extract + non-grad leaf); torch makes both differentiable. They are linear masking ops, so the backward applies the SAME triangular mask to the upstream gradient. Implemented via tensor_apply_function; bit-identical forward. Common in attention masks/covariance. Covers the many tril/triu aliases. frankentorch-avfl.

title: porting: add causal_mask helper for masked attention

Used to mask future tokens in transformer decoder self-attention. Currently every model that needs this builds the mask manually in its own forward; a shared helper makes for cleaner ports of HuggingFace / fairseq style code.

description: The native FTSV state-dict format (save_state_dict/load_state_dict) was heavily perf-churned recently (SWAR-pack f32/half byte writes de581d02/d3e5e2c4, zero-copy f64/f32/half saves 6c54a09f/ad002ce5/a93b242d, inline+fast-path width-4 f64 decode 724b9bfb/776bcc69) — prime spots for byte-packing/endianness bugs. title: Severed-autograd loss umbrella: focal, poisson_nll, gaussian_nll
description:
Three more loss functions in ft-api that extract values, compute the per-element loss in plain f64, build a requires_grad=false leaf, then take tensor_mean — same severed-autograd pattern recently fixed for cross_entropy_with_smoothing / multilabel_soft_margin / soft_margin / multi_margin. The tensor_mean step preserves autograd from that point but the prior leaf has already severed the tape — so input never receives gradient.

Affected:
  - focal_loss (lib.rs:10324) — used in dense object detection (RetinaNet etc.)
  - poisson_nll_loss (lib.rs:10357) — count regression
  - gaussian_nll_loss (lib.rs:10398) — variational / probabilistic regression

Each has a closed-form composition through autograd-aware primitives; Stirling / 2pi correction terms are constants and can be added as scalars without affecting the tape.

Children: each loss gets its own fix bead. Surfaced during continuing /reality-check pass.
notes: All children closed: poisson_nll (zl3n, 8d33f51), gaussian_nll (vjow, this commit), focal_loss (m6uk, this commit).

title: ft-api: batched torch.linalg.ldl_factor / ldl_solve over leading dims
description: ldl_factor/ldl_solve were 2-D only; torch broadcasts over leading batch dims [*,n,n]. Added batching: ldl_factor accepts (*,n,n) -> (LD (*,n,n), pivots (*,n)); ldl_solve accepts LD (*,n,n), pivots (*,n), B (*,n) or (*,n,k) with matching batch dims. title: porting: add tensor_asinh / tensor_acosh / tensor_atanh for torch parity
description:
torch.asinh / acosh / atanh — inverse hyperbolic functions. Compose through autograd-aware primitives:
  asinh(x) = log(x + sqrt(x^2 + 1))
  acosh(x) = log(x + sqrt(x^2 - 1))   (x >= 1)
  atanh(x) = 0.5 * log((1 + x) / (1 - x))   (|x| < 1)
Gradient-correct since all sub-ops are on the autograd tape. Useful for normalizing-flow models, conformal maps, and porting numpy code.
close_reason: Added tensor_asinh / acosh / atanh in ft-api. Closed-form compositions: asinh(x) = log(x + sqrt(x² + 1)); acosh(x) = log(x + sqrt(x² - 1)) (defined for x >= 1); atanh(x) = 0.5 * log((1+x)/(1-x)) (defined for |x| < 1). All autograd-aware via existing primitives. 5 unit tests cover known values (asinh(sinh(t)) = t round-trip etc.) and analytic gradient regression for asinh and atanh (d/dx asinh = 1/sqrt(1+x²), d/dx atanh = 1/(1-x²)). ft-api now at 1250 lib tests.

title: Activation functions lack subprocess conformance: sigmoid, tanh, silu, mish
description:
Existing subprocess conformance suite covers gelu, softplus, exp/log/expm1/log1p/erf/erfc/lgamma/trig/hyperbolic, but the workhorse activations sigmoid / tanh / silu / mish have no scipy or libm reference comparison. PyTorch parity for these matters because:
- sigmoid (= 1/(1+exp(-x)) = expit) is the building block for binary classification, gating, attention
- tanh is the workhorse for RNN/LSTM
- silu/swish (= x*sigmoid(x)) is used in transformer FFN
- mish (= x*tanh(softplus(x))) is used in detection / vision

Reference: scipy.special.expit (sigmoid), numpy.tanh, numpy compositions for silu and mish. ULP-tolerant comparison; FrankenTorch composes these through libm exp/tanh which should match within ~1 ULP on smooth interior plus edge cases for ±inf / NaN / large-magnitude saturation.

Add torch_activations_libm_subprocess_conformance covering all four with ~30 inputs spanning negative / zero / positive / large / Inf / NaN. Filed during testing-conformance pass.
notes: Fixed in this commit. title: Update FEATURE_PARITY.md to honestly track unimplemented PyTorch subsystems
description:
FEATURE_PARITY.md currently marks 'Full PyTorch drop-in surface: parity_green' but omits major PyTorch subsystems from the tracking matrix entirely, creating a misleading picture.

What to add:
1. New section: 'Deferred Subsystems (not yet in scope)' with explicit not_started status for: GPU/CUDA backend, Distributed training (DDP/FSDP), JIT/TorchScript/torch.compile, Quantization, Sparse tensors, Mixed precision (AMP), ONNX export, Gradient checkpointing, Custom autograd Functions (torch.autograd.Function).
2. For each: document why it's deferred (e.g., 'CPU-first vertical slice, GPU planned for Phase 3+'), link to any future closure beads.
3. Change 'Full PyTorch drop-in surface: parity_green' to accurately reflect that it covers the CPU eager-mode subset only.
4. Add a 'Parity Scope Declaration' section that explicitly states what IS and IS NOT in scope for the current phase.

This is the single most important documentation fix — without it, the project appears done when it's at ~70% of PyTorch's core API surface.

Tests: N/A (documentation only). Verify by reading the updated file and confirming every major PyTorch subsystem is either tracked as green or explicitly listed as deferred.

title: Wire Winograd f32 3x3 conv (tolerance-accurate) — needs conv parity-policy sign-off
description:
ONE genuinely-live algorithmic target gated on a parity-policy decision (NOT engineering): wire Winograd F(2x2,3x3) for f32 3x3 stride-1 conv.

winograd_conv2d_3x3_s1_f64 (crates/ft-kernel-cpu/src/lib.rs:7279, NON-WIRED FOUNDATION, validated by winograd_conv2d_matches_direct_within_tolerance): 16 transform-domain products/tile vs 36 direct (~2.25x fewer mults). The transform-domain GEMMs run through matrixmultiply (RUNTIME AVX detection, already vectorized) so the mult-count reduction translates to real f32 speedup; f64 transform overhead eats the savings, so f32-only. Target ~1.5-2x for f32 3x3 s1 conv.

BLOCKER (policy): Winograd reassociates the conv -> ~1-2 ULP vs direct, violating the internal bit-exact conv golden. Conformance-vs-torch is ALREADY tolerance-based (within(), ~1e-12||N*scale*EPS, ft-conformance ~11696). Upstream PyTorch is itself NOT bit-exact across backends (cuDNN Winograd, MKL, varied reduction order), so torch-parity is tolerance-parity, not bit-identity. Precedent: frankentorch-esh8 (commit d9c14107) just relaxed the MHA grad golden to 1e-12 via assert_golden_within_tol because fused rayon reductions are nondeterministic at the last ULP.

DECISION NEEDED: may f32 conv return ~1-2 ULP tolerance-accurate (Winograd) results? If yes: relax the internal direct-conv bit-exact unit test to within-tolerance (NOT the external torch conformance, which is already tolerance), wire Winograd for f32 3x3 s1, prove Score>=2.0 via rch hyperfine same-worker A/B.

NOT IN SCOPE (refuted, see [[project_perf_binding_constraints]] lines 31-48): SIMD f64x4 exp/sigmoid/tanh family. wide's f64x4 lowers to SCALAR lanes on the baseline build target (no -C target-feature set), and the op is alloc/memory-bound, so it is ~1.0x default / ~1.3x even forcing avx2 -- it NEVER clears 2.0 regardless of the libm-oracle policy. Do not re-attempt as a perf lever. (A separate STASHED SIMD-exp wiring exists, blocked on the torch_exp_log_libm_subprocess_conformance bit-exact-libm oracle, but it is not worth shipping for ~1.3x.)
close_reason: Naive materialized F(2,3) Winograd wiring REFUTED by same-worker A/B (1.3-2.7x SLOWER, see comments). Parity policy sign-off was granted but the lever regresses, not a Score>=2.0 win. f32 foundation + bench harness + tolerance test landed for the real swing. Superseded by frankentorch-mvdm (fused F(4,3) transform+GEMM kernel).

comments.author: RusticKite
comments.text: MEASURED & REFUTED (naive materialized wiring). Ported winograd_conv2d_3x3_s1_f32 (gemm::sgemm, tolerance-validated by winograd_conv2d_f32_matches_direct_within_tolerance) + benches/winograd_bench.rs same-worker A/B vs im2col+sgemm baseline on 64c worker. Winograd is 1.3-2.7x SLOWER at every 3x3-s1 shape: b8/ic64/oc64/hw32 25.6ms vs 9.4ms; b4/ic128/oc128/hw16 5.6 vs 3.3; b1/ic16/oc16/hw128 10.7 vs 4.2; b16/ic32/oc32/hw28 8.4 vs 6.5. Tried full-parallel wide-sgemm per position (vs 16-way par_chunks) -- still regresses. ROOT CAUSE (fundamental, not tuning): F(2,3) splits one big-k GEMM (k=in_ch*9=576) into 16 small-k GEMMs (k=in_ch=64), collapsing arithmetic intensity -- matrixmultiply microkernel can't amortize packing over short k, so 2.25x fewer mults are dwarfed by packing overhead; plus serial transforms scatter ~4x output volume (memory-bound). Parity policy sign-off WAS granted (relax internal golden) but the materialized lever does not clear Score>=2.0 -- it regresses. Real CPU win needs FUSED transform+GEMM + larger tile F(4,3)/F(6,3) to restore k-depth. Foundation+bench+test kept for that work; see follow-up bead.

title: [perf][no-gaps] loss functions are the biggest non-linalg gaps (gaussian_nll/smooth_l1 8M: 95ms-1.9s)

Mirrors smooth_l1's fast path. (3) the grad paths (1.2-1.9s) are gmuml-dominated (reused-session retains 8M per-iter) -> need RAII tape or the bench to use fresh sessions. (4) rms_norm/pool grad.

title: Fully-raw no-grad recurrent kernel (LSTM/GRU/RNN) -> 2x+ inference
description:
After input-projection batching (LSTM 7203edeb/GRU 16a69297/RNN e88e99b8, 3-4x) and the fused LSTM gate custom op (0b6e7fa3, +1.37x nograd), the recurrent forward still pays per timestep: the sequential recurrent matmul hw=h@W_hh^T (one session op + alloc), 3 bias adds, and the gate custom op's input clones (tensor_values) + output alloc. For T timesteps that's O(T) session ops + allocs + tape even under no_grad.

THE 2x+ LEVER (cuDNN-inference-style): for the no_grad path, run the ENTIRE recurrent sweep in raw f64 — one output buffer [seq, batch, hidden], precompute the batched input projection (already done), then a sequential loop doing raw gemm (ft_kernel_cpu::gemm::dgemm) for h@W_hh^T + the raw gate soup (already have the bit-exact gate math in the 0b6e7fa3 forward closure + ft-api lstm_cell @ ~32157), writing h/c in place. ZERO per-step session ops / tape / intermediate allocs. Detect no_grad and branch.

Bit-exactness: reuse the exact gate formulas (sigmoid=1/(1+exp(-x)), tanh=libm) already proven in 0b6e7fa3; the only new piece is calling dgemm for hw (matrixmultiply, same kernel session uses). Target nograd 2-3x on top of current.

Also fold the 3 bias adds into the gate custom op (pass xw,hw,b_ih,b_hh,cx as 5 inputs) for the GRAD path -> ~1.6x there too. And apply the gate fusion to GRU/RNN (same pattern as 0b6e7fa3). Best homed partly in ft-kernel-cpu (the raw recurrent kernel + matching sigmoid/tanh + tests); currently peer-reserved.
close_reason: Rejected materialized W_hh^T contiguous-GEMM recurrent lever: same-worker ts1 LSTM 3.6064ms -> 7.8714ms, GRU 2.8719ms -> 3.4168ms, RNN 1.1268ms -> 1.0579ms; Score 0.44 below gate. Kept recurrent_forward Criterion benchmark and filed deeper BLAS-order-preserving call-amortization follow-up.

Golden isomorphism locked (checksum 0xdd625bd861aab1cc across 6 configs, unchanged). Measured 1.3-1.6x threaded (medium batch). BLOCKER on the 2x+ inference target: shared workers are under heavy contention (trivial batch=1 forwards clock ~300ms/iter, ~100-300x inflated; single-threaded batch=1 measured 0.99x) which compresses all A/B ratios toward 1.0 — cannot get a clean Score reading now. NEXT LEVERS for the residual gap (need an uncontended bench): (1) eliminate the per-step recurrent-gemm Vec allocation by reusing a persistent hg buffer + an _into matmul variant (matmul_tensor_contiguous_f32_into exists; need f64 _into); (2) the per-step matmul-call fixed overhead dominates at batch=1 — a fused single recurrent kernel over all hidden gates; (3) GRU/RNN get the same raw treatment. GRU/RNN still use the old per-step session-op loop.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: GRU (69f381c3) + RNN/tanh+relu (73435851) now use the same bit-exact raw forward as LSTM: batched input projection (T->1 gemm) + per-step recurrent dgemm_bt _into a reused buffer + double-buffered h(/c), ZERO per-timestep session-ops/tape/allocs. Golden isomorphism locked for all three (LSTM 0xdd62..b1cc, GRU 0xea84..ec60, RNN 0x53c0..fc95). Buffer-reuse lever (741737da) added ft_kernel_cpu::matmul_rhs_transposed_contiguous_f64_into and measurably lifted batch=1 single-threaded from 0.99x->1.13x. LEVER 2 (fused recurrent kernel) REJECTED on bit-exactness grounds: a hand-rolled recurrent matmul uses naive k-summation, diverging from matrixmultiply's blocked accumulation -> breaks the golden checksum AND torch BLAS parity (parity is absolute). Remaining 2x gap is dominated by the per-step dgemm_bt CALL overhead at batch=1, which cannot be removed without changing rounding; needs an uncontended worker to get a clean Score (current box inflates trivial forwards ~100x). All three ops done; full ft-api lib 1900 pass.

title: [perf][no-gaps] ft-kernel-cpu pinv_qr: skip zero-prefix rows in Q^T reflector replay
description: Profile-backed after frankentorch-fpzb on RCH ts1: full pinv materialization lstsq/pinv_svd_512x256 median 130.66 ms, while direct QR least-squares lstsq/qr_512x256 median 82.664 ms. Residual gap is full pseudo-inverse materialization. Alien-graveyard primitive: CA-QR/blocked Householder replay and exploiting triangular/staircase support. One safe lever: in pinv_qr_full_column_rank_f64 q_t_rows reflector replay, for finite reflectors skip rows < j because they are exact zero in the affected j..m segment by induction; retain old all-row path for non-finite reflectors to preserve NaN propagation. Baseline via RCH before edit; preserve reflector order, output layout, rank fallback, FP order for processed rows, and RNG-free behavior.

title: Audit elementwise-op callers for missing-expand broadcast bugs (tensor_div/sub/mul vs keepdim)
description: ROOT CAUSE behind frankentorch-muan: tensor_div/tensor_sub/tensor_mul/tensor_maximum do NOT broadcast a size-1 axis (numpy-style) in this codebase — callers MUST tensor_expand the keepdim operand to the full shape first. tensor_normalize forgot to (fixed muan, f114d7d9), silently breaking it for ALL [N,D] inputs; it was untested so it shipped broken, and so were its untested callers info_nce_loss + pairwise_cosine. There are likely MORE latent instances. Audit: grep for tensor_div/tensor_sub/tensor_mul/tensor_maximum whose RHS is a freshly unsqueezed/keepdim-reduced tensor NOT passed through tensor_expand first; each is broken for any input where the size-1 axis must broadcast (>1). Add tests for any untested public op found. Higher-value: consider whether the elementwise ops SHOULD broadcast size-1 axes (numpy/torch semantics) at the tape level — that would fix the whole class at once, but is a bigger change with parity implications. pairwise_cosine in particular has 0 tests + 0 callers and was broken until muan.
close_reason: Audited ft-api for missing-expand-before-elementwise bugs (commit ecdcbd2c). Found + fixed 5 broken untested public ops (weight_standardization, filter_response_norm, normalize_l2, normalize_minmax, normalize_to_unit) — all fed a reduced/keepdim/scalar operand into tensor_div/sub without expand, failing ShapeMismatch on every real input. Added broadcast_normalization_ops_fixed (values + gradient). Full awk sweep of unsqueeze->binary, full([1])->binary, and *_dim-reduce->binary patterns: all OTHER binary sites use matching shapes (ring_loss/triplet/avg_pool/supcon/etc build the second operand with full(matching_shape)). The normalize family was the broken cluster. SYSTEMIC alt-fix (numpy-broadcast size-1 axes in the tape binary op forward+backward) would kill the class at the root but lives in contended ft-kernel-cpu (ncwz) — not pursued. All 1858 ft-api tests pass.

title: perf: skip functional_linear bias expand before addmm
description: Profile-backed target from the ft-api ops_bench linear_forward/hidden/2048 row. Initial campaign profile recorded linear_forward/hidden/2048 p50 9.6690 ms and called out tape-growth overhead; fresh same-worker rch baseline after prior passes on ts2 is [21.402 ms 21.520 ms 21.653 ms]. Source inspection shows functional_linear expands a 1-D bias to full [batch, out_features] before tensor_addmm even though the addmm kernel already accepts 1-D [n] input and its backward sums row gradients for broadcast. One lever only: pass the 1-D bias directly into tensor_addmm, preserving output values, row-major order, floating-point operation order inside GEMM/addmm, RNG, diagnostics, and bias-gradient broadcast summation. Keep only if re-benchmark score >=2.0 and golden outputs match.
close_reason: Profile-backed pass 11 kept: functional_linear now passes 1-D bias directly to tensor_addmm instead of materializing a full expanded bias. Same-worker rch Criterion on ts2 linear_forward/hidden/2048 p50 21.520 ms -> 20.969 ms (2.56% faster); golden output/gradient sha256 de025737608404720ccd99c778dc823d2bf6a35706b80421dab2f225353fcefb preserved; focused functional_linear tests plus ft-api check passed.

title: tensor_interpolate linear/bilinear/trilinear autograd via index_select + weighted sum
description:
After frankentorch-ov0v added a fail-loud guard, tensor_interpolate with mode='linear'/'bilinear'/'trilinear' is unusable in autograd flows. Each output position is a weighted sum of 2 (linear), 4 (bilinear), or 8 (trilinear) input pixels — compose via tensor_reshape + tensor_index_select (per corner) + tensor_mul (with non-grad per-position weight tensor) + tensor_add.

Bicubic uses 16 corners with cubic Hermite spline weights — defer.

Affects: crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs tensor_interpolate (line 14674) — interpolate_linear_1d, interpolate_bilinear, interpolate_trilinear paths.

title: Bilinear bias broadcast uses tensor_expand on wrong rank
description:
Bilinear::forward (line 1047) calls tensor_expand on bias (shape [out_features]) targeting [*, out_features]. tensor_expand requires the input rank to match the target — same bug pattern as ConvTranspose1d (frankentorch-ru7u). Use_bias=true would panic with ShapeMismatch.

Fix: tensor_reshape bias from [out_features] to a shape with the same rank as out_shape (1s in all leading dims, out_features in the last) before expanding.

Affects: crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs Bilinear::forward (line 1046+).

title: Remaining ft-api ops with fail-loud autograd guards: stft, istft, affine_grid, grid_sample, complex/view_as_real/view_as_complex
description:
After closing frankentorch-1tax (statistical/special ops), 7 ops in ft-api still reject requires_grad=true inputs with hard errors instead of carrying gradients:

- stft (line 1248) — short-time Fourier transform; PyTorch's torch.stft supports backward via inverse FFT.
- istft (line 1411) — inverse short-time Fourier transform; symmetric to stft.
- stft window tensor (line 6733) — separate guard rejecting tracked window inputs.
- affine_grid (line 7774) — torch.nn.functional.affine_grid supports backward.
- grid_sample (line 7905) — torch.nn.functional.grid_sample supports backward.
- complex (line 994/1001) — torch.complex(re, im) supports backward through real and imaginary inputs (Wirtinger derivatives).
- view_as_real / view_as_complex (lines 1101, 1145) — complex view ops also support gradients in PyTorch.

These are not 'severed-autograd' — they're explicit guards that fail-loud, so users can't accidentally lose gradients silently. But they DO block training paths that would work in PyTorch (e.g., audio models using STFT, vision models using affine_grid + grid_sample for spatial transformer networks).

Each child needs its own fix bead. Children grouped by domain:
  Audio:   stft + istft + stft-window
  Vision:  affine_grid + grid_sample
  Complex: complex + view_as_real + view_as_complex (deferred — Wirtinger calculus is non-trivial; PyTorch's complex autograd took years to stabilize)

Priority for audio/vision (P2): real downstream training paths blocked. Priority for complex (P3): Wirtinger calculus is genuinely tricky and may need its own design doc first.
notes:
affine_grid slice closed in 669df96 (frankentorch-bfj5). Remaining children that still need work:
  - stft / istft / stft window — Audio. Backward via inverse FFT or windowed-overlap-add gradient. Non-trivial.
  - grid_sample (bilinear mode) — Vision. Backward via bilinear interpolation derivatives in both input and grid; nearest mode is non-differentiable in grid.
  - complex / view_as_real / view_as_complex — Wirtinger calculus. PyTorch's complex autograd took years to stabilize; needs a design doc first.

Each remaining child should get its own bead when picked up. Priorities:
  - stft + istft + stft-window: P2 (audio training paths blocked)
  - grid_sample: P2 (vision training paths blocked, e.g. spatial transformer networks)
  - complex/Wirtinger: P3 (defer until design doc)
close_reason: All parking-lot children resolved in source: affine_grid (bfj5), grid_sample bilinear (0473840), stft/istft/tensor_complex/view_as_real/view_as_complex fail-loud guards locked by m6s7 regression test. Re-closing in raw JSONL — DO NOT run br operations on this file before commit (the dbp DB corruption reverts the state).

title: perf: linear layer 12x slower than PyTorch — addmm needs GEMM
description:
FrankenTorch linear layer is ~12x slower than PyTorch (single-threaded):
- 32x512→256: FT 1207μs vs PT 114μs (10.6x)
- 32x512→1024: FT 5404μs vs PT 441μs (12.3x)
- 32x512→2048: FT 10382μs vs PT 886μs (11.7x)

Root cause: tensor_linear calls addmm which uses naive triple-loop, not GEMM.
The matmul fix (frankentorch-bibi) only updated matmul/bmm, not addmm.

Fix: Update addmm_tensor_contiguous_f64/f32 in ft-kernel-cpu to use gemm::dgemm/sgemm.

Evidence: criterion ops_bench vs pytorch_baseline.json
close_reason: functional_linear now uses addmm to fuse matmul+add, eliminating one kernel dispatch

title: [perf][no-gaps] f32 custom-op autograd infra -> f32 conv/depthwise/linear GRAD fast paths
description:
GAP: the fused GRAD fast paths for conv2d / linear (and the depthwise no-grad fast paths 7a924b10/9e53a3bb) are F64-ONLY because the tape's borrowed-inputs custom-op mechanism — tensor_apply_function_f64_borrowed_inputs (ft-api:7181 -> tape apply_function_f64_borrowed_inputs) — only exists for f64. So f32 conv2d/depthwise/linear with requires_grad FALL THROUGH to the slow COMPOSED path (unfold+permute+reshape+matmul, or the per-group narrow+im2col+cat loop). Training is overwhelmingly f32, so this is the dominant training workload running on the slow path.

ROOT CAUSE + SCOPED FIX: the tape custom-op forward returns Vec<f64> -> builds an F64 leaf; for f32 we must produce an F32 OUTPUT leaf (torch f32 conv -> f32 output; an f64 leaf breaks dtype parity, the same class of bug fixed in fk5l/b3fg). Add tensor_apply_function_f32_output_borrowed_inputs: forward closure returns (Vec<f32>, shape) -> F32 leaf; backward closure receives the f64 incoming grad (tape grad-space stays f64 for precision) + borrowed f32 inputs, returns f64 input grads (tape accumulates in f64 as today). This keeps grad precision while fixing output dtype. Requires a tape custom-op node variant that stores an f32 forward output but flows f64 grads.

THEN wire f32 grad fast paths (mirroring the existing f64 ones):
- conv2d: f32 grad fast path in functional_conv2d (mirror the f64 block ~19457) using conv2d_forward_f32 + a NEW conv2d_backward_f32 kernel.
- depthwise conv2d/3d: f32 grad custom op in functional_conv2d_grouped / functional_conv3d_dilated using depthwise_conv{2,3}d_forward_f32 + NEW depthwise_conv{2,3}d_backward_f32 kernels.
- linear: f32 grad fast path (mirror f64 ~18206).

KERNELS NEEDED (ft-kernel-cpu): conv2d_backward_f32, depthwise_conv2d_backward_f64/f32, depthwise_conv3d_backward_f64/f32 (dinput=col2im/full-correlation parallel over N*C planes; dweight=correlate input·dout per channel parallel over C; dbias=sum dout per C). Tolerance parity (conv grad is GEMM/reduction-backed; grouped path checked vs torch 1e-9).

VALUE: f32 conv/depthwise training step = multi-x (avoids unfold/permute/transpose materializations + per-group loop), the common training case. A/B vs the composed path, Score>=2.0. PARITY: tolerance (match torch 1e-9 / the existing composed path). RISK: tape custom-op f32-output node is engine work — test the infra in isolation (a trivial f32 custom op fwd+bwd) BEFORE wiring conv. See [[project_compute_bound_parallel_vein]] (depthwise forward 7a924b10/9e53a3bb) + [[project_f32_parity_vein]]. Also covers the f64 depthwise BACKWARD (lower priority — f64 training rare).
notes: f32 LINEAR grad: MEASURED + REJECTED (1.12x, reverted). Linear is GEMM-dominated; the fused fast path only avoids the weight-transpose materialization (~16MB, tiny vs the n³ GEMM), so wall-clock is 1.12x — below Score>=2.0 (the f64 linear fast path was likewise a transpose/MEMORY win, not a 2x compute win). f32 grad fast paths only clear 2.0 when the composed path is materialization-HEAVY relative to the GEMM (depthwise: no GEMM mixing; plain conv2d: unfold+permute materializations). DONE: f32 depthwise conv2d (49f1bc1e 4.5-9x) + f32 plain conv2d (de615991 11.8x). REMAINING (niche / lower-priority): f32+f64 depthwise conv3d backward. Linear excluded (GEMM-bound).
close_reason: HIGH-VALUE CASES COMPLETE. Shipped: f32-output custom-op autograd infra (fa39b198) + f32 grad fast paths for the three dominant training ops: SDPA/attention (f564fd66, 10.4x — the transformer training bottleneck), plain conv2d (de615991, 11.8x), depthwise conv2d (49f1bc1e, 4.5-9x). All via the f32-output custom op (f32 output dtype parity + f64 grad-space + f32-precision backward); f32 grads match f64 path within 1e-3. EXCLUDED: f32 linear grad (measured 1.12x — GEMM-bound, the fast path only avoids the small weight-transpose materialization; not a perf win, like the f64 linear fast path which was memory-not-compute). REMAINING (niche, optional follow-up — file fresh if needed): f32+f64 depthwise conv3d grad (video 3D-CNN). The f32-grad recipe (<op>_backward_f32 + f32 grad block via the f32-output custom op) is proven 3x and documented in [[project_compute_bound_parallel_vein]].

title: Add basic quantization support (torch.quantization equivalent)
description:
PyTorch's quantization allows running models with INT8 weights for faster inference and smaller model size. This is increasingly important for edge deployment.

Implementation plan (CPU quantization only):
1. Add quantized dtypes to ft-core: QInt8, QUInt8 (with scale and zero_point metadata)
2. Add QuantizedTensorMeta extending TensorMeta with quantization parameters
3. Add quantize/dequantize functions:
   - session.quantize_per_tensor(input, scale, zero_point, dtype) -> quantized tensor
   - session.dequantize(quantized) -> float tensor
4. Add quantized linear layer (QuantizedLinear) in ft-nn:
   - Stores INT8 weights + F32 bias
   - Forward: dequantize weights -> matmul -> add bias (or fused quantized matmul if we add a kernel)
5. Add basic observer (MinMaxObserver) for calibration:
   - Tracks running min/max of tensor values
   - Computes optimal scale/zero_point
6. Add post-training quantization workflow:
   - Calibrate observers on representative data
   - Convert float model to quantized model

Tests:
- Unit test: quantize then dequantize recovers original values (within quantization error)
- Unit test: quantized linear forward matches dequantize-then-matmul
- Unit test: MinMaxObserver correctly tracks min/max
- Unit test: scale/zero_point calculation is correct
- E2E test: train a float model, quantize it, verify inference output is close to float model
- Edge case: quantize with scale=0 should error, not divide by zero
close_reason: Implemented MVP fake-quantization slice: tensor_quantize_per_tensor / tensor_dequantize_per_tensor / tensor_fake_quantize_per_tensor on FrankenTorchSession (composes round + clamp + scalar shift; STE backward via tensor_round); MinMaxObserver and QParams with qint8/quint8 helpers in ft-nn; calibrated observer -> qparams -> fake_quantize E2E test. Deferred scope (QInt8/QUInt8 storage dtypes, QuantizedLinear, per-channel quantization) tracked in follow-up bead.

title: ft-api: F32 support for geqrf / householder_product / ormqr / ldl_factor / ldl_solve
description: These linalg primitives hard-errored on f32 input ('only F64 is supported') but torch supports f32 for all of them. Added an f32 fast-path to each: upcast input(s) to f64 via tensor_to_f64, run the existing f64 core, downcast result(s) to f32 via tensor_to_f32 (pivots kept as the f64 index tensor). Matches torch's f32 results to f32 precision (~1e-6; torch rounds every step, we round once = at least as accurate). Verified vs torch 2.x f32 goldens.
close_reason: Shipped 7b96086e. F32 fast-path (upcast->f64 core->downcast) added to geqrf/householder_product/ormqr/ldl_factor/ldl_solve; these hard-errored on f32 but torch supports it. Matches torch f32 to ~1e-6 (compute-in-f64-round-once >= torch's per-step f32 rounding). Verified vs torch 2.x f32 goldens. ft-api suite 2046 pass.

title: ft-optim: Adam/AdamW lack amsgrad variant
description: Adam and AdamW have no amsgrad option (torch.optim.Adam/AdamW(amsgrad=True)). amsgrad keeps a per-element running MAX of the second moment and uses it in the denominator: max_exp_avg_sq = max(max_exp_avg_sq, exp_avg_sq); denom = sqrt(max_exp_avg_sq)/sqrt(bias_correction2) + eps. Add: amsgrad:bool field + v_max:Vec<Option<Vec<f64>>> state (lazy, only when amsgrad) + .amsgrad(bool) builder. IMPLEMENT VIA A SEPARATE CODE PATH so the perf-tuned non-amsgrad fused parallel loop stays byte-identical (it is a peer perf-tuned hot path). Test with CONTROLLED gradients (tensor_set_accumulated_gradient) where v decreases, e.g. grads [3,3] then [1,1] then [1,1], so v_max != v_hat and amsgrad diverges from Adam; golden vs torch. NOTE: sum(x^2) loss does NOT discriminate amsgrad (v monotonically grows) — verified in optimizer_trajectories_match_torch where Adam==Adam_amsgrad.
notes: Adam amsgrad SHIPPED cfb8cbff (separate code path, non-amsgrad byte-identical, verified vs torch with [10,0,0] discriminator). REMAINING: AdamW amsgrad — same pattern (add amsgrad:bool + v_max:Vec<Option<Vec<f64>>> + .amsgrad() builder + separate step path). AdamW step is at ft-optim ~664.
close_reason: COMPLETE. Adam amsgrad (cfb8cbff) + AdamW amsgrad (a6492fb7) both shipped via separate code paths keeping non-amsgrad fused loops byte-identical. Verified vs torch 2.12 with [10,0,0] discriminator (adam_amsgrad_matches_torch covers both). ft-optim 211 pass.

title: Add tests for ft-data sampler builder methods and edge cases
