title: ft-api: torch.diff missing dim/prepend/append (only last-dim n-diff supported)
description:
tensor_diff only differenced the LAST dim with n iterations; torch.diff(input, n, dim, prepend, append) also takes an arbitrary dim (incl negative) and prepend/append tensors concatenated along dim before differencing (the standard length-preserving trick).

Added tensor_diff_full(input, n, dim, prepend, append); tensor_diff now delegates with dim=-1/None/None (existing behavior + tests unchanged). Composes via autograd-aware tensor_cat + narrow/sub so gradient flows to input, prepend, and append. n=0 returns input verbatim ignoring prepend/append (verified vs torch). length-<2 axis -> size-0 axis (torch parity).

Verified vs torch 2.12: A=[[1,3,6,10],[2,4,8,16]] diff dim0=[[1,1,2,6]], dim1=[[2,3,4],[2,4,8]], dim1 n2=[[1,1],[2,4]]; [1,4,9,16] prepend[0]=[1,3,5,7], append[25]=[3,5,7,9], n2 prepend[0]=[2,2,2]. Goldens diff_full_dim_prepend_append_torch_golden + diff_full_prepend_propagates_gradient. ft-api 1942/1942.
close_reason: Shipped tensor_diff_full(input,n,dim,prepend,append); tensor_diff delegates (existing behavior unchanged). Autograd-aware via tensor_cat+narrow/sub. Golden vs torch 2.12. ft-api 1942/1942.

title: tensor_linalg_solve severs autograd via direct LU kernel + non-grad rebuild
description:
tensor_linalg_solve in ft-api (line 11899) directly calls the LU factorize + LU solve kernels and rebuilds the result via session.tensor_variable(_, _, false). This silently severs autograd through every linear solve — used in normalizing flows, Bayesian models, optimization layers, GP regression, etc.

Fix: compose via tensor_linalg_inv + tensor_matmul. The linalg_inv was made autograd-aware in frankentorch-frq7 (-Y^T grad Y^T backward); composing X = inv(A) @ B delivers the standard gradient:
  dL/dB = A^{-T} dL/dX
  dL/dA = -A^{-T} dL/dX X^T = -A^{-T} (dL/dX) X^T

Trade-off: forward pass uses explicit inv instead of LU solve, slightly less numerically stable for ill-conditioned A. But the inv-based formulation matches PyTorch's autograd backward path, so gradient correctness is the same.

Affects: crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs tensor_linalg_solve (line 11899).

title: ft-api: quantile is flatten-only; torch supports dim=/keepdim= per-slice reduction
description: tensor_quantile/quantile_interpolation only computed the quantile over the FLATTENED tensor. torch.quantile(input,q,dim,keepdim,interpolation) reduces per-slice along dim. Added tensor_quantile_dim(input,q,dim,keepdim,interpolation) composing sort-along-dim + narrow (k-th order statistic of every slice sits at index k after sort) + lerp, with all 5 interpolation modes. keepdim keeps the reduced dim as size 1. Per-slice NaN propagation: max(isnan,dim) flags NaN slices, where() overwrites them with NaN (torch propagates NaN per-slice). Fully autograd-aware via the sort/narrow/lerp tape. Verified vs torch 2.12 (quantile_dim_matches_torch): dim0/dim1, keepdim, all modes, nan-slice, gradient. Follow-up: tensor-q (multiple quantiles) + nanquantile_dim.
close_reason: SHIPPED 217e2844. tensor_quantile_dim(input,q,dim,keepdim,interpolation): sort-along-dim + narrow + lerp, all 5 modes, keepdim, per-slice NaN propagation (max-isnan + where), autograd-aware. Verified vs torch 2.12 (quantile_dim_matches_torch: dim0/1, keepdim, modes, nan-slice, grad). ft-api 1930 pass. Follow-up: tensor-q + nanquantile_dim.

title: tensor_linalg_cond severs autograd via tensor_values + non-grad rebuild
description:
tensor_linalg_cond in ft-api (line 12858) severs autograd in both code paths:

1. p=±2 path: extracts svdvals to f64, picks sigma_max/sigma_min by index, computes ratio in plain f64, rebuilds via tensor_variable(_, _, false). Severs gradients to the input matrix through the SVD.

2. p != ±2 path: invokes tensor_matrix_norm (autograd-aware) twice but then extracts both norms to f64 scalars and multiplies in plain f64, rebuilding a non-grad leaf. The two intermediate norm tensors carry gradients but the multiplication is done outside autograd.

Fix:
- p=±2: use tensor_narrow + tensor_reshape to extract sigma_max (first element) and sigma_min (last element) as 1-element tensors, then tensor_div them. Handle the zero-divisor case via a small epsilon clamp or fail-loud guard since gradient at sigma=0 is undefined anyway.
- p != ±2: drop the two tensor_values calls; just call tensor_mul(norm_a, norm_inv) directly.

Affects: crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs tensor_linalg_cond (line 12858).

title: tensor_xlog1py masks NaN from y when x==0 (scipy propagates)
description: scipy.special.xlog1py(0.0, NaN) returns NaN — it short-circuits to 0 only when x==0 AND !isnan(y). FrankenTorch's tensor_xlog1py composes through where(x==0, 0, x*log1p(y)), so the where branch swallows NaN from y whenever x==0. This diverges from scipy/PyTorch on the (x=0, y=NaN) case. Fix: extend the mask to also exclude NaN y, e.g. where(x==0 & !isnan(y), 0, x*log1p(y)). Surfaced by torch_xlog1py_scipy_subprocess_conformance during the frankentorch-b5of work; current conformance test trims the failing case so the rest of the harness can land. Severity: low (NaN edge case, no observed downstream impact in tests), but breaks bit-exactness against scipy at the y=NaN boundary.
notes: Fixed in this commit: tightened the where mask in tensor_xlog1py from (x == 0) to (x == 0 && !isnan(y)) by composing tensor_eq + tensor_isnan + tensor_sub + tensor_mul. Now scipy / torch / FrankenTorch all return NaN at (0, NaN) instead of FrankenTorch's prior 0. Re-enabled the (0.0, NaN) case in torch_xlog1py_scipy_subprocess_conformance and added xlog1py_propagates_nan_from_y_when_x_is_zero regression test in ft-api.

title: Convert DenseTensor::storage() panic to Result return type
description:
## Context
The stub/placeholder audit (2026-04-02) found a single production panic!() in ft-core/src/lib.rs:971-974:

```rust
panic!(
    "called storage() on {:?} DenseTensor; use typed_storage() or contiguous_values_as_f64()",
    other.dtype()
)
```

This fires when `.storage()` is called on a non-F64 DenseTensor. Since FrankenTorch supports F32, F16, BF16, Bool, I32, I64, Complex64, Complex128 dtypes, this panic is reachable in normal usage — it is NOT a "should never happen" guard.

## Problem
A library crate should never panic on valid inputs. This violates the Deterministic Autograd Contract (DAC) principle of fail-closed behavior: panics are not catchable in a structured way and produce undefined recovery state.

## Plan
1. Change `fn storage(&self) -> &[f64]` to `fn storage(&self) -> Result<&[f64], DenseTensorError>` (or rename to `storage_f64()` and return `Result`)
2. Add a new `DenseTensorError::UnsupportedStorageAccess { dtype: DType }` variant if not already present
3. Update ALL callers of `.storage()` in ft-autograd, ft-api, ft-kernel-cpu, ft-dispatch to handle the Result
4. Run full workspace tests + clippy to verify

## Acceptance Criteria
- Zero panic!() calls in non-test production code across all crates
- All callers propagate the error properly
- Existing tests still pass

## Risk
Medium refactor — `.storage()` may be called in many places. Use `ast-grep` to find all call sites before starting.
close_reason: DenseTensor::storage() now returns Result with UnsupportedStorageAccess errors; updated ft-api call sites and verified workspace test/check/clippy via rch

Implemented via tensor_apply_function tracking original indices through the NaN-filter+sort; bit-identical forward. FD + scatter verified. Covers functional_/=_tensor aliases. frankentorch-o4j2.

comments.author: RusticKite
comments.text: FRESH VEIN found while hunting after the einsum rejection (bkt6). tensor_linalg_inv (ft-api ~42139) and siblings (solve/det/cholesky/slogdet) ONLY handle 2-D input ('if shape.len() != 2 ... Err'). torch.linalg.{inv,solve,det,cholesky,...} all support BATCHED [*, n, n] (broadcast batch dims). This is a behavior-PARITY gap (NO-GAPS) AND a clean parallelization: the batch items are INDEPENDENT -> rayon par_chunks over the batch calling the existing 2-D contiguous kernel per item, bit-exact. Both a feature (torch parity) and perf (parallel over batch). Multi-op (each needs the batched wrapper + grad), dedicated multi-session turn. Non-contended (ft-api, not the eigh/SVD peer lane). Start with batched inv+solve+det (reuse inv_tensor_contiguous_f64 / lu_solve_mixed_refine / det_contiguous_f64 per batch item).

With this, conv1d/2d/3d + conv_transpose1d/2d ALL cover stride/padding/groups/dilation; only conv_transpose3d remains (no functional, needs build-out). title: tensor_linalg_lstsq autograd via pinv + matmul for full-rank A
description:
tensor_linalg_lstsq currently uses tensor_linalg_svd internally which now fails loud on requires_grad inputs (frankentorch-rl4g). For full-rank A, the least-squares solution is X = A^+ @ B where A^+ is the pseudoinverse — which is now autograd-aware (frankentorch-d3gs).

Add an autograd-aware path that composes lstsq via tensor_linalg_pinv + tensor_matmul. The SVD-based path stays for non-grad inputs to handle rank-deficient cases.

Affects: crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs tensor_linalg_lstsq (line 12906).

title: log_softmax kernel loses ~1000 ULPs at large magnitudes due to operation ordering
description:
log_softmax_dim_tensor_contiguous_f64 (and the f32 variant) computes
    log_sum_exp = max + log(sum(exp(x - max)))
    output[i] = x[i] - log_sum_exp
which evaluates as x[i] - (max + log(sum_exp)). When x ~ 1000, max ~ 1000, the intermediate max + log(sum_exp) is ~1000-ish but the final result is ~−2; the catastrophic cancellation between two ~1000-magnitude values discards ~13 digits of mantissa.

scipy.special.log_softmax is mathematically equivalent but algebraically rearranged:
    output[i] = (x[i] - max) - log(sum_exp)
This keeps the intermediate small (~ −2 for x near max) and preserves full f64 precision.

Surfaced by torch_softmax_scipy_subprocess_conformance during the /reality-check pass: at large_positive_1d=[1000,1001,1002], FrankenTorch returns -2.4076059644444285 vs scipy's -2.4076059644443806 — a diff of ~5e-14 (about 1000 ULPs), well above the libm noise floor.

Fix: rewrite the output loop to compute (x - max) - log(sum_exp). The fast path (inner_size == 1) and the general path both need the change. Affects log_softmax_dim_tensor_contiguous_f64 in crates/ft-kernel-cpu/src/lib.rs:2134 and the f32 variant at line 5729.

Severity: medium — the precision gap matters for cross-entropy training with logits in extreme magnitudes (e.g., overconfident classifiers in late training). Most practical inputs stay within ~1e2 magnitude where the gap is negligible.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Rewrote log_softmax_dim_tensor_contiguous_f64 (and the f32 variant) to compute (x - max) - log(sum_exp) instead of x - (max + log(sum_exp)). The two are algebraically identical but the new form keeps the intermediate small (~result-magnitude) instead of ~max-magnitude, which preserves full f64/f32 precision through the subtraction. softmax conformance now passes large_positive_1d and large_negative_1d cases bit-for-bit with scipy.

title: Differentiable linalg_lu (P/L/U) + lu_solve backward (follow-up to lu_factor autograd)
notes:
PROGRESS: lu_unpack backward SHIPPED b68d00f3 (pure scatter, two-node pattern; composes the lu_factor->lu_unpack chain). Now differentiable: lu_factor (907419a1), linalg_lu (992d600c), lu_unpack (b68d00f3). FD-verified each.

LAST REMAINING: tensor_lu_solve (~lib.rs:45389) — solves A X = B from packed LU + pivots + B. Two differentiable inputs (lu_packed, b). Backward: grad_B = A^-T grad_X (solve A^T y = grad_X reusing the LU factors); grad_A = -grad_B X^T; map grad_A -> grad_packed via gL=stril(P^T grad_A U^T), gU=triu(L^T P^T grad_A). Needs the forward solution X saved + A^T solve. Pattern + FD harness established (lu_backward + 3 FD tests in crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs).
close_reason: COMPLETE: all four LU primitives now differentiable + FD-verified. lu_factor (907419a1), linalg_lu (992d600c), lu_unpack (b68d00f3), lu_solve (81d87ab6). Shared helpers lu_backward + lu_solve_backward in ft-api; 4 finite-difference tests (lu_factor/linalg_lu/lu_unpack/lu_solve _backward_matches_finite_difference) cover real-eigen pivot paths + multi-column B; fail-loud regression test now asserts all four are autograd-aware. 208 ft-api lu tests pass. Closes the LU-backward gap (was sn58's deferred work).

title: [perf] conv1d ~5x slower than its GEMM-equivalent (im2col/reorg overhead in conv2d_forward_f64 for height-1 / tall-skinny shape)
description: PROFILE-BACKED (ops_bench, degraded worker vmi, so use RELATIVE/same-worker ratios). conv1d/nograd_L/1024 (n=8,ic=64,oc=64,k=3,L=1024) = 41.2ms but its equivalent GEMM [8*1022,192]@[192,64] ~100M MACs should be ~1-2ms at the same worker's matmul rate (matmul/square/256=1.28ms for 16.7M MACs) -> ~5x+ overhead beyond the matmul. conv1d/nograd_L/4096 = 160.7ms (same ~5x). conv1d/grad_L/1024 = 193ms (4.7x the forward); grad_L/4096 = 685ms. ROOT: conv1d reshapes [N,C,L]->[N,C,1,L] and routes through functional_conv2d -> no-grad F64 fused conv2d_forward_f64 (ft-kernel-cpu) which does 'parallel im2col + dgemm_bt + parallel reorg'. For the height-1, many-patches (8176), skinny-N (64) shape the im2col gather and/or output reorg dominate (~39ms vs ~1-2ms GEMM). LEVERS TO TRY (one each, same-worker A/B, bit-exact vs current): (1) a NATIVE 1D im2col/gather avoiding the height-1 2D detour + tall-skinny dgemm; (2) parallelize/vectorize the im2col gather and the output reorg in conv2d_forward_f64 if serial for this shape; (3) cache-blocked im2col to fit L1. NOTE: conv2d_forward_f64 lives in CONTENDED ft-kernel-cpu (rd1s/peers) — coordinate. Needs a NON-degraded worker for a clean Score>=2.0 baseline (this worker had matmul/1024 at 32ms vs ~13-24ms normal).
notes: PROGRESS (b7a5540e): conv no-grad padded-input now uses a local Vec (no retained tensor_pad node), cutting conv1d/nograd_L/4096 181ms->73ms same-worker. The conv KERNEL was never the issue (gmuml tape retention); this removes the largest single retained tensor per conv. Remaining conv1d-vs-GEMM gap is the other retained reshape nodes + general tape growth (gmuml).
close_reason: Fixed in 548b5a2b: conv1d no-grad forwards now compact their tape (4 retained nodes/forward -> 1). The conv KERNEL was never the gap (per gmuml root-cause) — it was autograd node retention on the reused session. Worker-immune within-process A/B: HEAD degradation ratio 1.139 (1202 nodes) -> compacted 0.999 (302 nodes), flat. Combined with last session's conv-pad fix (b7a5540e), conv1d no-grad retention is now at the floor (1 result leaf/forward, unavoidable without RAII). Grad-path retention is correct/required. title: Flaky 17-digit MHA golden fixture fails on main (mha_self_flat_reuse)
description: ft-nn::tests::mha_self_flat_reuse_golden_output_matches_fixture FAILS on clean main (verified by stashing all uncommitted work). The fixture artifacts/optimization/golden_outputs/ft_nn_mha_self_flat_reuse_frankentorch-l3mm.txt pins MHA fwd+bwd output at 17 sig-digits (:.17?). The forward values+loss match EXACTLY; only the GRADIENTS (x_grad, param_grad_0/2/4) differ at the last ULP (~1e-16). Root cause: the MHA backward now runs through fused SDPA-grad / Linear-grad kernels whose rayon parallel reductions accumulate in nondeterministic order, so a 17-digit golden is inherently flaky. Fix options: (a) regenerate the fixture AND make the reductions deterministic, or (b) relax the test to tolerance (parse+compare numerically within ~1e-12) instead of exact string match. Added by commit c757d493 (frankentorch-m9qp). NOT caused by the layer_norm grad fusion (7f838cb4).
close_reason: Fixed: mha_self_flat_reuse golden now compares numerically within 1e-12 tolerance instead of exact 17-digit string match. Forward values/loss are deterministic; gradients flow through fused SDPA-/Linear-grad rayon reductions whose last-ULP order is nondeterministic across workers — a 17-digit exact golden was inherently flaky. Tolerance is ~10000x above the ~1e-16 noise floor, still catches real divergence.

title: Add E2E training test for Embedding + Mean + Linear text classifier
description:
Add a perfect-e2e regression for the typical bag-of-words text classification head: Embedding → mean over tokens → Linear → cross_entropy. Validates the Embedding's index_select autograd path end-to-end through Adam.

- Assert final loss < initial * 0.1

title: ft-nn PReLU and shrink activations sever autograd
description: Reviewer finding in crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs after the recent activation-gradient repair wave. Several activation modules still read tensor values, compute in plain f64, and return session.tensor_variable(..., false), so a requires_grad=true input produces a fresh untracked output and tensor_backward cannot propagate gradients. Concrete sites: PReLU::forward lines 3576-3642 reads both input and its learnable weight via tensor_values and returns an untracked leaf, so both input gradients and the PReLU weight gradient are dropped; Softshrink lines 3918-3938, Hardshrink lines 3966-3978, Tanhshrink lines 3991-4000, Softsign lines 4014-4023, Softmin lines 4046-4057, and RReLU lines 4154-4168 have the same severed-input pattern. Existing tests for these modules are forward-only (for example prelu_default_forward/prelu_has_parameters/prelu_multichannel, softshrink_shrinks, tanhshrink_values, softsign_bounded, softmin_is_softmax_of_negated, rrelu_module_*), and the full ft-nn test suite still passes: rch exec -- cargo test -p ft-nn reported 508 passed on 2026-04-29. Repro by code inspection: create a requires_grad=true input, run PReLU::forward (or any listed activation), reduce with tensor_sum, then tensor_backward; tensor_gradient for input is absent because the forward output is a requires_grad=false leaf. For PReLU, tensor_gradient for self.weight is also absent even though parameters() exposes that learnable slope. Fix by composing each activation through existing autograd-aware primitives (e.g. PReLU via tensor_where/tensor_mul with broadcasted weight; shrink ops via tensor_where; Softmin via tensor_neg or 0-input subtraction then tensor_softmax; RReLU via leaky_relu-style composition) and add regression tests asserting input gradients, plus weight gradients for PReLU.
close_reason: Composed all 7 listed activation modules through autograd-aware primitives: PReLU via tensor_reshape+tensor_expand+tensor_mul+tensor_where (now propagates to both input and learnable weight); Softshrink/Hardshrink via nested tensor_where + tensor_abs/tensor_sub/tensor_add + tensor_gt/tensor_lt; Tanhshrink via tensor_sub(input, tanh(input)); Softsign via tensor_div(input, 1+abs); Softmin via tensor_neg + tensor_softmax; RReLU via where(x>=0, x, slope*x). Added 8 gradient regression tests asserting analytical d/dx for each module (plus PReLU weight grads, both single-slope and multichannel). All 11 existing forward tests continue to pass — forward parity preserved.

title: porting: add tensor_isposinf + tensor_isneginf for torch parity

Two missing classification ops similar to existing tensor_isnan/isinf:
  - isposinf(x): 0/1 mask; 1 where x == +∞.
  - isneginf(x): 0/1 mask; 1 where x == -∞.
Compose via tensor_eq against a ±∞ constant. Non-differentiable (boolean masks); fail-loud on requires_grad like other classification ops.
close_reason: Added tensor_isposinf and tensor_isneginf in ft-api: 0/1 mask of element-wise x == ±∞. Composes via tensor_eq against ±∞ constants. Non-differentiable; fail-loud on requires_grad like other classification ops. 3 unit tests cover known values across {+∞, -∞, finite, NaN, -∞, 0}, requires_grad guard, and isposinf/isneginf disjointness. ft-api now at 1336 lib tests.

title: cdist p=2: matmul identity replaces broadcasted [P,R,M] sweep (50-62x)
description: tensor_cdist for p=2 (Euclidean, the dominant case AND torch's default compute_mode) composed through a broadcasted [P,R,M] (or [B,P,R,M]) difference tensor: unsqueeze+expand+sub+abs+pow(2)+sum_dim+pow(0.5). That materialises an O(P*R*M) intermediate (256x256x128 = 8.4M elements) and sweeps it 6+ times. Replaced with the matmul identity d^2=||x1||^2+||x2||^2-2*x1*x2^T: one GEMM-routed matmul/bmm for the cross term, two M-axis norm reductions, O(P*R) broadcast assembly, clamp_min(0)+sqrt. No O(P*R*M) intermediate. Same-binary A/B (thinkstation1): 256x256x128 361ms->5.8ms (62x), 512x512x64 709ms->13ms (54x). Autograd-aware (matmul/bmm/sum/clamp/sqrt on tape); grad (x1-x2)/d identical for distinct points; coincident points clamp to 0 (matches torch mm path). All 1853 ft-api tests pass; golden FNV digest pins output.
close_reason: Shipped commit 574001a2: p=2 cdist via matmul identity ||x1||^2+||x2||^2-2x1x2^T (torch default compute_mode). Same-binary A/B: 256x256x128 361ms->5.8ms (62x), 512x512x64 709ms->13ms (54x). Autograd-aware, grad identical for distinct points, golden digest pinned, all 1853 ft-api tests pass. Pushed main+master.

title: [parity-gap] FFT variants: rfft2, irfft2, hfft, ihfft
description:
Missing FFT variants from torch.fft:

- torch.fft.rfft2: 2D real FFT (real input -> complex output for non-redundant frequencies)
- torch.fft.irfft2: Inverse 2D real FFT (complex input -> real output)
- torch.fft.hfft: Hermitian FFT (complex Hermitian input -> real output)
- torch.fft.ihfft: Inverse Hermitian FFT (real input -> complex Hermitian output)

All can compose from existing 1D FFT primitives (tensor_fft, tensor_rfft, etc).

Ref: https://pytorch.org/docs/stable/fft.html
close_reason: done

title: ft-api: fft/ifft lack norm= modes (backward/forward/ortho)

Added tensor_fft_norm/tensor_ifft_norm(input, n, norm) + fft_norm_factor helper; tensor_fft/tensor_ifft delegate with 'backward' (existing behavior unchanged). norm is pure output rescale: fft backward x1 / forward x(1/N) / ortho x(1/sqrt N); ifft backward x1 (already /N) / forward xN / ortho x(sqrt N). Verified vs torch 2.12: [1,2,3,4] fft forward=[2.5,-0.5+0.5i,...], ortho=[5,-1+i,...]; ifft forward x4, ortho x2. Golden fft_ifft_norm_modes_match_torch_golden; invalid norm rejected; default == backward. fft suite 17/17.

title: ft-api: fft2/ifft2 lack norm= modes (multi-dim fft norm)

Added tensor_fft2_norm/tensor_ifft2_norm(input, norm) reusing fft_norm_factor(norm, rows*cols, inverse); default tensor_fft2/tensor_ifft2 delegate to backward (unchanged). Verified vs torch 2.12: arange(6).reshape(2,3) fft2 forward=÷6, ortho=÷sqrt(6); ifft2 forward x6, ortho xsqrt(6). Golden fft2_ifft2_norm_modes_match_torch_golden. fft family 18/18.

title: ft-api: fft_along_dim misreads complex input (split vs interleaved) — corrupts multi-dim fftn/ifftn
description: tensor_fft_along_dim read complex input via tensor_view_as_real then split it as vals[..half]=re / vals[half..]=im. But view_as_real produces INTERLEAVED layout (flat_map(|z| [z.re,z.im]) => [re0,im0,re1,im1,...]), as ifft/ifft2 correctly use (vals[i*2], vals[i*2+1]). So every transform after the first in fftn/ifftn (and any complex input to fft_along_dim) was corrupted: fftn(2D) gave (8,-6) for the DC term where fft2/torch give 15 (the sum). Not caught by existing parallel-vs-serial tests (both paths share the bad read). Fix: read interleaved. Now fftn==fft2==torch; full ft-api suite 1963/0. Found while adding fftn norm.
close_reason: Fixed: interleaved read. fftn/ifftn now match fft2/torch. ft-api 1963/0.

title: ft-api: fftn/ifftn lack norm= modes

title: ft-api: quantile only supports linear interpolation; torch has 5 modes (lower/higher/nearest/midpoint)
description: tensor_quantile only computed linear interpolation between order statistics. torch.quantile(input,q,interpolation=...) supports 5 modes: linear (default), lower, higher, nearest, midpoint (verified torch 2.12 on [1,2,3,4] q=0.3 -> 1.9/1.0/2.0/2.0/1.5; nearest uses round-half-to-even on the fractional index). Added tensor_quantile_interpolation(input,q,interpolation:&str); tensor_quantile delegates with linear. All modes compose through sort/narrow/lerp so autograd flows (lower/higher/nearest route to the single selected order stat, midpoint splits 0.5/0.5, linear splits 1-frac/frac). Golden test quantile_interpolation_modes_match_torch. Follow-up: nanquantile + dim-wise quantile could take the same param.

Autograd-aware via sort/narrow/lerp. Verified vs torch 2.12 (quantile_interpolation_modes_match_torch). ft-api 1928 pass. Follow-up: nanquantile/dim-wise quantile interpolation param.

title: [parity-gap] torch.nn.functional.celu
description: Continuously Differentiable ELU: celu(x) = max(0,x) + min(0, alpha*(exp(x/alpha)-1)). See https://pytorch.org/docs/stable/generated/torch.nn.functional.celu.html
close_reason: done

title: ft-api: f32 dtype-parity gaps in value-reading ops (errors or upcasts on f32 input)
description:
A probe across value-reading ops on f32 inputs found 11 broken (torch preserves f32; we error or silently upcast to f64). TWO root mechanisms.

MECHANISM A — composed op builds a CONSTANT via self.full(shape,val) which is F64, so a downstream div/mul/eq promotes the f32 result to f64 (upcast) or fails tensor_eq's matching-dtype check (hard error):
  cov, corrcoef, renorm  -> OK but dtype=F64 (silent upcast)
  xlogy, sinc            -> ERR 'tensor comparison requires matching f32 or f64 dtypes'
  (+ spherical_bessel_j0, same full+eq pattern, not in probe)
  FIX: build constants with full_like(reference,val) so they match the input dtype.

MECHANISM B — no-grad/direct path reads values via tensor_values / storage().to_vec() (F64-only) then rebuilds, so f32 hard-errors UnsupportedDType:
  pdist (p!=2), cdist (p!=2), nanmedian, kthvalue, copysign, logaddexp
  FIX: read via tensor_values_lossy_f64 + build output in the input dtype.

torch returns the input float dtype for all of these. Fix in mechanism-grouped commits with golden-value + dtype regression tests.
close_reason: Both mechanisms shipped. Mech A 58ab94aa (cov/corrcoef/renorm/xlogy/sinc/spherical_bessel_j0 via full_like dtype-matched constants). Mech B 3bccac4f (pdist/cdist/nanmedian/kthvalue/copysign/logaddexp lossy reads + dtype output; amax/amin narrowed). 11+ probed ops now preserve f32 (were erroring or upcasting). Tests f32_constant_ops_preserve_dtype + f32_value_read_ops_preserve_dtype (dtype + golden values + f64 no-regression); ft-api 1897 pass. Follow-up: deeper max_dim/min_dim/max/min widening filed separately.

title: ft-api: orthogonal-polynomial autograd (chebyshev_t/hermite_h/he/laguerre_l/legendre_p)
description: 5 orthogonal-polynomial special fns silently severed autograd; torch differentiable in x (degree n is non-diff const). Added special_unary_n_with_deriv helper. Derivatives via sibling-degree evaluators: T_n'=n U_{n-1}; H_n'=2n H_{n-1}; He_n'=n He_{n-1}; L_n'=n(L_n-L_{n-1})/x; P_n'=n(x P_n - P_{n-1})/(x^2-1). Bit-identical forward (par_map_f64 preserved), FD-verified all 5. frankentorch-avfl-poly.

title: Metamorphic: cat(chunk(x, n, dim), dim) == x bit-exact
description: Symmetric companion to fuzz_metamorphic_cat_narrow_round_trip: chunk a 2-D tensor along a chosen dim then cat the chunks back, asserting bit-exact equality with the original. tensor_chunk and tensor_cat are widely used in transformer / GRU / LSTM gate splits — a round-trip identity is a strong invariant on the autograd-aware tape implementation.
close_reason: Added fuzz_metamorphic_chunk_cat_round_trip — proptest with random 2-D inputs (shapes 1..=4 each axis, dim_size divisible by chunk count). Asserts cat(chunk(x, n, dim), dim) is BIT-EXACTLY identical to x — symmetric companion to fuzz_metamorphic_cat_narrow_round_trip. Both ops are pure layout (no arithmetic) so any deviation indicates a tape bug. ft-conformance now at 193 fuzz tests.

title: softmax / log_softmax lack subprocess conformance against torch/numpy
description:
tensor_softmax and tensor_log_softmax are the canonical activation/output functions for classification heads, attention layers, and most loss formulations. They have unit tests in ft-api but no subprocess conformance against an authoritative reference (torch.nn.functional.softmax / scipy.special.softmax / numpy). Bit-exact agreement matters because:

- Numerical stability: log_softmax(x) = log(softmax(x)) is computed via the max-subtraction trick to avoid exp overflow; the equivalence with the naive form must hold within libm precision across all inputs.
- Gradient stability: softmax/log_softmax forward/backward are coupled (dL/dx = softmax(x) - target for cross-entropy). Drift between FrankenTorch and PyTorch here corrupts every classification training run.
- Edge cases: large positive inputs (exp overflow risk), large negative (underflow), uniform input (all equal probabilities), single-element softmax (degenerate).

Add torch_softmax_torch_subprocess_conformance covering interior, large-magnitude, near-uniform, and edge-case inputs along multiple dims; assert ULP-tolerant equality against scipy.special.softmax (or numpy formulation if scipy unavailable). Same for log_softmax.

Bias toward filing-from-/reality-check: this is a pre-existing instance test gap, not a new bug.
notes: Closed by adding torch_softmax_scipy_subprocess_conformance covering interior, uniform, scalar, large-positive / large-negative (with max-subtract numerical stability), mixed-sign, and 1-D / 2-D / 3-D shapes across multiple dims. 8-ULP tolerance with 1e-15 absolute floor. Also surfaced the log_softmax precision bug tracked under frankentorch-ebrb (now fixed in the same commit).

title: [fix] EmbeddingBag rejects non-integer indices
description: EmbeddingBag::forward_with_offsets casts f64 index tensor values to usize before validating, so negative or fractional values can alias valid rows instead of failing closed. Require index values to be non-negative integers before padding/out-of-range handling and add inline no-mock coverage.

title: [test] DataLoader custom indices preserve duplicates and order
description: No-mock DataLoader invariant: with_indices is how replacement samplers feed duplicate samples into batching, so it must preserve exact index order including repeated indices. Add inline coverage that duplicate custom indices produce duplicate target rows without de-duplication or sorting.

Baseline via rch before edit; one lever only.

title: Implement tensor_linalg_inv autograd: grad_A = -A_inv^T grad_out A_inv^T [child of tw0y]
description:
Wrap tensor_linalg_inv in tensor_apply_function with the standard matrix-calculus backward:
    grad_A = -Y^T @ grad_Y @ Y^T   where Y = A^{-1}
Save Y (the forward output) for backward to avoid recomputing the inverse. Output shape is [n, n] for n×n input. F64-only autograd path; F32 input with requires_grad continues to fail-loud (consistent with the affine_grid pattern).

Add tensor_linalg_inv_propagates_gradient regression test in ft-api: for diagonal A, the inverse is reciprocal-diag and the gradient under sum loss simplifies to a clean closed-form for verification.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Wrapped tensor_linalg_inv in tensor_apply_function with the analytical backward grad_A = -Y^T @ grad_Y @ Y^T where Y = A^{-1}. Y is saved for backward to avoid recomputing the inverse; the matmul chain is implemented inline in the closure (n×n is small enough). Added linalg_inv_propagates_gradient_via_inv_transpose_outer regression test (diagonal A=[[2,0],[0,4]] under sum loss → grad = [-1/4,-1/8,-1/8,-1/16]). ft-api lib tests: 1140.

title: ft-api: 7 heavy numeric lib tests flake under full-suite parallelism (pdist/rnn/lobpcg)
description: Running the FULL ft-api --lib suite (1955 tests, default parallel) flakes ~7 tests: pdist_l2_matmul_matches_direct (lib.rs:83896), rnn_raw_forward_golden_isomorphism (108434), lobpcg_api_matches_eigh_extreme_pairs (85347), +4. They PASS in isolation and in scoped runs. Confirmed PRE-EXISTING (clean HEAD 39f75103 with no working changes also fails the same 7 under the full suite; a scoped run of the same tests passes). Likely rayon thread-pool contention / memory pressure when ~1955 tests run concurrently on the rch worker, or a numeric-tolerance test sensitive to parallel scheduling. Needs: pin these tests to a tolerance robust under contention, or mark them serial (cargo --test-threads or a serial guard), or reduce their working-set. Not blocking feature commits (verify scoped).
design: DIAGNOSIS (cc, 2026-06-07): NOT flaky/random — DETERMINISTIC test-ordering pollution. The 7 (cdist_l2_matmul_matches_direct_2d_and_batched, functional_conv2d_no_grad_fast_path_matches_composed_path_bit_exact, gru/lstm/rnn_raw_forward_golden_isomorphism, pdist_l2_matmul_matches_direct, lobpcg_api_matches_eigh_extreme_pairs) are ALL bit-exact GOLDEN-DIGEST tests (fnv/checksum of f64 output). They FAIL with a different digest but still pass their loose abs<1e-10 value checks => output drifts ~ULP. Ruled out: (1) rayon non-determinism — RAYON_NUM_THREADS=1 still fails the same 7 (ran 7.77s, serial confirmed); (2) cargo test-thread concurrency — --test-threads=1 (fully serial) STILL fails the same 7; (3) running the 7 alone as a group PASSES (0.29s), and each passes isolated. So the cause is a test EARLIER in full-suite order mutating bit-affecting GLOBAL state that leaks forward. No static mut/Atomic/OnceCell config in ft-kernel-cpu or ft-api. PRIME SUSPECT: x86 MXCSR FP control register (rounding mode or FTZ/DAZ denormal flush) toggled by some test's SIMD/unsafe path (matrixmultiply or a transcendental approx) and not restored — per-thread, so with serial execution it persists to the 7 and changes denormal/rounding behavior => digest drift. NEXT STEP: bisect the ~1948 preceding tests (binary search by name prefix or insert a MXCSR read/assert before each of the 7) to find the polluter; then restore FP state after it (or stop the global mutation). Alternatively relax these 7 to tolerance digests, but that weakens the perf-isomorphism guarantee. NOT my change (clean HEAD reproduces). Validate ft-api edits via SCOPED runs.
notes: SESSION-4: DETERMINISTIC confirmed — two full-suite runs produced BYTE-IDENTICAL computed (wrong) digests (cdist left=3018070494873948101, gru=14497563193174927436, etc. both runs). So it is deterministic state pollution, NOT a race. Op-FP-state theory ALSO REFUTED: in-process repro running 3000 iters of exp/log/tanh/sqrt/pow/div/neg/abs/clamp on denormal-prone (~1e-160) inputs between two pdist digests => NO drift. Second-ingredient bisection: base+transcendentals=366 PASS, +div=380 PASS, +abs PASS; base+both(448) FAIL — so the trigger is a narrow COMBINATION, not a single op class. CUMULATIVE REFUTED LIST: rayon (RAYON=1 fails), cargo concurrency (--test-threads=1 fails), single polluter, ID counter + allocator churn (40k allocs no drift), backward (467 grad tests pass), op computations (3000 transcendental+arith no drift). EVERY in-process repro passes; only the real multi-#[test] suite at ~448+ tests fails. ONLY remaining decisive experiment: instrument MXCSR (needs unsafe -> gemm module or test-only crate), read+assert ==0x1F80 before each golden in a full-suite run, to catch WHICH test leaves it dirty. The computed-digest drift is real (forward output depends on accumulated suite state) — a genuine determinism bug, just elusive. DO NOT retry: count/alloc/backward/single-op-FP repros (all refuted).
close_reason: ROOT CAUSE FOUND + FIXED: matrixmultiply SIMD micro-kernel sets x86 MXCSR FTZ/DAZ and does not restore it, leaking flush-to-zero into later FP ops on the same (libtest-reused) thread, so GEMM-heavy golden digests drifted ~1 ULP depending on whether a GEMM ran earlier. Fix: dgemm_mm/sgemm_mm wrappers restore MXCSR 0x1F80 after each matrixmultiply call (matmul output unchanged). Full ft-api 1960/0 parallel AND --test-threads=1 (was 7 failing). Guard: kernels_do_not_dirty_mxcsr.

title: [perf][no-gaps] ft-api: precompute ISTFT inverse-DFT twiddle table
description: Fallback profile-backed target because br ready --json had no unclaimed perf beads after a short wait. Profile target: ft-api Criterion ops_bench istft/nfft512_frames256 via rch on worker vmi1293453 baseline [200.55 ms 220.72 ms 243.49 ms]. Hotspot: istft_reconstruct_f64 computes each inverse DFT sample by rebuilding angle/cos/sin for every (frame,n,k) term. One lever: precompute exact inverse-DFT twiddle (cos(angle), sin(angle)) for each (n,k) once per call and reuse it across frames while preserving full_spectrum construction, n output order, k summation order, overlap-add order, scale/window/trim semantics, ledger text, and no-RNG behavior. Alien primitive: FFT-lineage twiddle table / communication-avoiding precomputation harvested from alien graveyard NTT/FFT twiddle-factor guidance and no-gaps directive. Proof obligations: representative ISTFT output sha256, serial reference bit equality via istft_parallel_match_serial_bit_exact, dtype/shape/order unchanged. Keep only if re-benchmark Score >= 2.0.
notes: Evidence correction by BoldOx: committed golden_istft_outputs.txt sha256 is 5f475c79d31d355c6ad764080067b57148c564532608833d2dc05f46802cade5 and the committed sha256 manifest verifies the ISTFT artifact files. Actual validation run for this bead: rch exec -- cargo test -p ft-api istft_parallel_match_serial_bit_exact -- --nocapture passed on worker vmi1149989; rch exec -- cargo check -p ft-api --all-targets passed on worker vmi1227854; git diff --check and hook-run UBS staged scan passed. The earlier note naming RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE runs on other workers was inaccurate.
close_reason: Completed ISTFT inverse-DFT twiddle precompute. Same-worker remote Criterion istft/nfft512_frames256 p50 220.72 ms -> 16.698 ms (13.2x faster); golden sha256 51e4bf2b630058b6f2cc2e36008e743f3d20e25e16dfd8e32090b6d794473cab; istft_parallel_match_serial_bit_exact and ft-api check passed via rch; score 10.0.

title: [perf] ft-nn: fuse self-attention QKV projection

One lever: for self-attention only, concatenate q/k/v projection weights and biases, run one functional_linear over q_flat, then narrow q/k/v chunks. Prior rejected families: key materialization, sorted-map construction, delayed materialization, rank-1 metadata, scalar payload collection, borrowed record index, and shared Arc<Vec<f64>> slab without a usable same-worker timing. Attack a deeper primitive: make tensor-local typed storage exposure or inline fixed-width F64 storage safe and observable, then decode many small F64 tensors without per-tensor Vec allocation. Preserve BTreeMap ordering, duplicate-key failure, malformed diagnostics, exact f64 raw bits, dtype/shape/storage_offset contracts, COW mutation independence, and golden SHA. Same-worker vmi1293453 baseline inline-disabled [355.39 us 365.38 us 377.13 us], candidate [278.56 us 287.41 us 295.62 us], median 1.27x. Proof: ft-core f64_inline 2/2, ft-serialize native_ 16/16, golden SHA OK, fmt/check/clippy passed, UBS exit 0. Score 2.54.

title: [parity] heaviside(nan, values) returns nan, torch returns 0.0
description: Differential probe vs torch 2.12: tensor_heaviside with a NaN input returns nan in ft, but torch returns 0.0 (confirmed torch.heaviside(nan,1)=0.0 and heaviside(nan,nan)=0.0). torch's heaviside treats a NaN input as the x<0 branch (returns 0), not NaN-propagating. LOW priority / quirky — verify the exact torch branch semantics before matching (heaviside(x,v)= 0 if x<0 else v if x==0 else 1; NaN apparently maps to the 0 branch). Probe: crates/ft-api/examples/diff_probe.rs.
close_reason: Fixed in cd899f1c: heaviside now computes (x>0)?1:(x==0)?v:0 eagerly (both out-of-place and in-place). heaviside(NaN,_)=0 (was NaN) and heaviside(-1,inf)=0 (was 0*inf=NaN) now match torch 2.12. Verified vs torch over diff_probe edge set (0 real mismatches); 6 heaviside tests pass; corrected the prior test that pinned wrong NaN-propagation. Pushed main+master.

title: [perf][no-gaps] fql10-C2: strict-fallback Schur-window shift-list kernel for geev/eigvals
description: Successor to closed frankentorch-npxbw. Profile evidence remains the non-symmetric Francis QR floor: vmi1227854 eigvals_f64_256x256 current gate [25.052 ms 25.370 ms 25.694 ms], pass5 profile n=256 sweeps=319 fallback=0 exceptional=0; n=1024 sweeps=1132 fallback=0 exceptional=0. Pass6 rejected a direct two-bulge/four-shift source hunk because the current EISPACK-style scalar sweep interleaves row/column updates and deflation after each single-bulge sweep; a real two-bulge schedule is not a one-pass strict eigvals_golden-preserving source edit. Build the next primitive instead: a standalone private Schur-window kernel / AED-derived shift-list artifact that owns active-window boundaries, bulge collision/end handling, Hessenberg invariant checks, exceptional-shift and max_total accounting, and strict scalar fallback for unsupported windows. Acceptance: profile first via rch crate-scoped rows; one lever only; strict eigvals_golden SHA 24ed0e24afc1b41d3b23198f60fc1d06727374bf3551c026941a25785b7c9725 for fallback; focused eig tests and ft-kernel-cpu check/fmt/clippy/UBS; keep only Score>=2.0 with same-worker before/after. Do not repeat range/index micro-cuts or diagnostic-only shift helpers.
close_reason: Rejected/rerouted: pass4 AED-derived shift-list source hunk changed strict n=256 eigvals digest from 0x00b87b4996340204 to 0xdaa6738e0f31a016; source removed and post-revert golden restored. Successor exact-shift blocked sweep primitive filed as frankentorch-8ubk3.

title: Own deterministic mul_add GEMM for bit-identical matmul across CPUs (replace matrixmultiply runtime FMA dispatch)
description: matrixmultiply selects its AVX2/AVX512/FMA microkernel by runtime CPU detection, so matmul output is not bit-identical across heterogeneous workers (root cause of the now-fixed qb1g2 test flake). NO-GAPS swing: build our own cache-blocked, register-tiled safe-Rust dgemm/sgemm whose inner accumulation uses f64::mul_add / f32::mul_add EXCLUSIVELY. f64::mul_add is IEEE single-rounding and bit-identical on every CPU (hardware vfmadd where present, correct soft-fma fallback otherwise) AND matches torch's FMA-BLAS rounding -> simultaneously cross-worker-deterministic and torch-bit-exact. Constraint: must stay perf-competitive with matrixmultiply on FMA hardware (register-blocked microkernel; mul_add lowers to vfmadd so FMA throughput is preserved). Verify: re-pin bit-exact matmul goldens, run on >=3 heterogeneous workers, same-worker GEMM bench A/B vs matrixmultiply (no regression). Once shipped, the cdist/pdist/lobpcg/lstm/gru/rnn goldens could be restored to bit-exact.
close_reason: Rejected scalar row-major mul_add GEMM pilot: proof test passed, but same-worker vmi1167313 f64 512 median regressed 8.3931 ms -> 232.25 ms (0.036x, Score 0.0). Source hunk removed; follow-up frankentorch-g1upz.1 targets packed/register-blocked deterministic panels.

title: [perf] Packed/register-blocked deterministic mul_add GEMM panels
description: Profile-backed follow-up after frankentorch-g1upz rejected scalar row-major mul_add GEMM: same-worker vmi1167313 f64 512 median regressed 8.3931 ms -> 232.25 ms, Score 0.0. Implement a fundamentally different safe-Rust GEMM primitive: pack B into cache-sized deterministic panels, use a register tile (start 4x4 or 6x4), keep explicit f64::mul_add accumulation order per output lane, and preserve row/column/output order. Baseline via crate-scoped rch before edits; one lever per commit; prove ordering/tie/FP/RNG/golden SHA; rebench same worker; keep only Score >= 2.0. Do not repeat scalar row-major replay.
close_reason: Rejected: packed/register std::simd FMA tile failed score gate. Same-worker vmi1156319 median 13.464 ms -> 232.03 ms (0.058x, 17.23x slower), Score 0.0. Source hunk removed; evidence in artifacts/perf/frankentorch-g1upz-1/report.md. Next child targets safe-Rust software-FMA microkernel/artifact, not another packed SIMD tile.

title: [perf] Safe-Rust software-FMA microkernel for deterministic GEMM
description: Follow-up after frankentorch-g1upz.1 rejected packed/register std::simd FMA panels: same-worker vmi1156319 f64 512 median regressed 13.464 ms -> 232.03 ms, Score 0.0. Attack a different primitive before revisiting macro-panels: derive and implement a safe-Rust software-FMA/microkernel artifact that avoids std::simd simd_fma's slow fallback path while preserving IEEE single-rounding semantics required for cross-worker deterministic GEMM. Baseline via crate-scoped rch; one lever; prove ordering/tie/FP/RNG and golden SHA; same-worker rebench; keep only Score >= 2.0. Target ratio: at least 8x faster than the rejected 232 ms class and then re-evaluate against matrixmultiply baselines.
close_reason: Rejected: row-parallel software-FMA GEMM median 13.464 ms -> 251.31 ms on vmi1156319 (0.0536x, Score 0.0); source hunk removed, proof/golden artifacts retained

title: [perf] Kulisch-binned deterministic GEMM accumulator tile
description: Follow-up after frankentorch-g1upz.2 rejected row-parallel software-FMA (vmi1156319 median 13.464 ms -> 251.31 ms, Score 0.0) and earlier scalar/packed/std::simd software-FMA families. Attack a fundamentally different deterministic primitive: a safe-Rust Kulisch/ExBLAS-style binned accumulator tile for GEMM dot products, with explicit rounding proof obligations and no C BLAS/MKL/XLA. Baseline via crate-scoped rch; one lever; prove ordering/tie/FP/RNG and golden SHA; same-worker rebench; keep only Score >= 2.0. Target ratio: at least 10x faster than the 251 ms software-FMA class before comparing against matrixmultiply baselines.
close_reason: Rejected by structural analysis (no new bench needed). The deterministic-GEMM vein is unwinnable on Score>=2.0: this bead's OWN target is '10x faster than the 251ms software-FMA class' = ~25ms, but the real matrixmultiply baseline is ~13ms, so even SUCCESS = ~2x SLOWER than baseline (Score ~0.5). ANY reproducible/binned accumulator (Kulisch/ExBLAS) is inherently slower than matrixmultiply's hardware-FMA blocked kernel (ExBLAS literature: 2-9x over naive non-reproducible, and naive < matrixmultiply) — replacing matrixmultiply with it REGRESSES matmul (a NEW NO-GAPS gap). This is the 5th attempt in the streak (scalar mul_add 0.036x, packed, std::simd, row-parallel 0.0, now Kulisch); per the NO-CEILING addendum a rejected-lever streak is a TRIGGER to STOP iterating this loop. The determinism GOAL (cross-CPU bit-stable matmul-backed test goldens) was ALREADY correctly resolved in qb1g2 by making those goldens tolerance-based (the only achievable answer — matching torch's exact FMA-blocked rounding requires replicating matrixmultiply bit-for-bit, which defeats the purpose). NEXT real perf swing instead = FFT-based conv for large kernels (convolution theorem O(N*K)->O(N log N), uncontended ft-api, Bluestein/radix-2 machinery already built) — a different complexity class, not a GEMM tweak.

title: in-place softplus_/mish_/logsigmoid_ + multilabel_soft_margin_loss use naive ln(1+exp(x)) -> overflow to +inf at large x; logsigmoid_(x>0) computed -ln1p(-x)=NaN
close_reason: FIXED (cb88d7a7): added stable_softplus helper (log1p(exp(x))+threshold-20, == kernel softplus_value) and routed tensor_softplus_/mish_/logsigmoid_ + multilabel_soft_margin_loss through it. logsigmoid_ was NaN for ALL x>1 (-ln1p(-x), missing exp) — now -stable_softplus(-x), bit-exact to out-of-place tensor_logsigmoid. title: Add fail-loud guards on non-diff'able ops: unique, unique_consecutive, histc, isin
description:
tensor_unique, tensor_unique_consecutive, tensor_histc, tensor_isin all extract tensor_values, run their discrete operation in plain f64, and rebuild non-grad leaves. Each is intrinsically non-differentiable (set/count/membership) — silent severance is benign in PyTorch parity terms but unhelpful when a user passes requires_grad=true.

Add fail-loud guards on requires_grad input — matches the bernoulli/poisson/L0/SVD parking-lot pattern.

Affects (all in crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs):
- tensor_unique (line 3514)
- tensor_unique_consecutive (line 3593)
- tensor_histc (line ~3760)
- tensor_isin (line 15644)

title: f32 create_graph over f32 leaf params ERRs UnsupportedDType (blocks f32 weight-grad-of-grad / full f32 WGAN-GP)
description:
The f32 conv double-backward INPUT-Hessian works (conv2d/grouped f32 verified vs
torch — frankentorch-lboou), but the WEIGHT-gradient-of-gradient path (the full
WGAN-GP: grad(sum(grad(loss,x,create_graph)^2), weight)) ERRs with
DenseTensor(UnsupportedDType(F32)) for BOTH conv2d-f32 and grouped-f32.

ROOT (suspected): the create_graph backward machinery hits an f64-only DenseTensor
op (ft-core lib.rs ~1155/1161 UnsupportedDType) when processing an f32 LEAF
parameter (weight/bias) that requires grad in 2nd-order mode. NOT a bug in the
conv cg closures — their input adjoints are verified, and the dweight/dbias nodes
are correct-by-construction (same recipe as the verified f64 + f32-input paths).
Likely the same dtype-awareness class as ng1hw's backward_create_graph numel/dtype
allocation gaps.

REPRO: f32 gradient-penalty over conv2d/grouped weights (see the f32 cg closures in
functional_conv2d / functional_conv2d_grouped, frankentorch-lboou). Affects ALL f32
second-order-over-f32-parameters, not just conv.

IMPACT: f32 input-Hessian (analysis, input-only penalties) works; full f32/mixed-
precision WGAN-GP weight updates via gradient penalty do not. f64 WGAN-GP is fully
supported (j4uio).
notes:
ROOT CAUSE PINNED (cc): the UnsupportedDType(F32) is thrown by
DenseTensor::from_typed_storage (ft-core lib.rs:1154-1155) on a meta.dtype() !=
storage.dtype() MISMATCH — NOT 'f32 unsupported'. So backward_create_graph builds
a grad NODE for the f32 leaf weight using the LEAF's F32 meta but F64 grad-space
storage → mismatch. FIX = make the create_graph grad-node construction
dtype-aware: grad nodes live in F64 grad-space, so build them with an F64 meta
(mirror the leaf SHAPE but force dtype=F64), the same way the regular backward
accumulates f64 grads onto f32 leaves via the length-generic
accumulate_tensor_gradient. Locate the grad-node build in backward_create_graph
(ft-autograd) — likely where it clones the input node's meta to make the grad
leaf/accumulator. HIGH BLAST RADIUS: this path serves ALL create_graph backward
(f64 works today) — verify the full f64 double-backward suite (conv/linear/special-
fn/linalg probes) stays bit-exact after the change, plus the f32 gradient-penalty
probes (conv2d/grouped) then match torch. Related: ng1hw (same dtype-awareness
class in the complex create_graph alloc).
close_reason:
Shipped e4313739: full f32 gradient-penalty (WGAN-GP) for conv2d-f32 + grouped/
depthwise-f32 now matches torch f32 exactly (pen/gw_sum/weight+bias grads).
ROOT was NOT a deep create_graph infra gap (the original framing): the f32 conv
cg closures built grad nodes via tape.apply_function, which infers output dtype
from its inputs — feeding the F32 weight/padded made the grad NODES F32, so the
F64-only readers (tensor_values/autograd_grad) rejected them. FIX: cast the F32
weight/padded inputs to F64 (tape.to_f64 -> differentiable CastF64) before the
nested apply_function, so grad nodes live in the F64 grad-space (kernel math stays
f32-exact). Verified vs torch (conv_f32_gp_values_probe). The general f32
create_graph-over-f32-leaf path was already fine (minimal elementwise f32
gradient-penalty worked) — this was conv-cg-specific.
Residual f32 tail tracked on lboou: conv3d-f32 (ERRs UnsupportedDType even for
input-Hessian — its f32 grad path is unwired / hits an f64-only op; distinct
issue) and conv_transpose2d-f32 (silent-zero Hessian — detachment). Both niche.

title: [perf][no-gaps] ft-kernel-cpu: mixed-precision iterative-refinement linear solve (~1.8-2x)
close_reason: Completed: mixed-precision iterative-refinement solve kept. Same-worker ts1 Criterion median improved lu_solve 512x512 rhs32 from 92.863 ms to 28.561 ms (3.25x). Proof max_diff 4.77e-18, max_residual 4.44e-16; checksums in artifacts/perf/frankentorch-gj8t/evidence.sha256.

comments.author: RusticKite
comments.text:
ALIEN-GRAVEYARD PRIMITIVE: mixed-precision iterative refinement (the classic LAPACK dsgesv / Wilkinson refinement). torch.linalg.solve / lu_solve_contiguous_f64 (ft-kernel-cpu ~8053) factor + solve entirely in f64. SWING: factor LU in f32 (matrixmultiply sgemm ~2x faster + half the memory bandwidth), then refine in f64: r = b - A*x (f64 matvec), solve A*dx = r reusing the f32 LU factors, x += dx; repeat 1-3 iters (converges for well-conditioned A) -> f64-accurate result at ~f32 factorization speed. Target ~1.8-2x on large solves.

RECON (done): lu_solve has VALUE/reconstruction tests (lu_solve_simple_system/3x3_system/multiple_rhs @~19293), NOT a bit-exact sha256 golden -> if those asserts are approximate (within tol), refinement ships CLEAN; if exact assert_eq! on f64, last-ULP diff needs a tolerance relax. lu_factor_parallel_golden (@19076, n=128) pins the f64 LU output but is UNTOUCHED (keep f64 lu_factor; add a separate f32-factor+refine solve path). NO isolated solve bench exists -> must ADD one (large well-conditioned SPD/general system, e.g. n>=512/1024) to prove Score>=2.0; the existing inv(256/512) bench may be too small for f32 to clear 2x against refinement overhead.

PLAN (dedicated turn, WORKTREE off origin/main to sidestep the rd1s eigh peer churn in the same file): (1) add solve bench; (2) sgemm-based f32 LU factor + f32 triangular solves; (3) f64 residual + correction loop with a residual-norm stop; (4) isomorphism: solution matches direct f64 lu_solve within tol + A*x=b residual < f64 eps*cond; (5) fallback to direct f64 for ill-conditioned (refinement stalls) or tiny n. ONE lever, Score>=2.0 keep gate. Found while pivoting off the weak-scalar parity vein per no-ceiling.

title: [perf] tensor_values / no-grad ops degrade as the session's tape grows (repeated forwards slow ~linearly)
description: PROFILE-BACKED (instrumented conv1d/nograd_L/1024 on vmi1167313, 2 runs). Calling tensor_values(padded) on the SAME session degrades from 3.1ms (call#5) to 8.3ms (call#60) as the session accumulates nodes — make_output_var stays ~5us. The ops_bench runs 110 convs on ONE long-lived session (b.iter reuses s), so the session retains every iteration's intermediate tensors (padded input ~4.2MB + conv output ~4.2MB per call), and tensor_values gets progressively slower (allocator/cache pressure or an O(nodes) access path). NET: this dominates the conv1d 'gap' — at call#60 the ft-api WRAPPER is ~30ms (tensor_values 8.3ms + tensor_pad + reshapes, all tape-growth-sensitive) vs the conv2d_forward_f64 KERNEL only ~11.8ms (im2col 5.8 + dgemm 4.3 + transpose 1.7). The conv kernel is FINE; the gap is autograd-engine node retention. SYSTEMIC: affects ALL repeated no-grad forwards (inference loops), not conv. FIX CANDIDATES (one lever each, same-worker A/B): (1) no-grad ops should NOT register intermediate tensors on the session's retained node store (free after read / arena-per-forward / a with_no_grad scope that drops nodes); (2) make tensor_values an O(1) storage lookup if it isn't; (3) conv no-grad path: pad into a local Vec instead of a tensor_pad SESSION node (halves retained storage/iter). Profile which dominates first (allocator vs node-access).
notes: with_no_grad_region shipped (6d9eb3f4, t5mte) — bounds tape for single-output no-grad composites. REMAINING deep frontier for the general engine fix: (1) multi-output regions (return Vec<TensorNodeId>: clone+truncate+re-leaf each, remap ids) for models with multiple outputs; (2) the hard case — grad-path retention + arbitrary live handles needs RAII/refcounted-or-generational tensor handles (TensorNodeId with Drop), a core autograd rewrite touching every op, parity-absolute. That rewrite is the true gmuml close.
close_reason: Completed this optimization slice: conv1d no-grad tape compaction shipped in 548b5a2b using compact_nograd_tensor_since. Clean proof passed on vmi1152480; cargo check -p ft-api --all-targets passed on vmi1227854; clean HEAD Criterion confirmation on vmi1152480 was [31.411 ms 32.219 ms 33.034 ms]. Remaining generalized no-grad boundary work is tracked in frankentorch-t5mte.

title: tensor_renorm severs autograd; composable via permute/reshape/norm/clamp/div/mul
description:
tensor_renorm at lib.rs:15185 normalizes sub-tensors along a dim so their p-norm is bounded by maxnorm. The body extracts values, computes the per-slice norm in plain f64, conditionally scales, and rebuilds a non-grad leaf — silently severing autograd. PyTorch's torch.renorm IS differentiable (sub-gradient at the boundary norm == maxnorm).

Closed form for the scale factor:
   scale_d = maxnorm / max(norm_d, maxnorm)
which equals 1 when norm_d <= maxnorm and maxnorm/norm_d when norm_d > maxnorm.

Composable via:
   permute dim to position 0
   reshape to [dim_size, other_size]
   abs + pow + sum_dim(1) + pow(1/p)  → norm per slice
   denom = clamp_min(norm, maxnorm)
   scale = maxnorm / denom
   broadcast scale via unsqueeze + expand
   mul, reshape back, permute back

Used heavily in embedding regularization (torch.nn.Embedding(max_norm=...)). Surfaced during /reality-check pass.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Composed tensor_renorm through permute/reshape/abs/pow/sum_dim/clamp_min/div/mul/expand. The closed-form scale per slice is maxnorm / max(norm, maxnorm), which equals 1 when norm <= maxnorm and maxnorm/norm when norm > maxnorm — clamp_min(norm, maxnorm) gives exactly the denom we want. Strategy: permute dim to position 0, reshape to [dim_size, other_size], compute per-row L_p norm, scale each row, reshape + permute back. dim==0 case skips the (no-op) permutes. Added renorm_propagates_gradient_through_input regression test (mixed clamp / no-clamp rows; row 1 in identity branch gives grad=1, row 0 in scaled branch gives finite non-zero). ft-api lib tests: 1166.

title: LOBPCG: top-k symmetric eigenpairs (torch.lobpcg) — O(n^2 k) vs full eigh O(n^3)
description:
torch.lobpcg has no FrankenTorch equivalent — the only way to get the top/bottom-k eigenpairs of a large symmetric matrix is the full eigh (O(n^3)). LOBPCG (Locally Optimal Block Preconditioned Conjugate Gradient) computes them iteratively in O(n^2 k) per iteration x few iterations — the eigh analog of the randomized SVD just shipped (svd_lowrank a3fb0f54 / pca_lowrank b87c54e0), and a real torch parity gap + big algorithmic win for spectral methods (graph Laplacians, spectral clustering, covariance PCA).

ALGORITHM (reuses the SAME building blocks as randomized SVD: gemm::dgemm + qr_contiguous_f64 + the now-CORRECT eigh_contiguous_f64 for the small Rayleigh-Ritz): (1) X = random n x k, orthonormalize (QR). (2) iterate: R = A X - X (X^T A X) [residuals]; build search subspace S = [X | R | P] (P = prev conjugate direction, empty on iter 0); orthonormalize S (QR); Rayleigh-Ritz = small eigh of (S^T A S) [3k x 3k], take the k extreme eigenpairs; X_new = S Coeff; update P; check residual norms < tol. (3) eigenvalues = the k Ritz values, eigenvectors = X.

SUBTLETIES (why it needs a dedicated turn, not a rushed commit): orthogonalization stability (re-orthonormalize against converged/locked vectors), soft-locking of converged pairs, handling the empty-P first iteration, ill-conditioned S^T A S. VALIDATION GATE: lobpcg top-k eigenvalues match full eigh's top-k to tol; A v = lambda v residual < tol; on a moderate SPD matrix (n=64, k=4). Optionally support largest=true/false. ft-kernel-cpu + ft-api wiring. Use /alien-artifact-coding for the convergence/locking proof obligations.
close_reason: Shipped commit 12f5f72f: tensor_lobpcg / functional_lobpcg wired into ft-api (kernel was 31f0e5d9). torch.lobpcg parity gap closed — top-k symmetric eigenpairs O(n^2 k)/iter. Test lobpcg_api_matches_eigh_extreme_pairs passes (n=32 k=3, largest true/false, evals match eigh to 1e-6, A*v=lambda*v < 1e-5). Pushed main+master.

title: OneCycleLR phase boundary off-by-one vs torch (warmup peak lands a step late)
description: FT OneCycleLR computes phase1 = floor(pct_start*total_steps) (integer), but torch uses end_step = pct_start*total_steps - 1 (float), spanning steps [0, total_steps-1]. Result: FT's warmup peak lands one step late and the whole lr/momentum trajectory is shifted. Confirmed vs torch 2.12: max_lr=1.0,total=10,pct_start=0.3 -> torch peaks at step 2 (lr=1.0) then descends (0.857 at step3); FT peaks at step 3. Two existing tests encode the buggy values (one_cycle_lr_linear_phase_boundaries asserts peak@step3; one_cycle_lr_edge_cases asserts pct_start=0 -> 1.0 but torch=4e-6). Fix: replace integer phase_steps with torch's float end_step schedule (start/end lr+momentum per phase, progress=(step-start)/(end-start)).
close_reason: done

title: ft-api: amax/amin backward routes full grad to first argmax instead of splitting across ties
description: torch.amax/amin split the upstream gradient EQUALLY among all elements tied at the extremum (verified torch 2.12: amax([3,1,3,2]) grad [0.5,0,0.5,0]); torch.max(dim)/min(dim) (which return indices) route the whole grad to the single first argmax. FrankenTorch's tensor_amax/amin wrapped tensor_max_dim/min_dim, inheriting the route-to-first backward -> wrong for amax/amin at exact ties. FIXED: tensor_amax_amin_split computes the reduction via apply_function with a split-equally backward (grad_out/tie_count to each tied element), upcasting non-f64 inputs to f64 for apply_function then casting the result back to preserve dtype (b3fg). NaN propagates in forward. Golden test session_backward_kink_grads_match_torch (also covers maximum/minimum/clamp/relu/hardtanh/softshrink/hardshrink/threshold/median — all already correct).
close_reason: SHIPPED 6083dfd0. tensor_amax_amin_split: apply_function with split-equally backward (grad/tie_count), upcast non-f64 for the f64-only apply path + cast back to preserve dtype, NaN propagates. Verified vs torch 2.12 (session_backward_kink_grads_match_torch). ft-api 1922 + ft-autograd 436 pass.

title: porting: add tensor_maximum / tensor_minimum with broadcasting
description: torch.maximum(a, b) and torch.minimum(a, b) compute element-wise max/min with NumPy-style broadcasting. The existing tensor_max / tensor_min only accept matching shapes. Add the broadcasting variants by composing through tensor_broadcast_to (added under h2zt) followed by tensor_max / tensor_min on the broadcasted operands. Autograd-aware via the underlying max/min's argmax-routed backward (then sum-reduction over broadcast dims via the broadcast_to backward).
close_reason: Added tensor_maximum / tensor_minimum with NumPy-style broadcasting in ft-api. Composes through a private broadcast_shape helper (right-aligned per-dim max with size-1 broadcast rule) + tensor_broadcast_to + tensor_max / tensor_min. Autograd-aware: max/min routes upstream gradient to the per-element winner; broadcast_to backward sums gradient over broadcasted dims. 5 unit tests cover same-shape, scalar-vs-vector broadcast, prepend-dim broadcast (rank promotion), incompatible-shape rejection, and gradient routing including tie-handling. ft-api now at 1241 lib tests.

title: porting: add tensor_broadcast_to + tensor_expand_as for torch parity
description: torch.broadcast_to(input, shape) explicitly broadcasts input to a target shape if compatible. Differs from tensor_expand in that it accepts a target shape with HIGHER rank than input — it first prepends size-1 dims on the left to match rank, then expands. tensor_expand_as is a one-line wrapper that takes a reference tensor instead of an explicit shape. Both are autograd-aware via reshape + expand composition.
close_reason: Added tensor_broadcast_to and tensor_expand_as in ft-api. broadcast_to: prepends size-1 dims on the left to match target rank then expands; validates per-dim broadcast compatibility (input dim must be 1 or equal to target dim). expand_as: thin wrapper using target tensor's shape. Both compose through autograd-aware tensor_reshape + tensor_expand. 6 unit tests cover prepend+expand, size-1 broadcast, no-op same-shape passthrough, incompatible-shape rejection, gradient-summed-across-broadcasts (key autograd correctness check), and expand_as alias. ft-api now at 1234 lib tests.

comments.author: RusticKite
comments.text: PROVEN but UNLANDABLE this session (peer agent continuously editing ft-kernel-cpu -> shared working tree clobbered between every read+edit; cumsum was already taken by the peer as 686ab41b). Implementation = EXACT mirror of the peer's cumsum parallelization, applied to cumprod_tensor_contiguous_f64 (~7388) and _f32 (~13616): replace the serial 'for outer/for inner/for d acc*=...' loop with a gate 'let lane=dim_size*inner_size; if numel>=PARALLEL_THRESHOLD && outer_size>=2 { output.par_chunks_mut(lane).enumerate().for_each(|(outer,out_chunk)| { let base=outer*lane; for inner.. { let mut acc=1.0; for d.. { let idx=d*inner_size+inner; acc*=data[base+idx]; out_chunk[idx]=acc; } } }) } else { <serial> }'. PROVEN same-worker RAYON_NUM_THREADS A/B on cumprod[8192,1024] f64 dim=1: serial 26.32ms -> parallel 4.10ms = 6.4x, bit-exact (test cumprod_parallel_matches_serial_bit_exact: f64+f32, dim=last + middle dim, to_bits). === SEPARATE HIGH-VALUE FINDING (file own bead): the PUBLIC f32 tensor_cumprod path is ~20x SLOWER than f64 (f32 87ms vs f64 4.1ms for the SAME [8192,1024] parallel cumprod) — the f32 scan API path (dispatch_tensor_scan_dim_contiguous_f32 / tensor_variable_f32 handling) has massive overhead unrelated to the kernel. Worth profiling. ===

comments.author: RusticKite
comments.text: DISPUTE + measurement caveat. Peer REJECTED this as 66pe (fa8b5ed1): rigorous same-worker A/B ts1 serial 42.2ms vs parallel 77.3ms + vmi 93.6ms = 1.83x REGRESSION. MY 6.4x (serial 26ms / parallel 4.1ms) is UNRELIABLE: I used TWO SEPARATE rch exec commands (parallel run, then serial run) -> DIFFERENT workers -> worker-variance confound, NOT a valid same-worker A/B (violates the project's RAYON_NUM_THREADS-in-one-exec rule). So I DEFER to the peer's rejection and am NOT shipping cumprod. HOWEVER, the rejection is suspicious: (1) peer's parallel runs 77ms then 93ms (~20% apart, slower than serial) = the classic CONTENTION signature per the f32-fused-linear near-miss lesson; (2) the peer ACCEPTED cumsum as a parallel win (686ab41b) but REJECTED cumprod for a MEMORY-IDENTICAL kernel (read input, write output, 1 op/elem) — those two conclusions contradict. RESOLUTION NEEDED (not by me this session — ft-kernel-cpu is peer-contended): a single-process ANCHORED same-worker A/B running cumsum (known parallel-win anchor) AND cumprod parallel in ONE bench binary on ONE idle worker; normalise cumprod by the cumsum ratio. If cumprod tracks cumsum, the 66pe rejection was a contention confound and cumprod IS a ~5x win.

title: Tape elementwise binary ops now NumPy-broadcast (root fix for muan/3pvd bug class)
description: The tensor autograd engine's elementwise binary ops (add/sub/mul/div/min/max/atan2/fmod/remainder) required EXACTLY matching shapes and errored with ShapeMismatch otherwise — a fundamental torch-parity gap that caused a whole class of latent bugs (tensor_normalize muan, 5 norm ops 3pvd, info_nce) where callers forgot to expand manually. Fixed at the root in TensorTape::binary (ft-autograd): when operand shapes differ but are NumPy-broadcast-compatible, materialise each operand to the common shape via autograd-aware reshape (prepend leading axes) + expand BEFORE the kernel. The inserted expand nodes' existing backward (cg_sum_to_shape) reduces gradients over the broadcast axes, so the op's own backward stays matching-shape and UNCHANGED. Matching-shape and non-elementwise (matmul/dot/outer/bmm) paths are bit-identical -> all 1858 ft-api + 433 ft-autograd + 708 ft-nn + 207 ft-optim tests pass unchanged. New test elementwise_binary_ops_broadcast_forward_and_grad verifies forward values ([2,3]op[3], op[2,1], op[1]) + gradient reduction over broadcast axes vs finite-diff. Callers' manual expands are now redundant (still correct) — optional future cleanup.
close_reason: Shipped commit ef7d3dfd: TensorTape::binary now NumPy-broadcasts elementwise ops via autograd-aware reshape+expand insertion; expand backward (cg_sum_to_shape) reduces gradients over broadcast axes so the op backward is unchanged. Root fix for the muan/3pvd missing-expand bug class. All 1858 ft-api + 433 ft-autograd + 708 ft-nn + 207 ft-optim tests pass unchanged; new forward+grad broadcast test (FD-verified). Pushed main+master.

title: tensor_unfold severs autograd; gather-then-scatter backward needed
description:
tensor_unfold at lib.rs:15083 extracts sliding windows. Each output element is a copy of an input element via a deterministic mapping (flat_out → in_flat). PyTorch's torch.Tensor.unfold IS differentiable.

Backward: scatter — grad_input[in_flat] += grad_output[flat_out] for each (flat_out, in_flat) pair the forward used. The mapping is deterministic given (dimension, size, step) and the input shape, so we can recompute it inside the backward closure.

Wrap in tensor_apply_function with this gather-then-scatter pattern.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Wrapped tensor_unfold in tensor_apply_function with gather-then-scatter backward. The (flat_out -> in_flat) mapping is computed once in the outer body and captured by both forward (gather) and backward (scatter) closures so the same routing is used in both directions; overlapping windows (step < size) accumulate via += in the scatter. Added unfold_propagates_gradient_via_scatter regression test (x=[1,2,3,4], size=3, step=1 → 2 overlapping windows, grad_x = [1,2,2,1] under sum loss). ft-api lib tests: 1164.

title: ft-api: hermitian_extend misreads complex input (split vs interleaved) — breaks irfftn 1-D & N-D
description: Same root cause as the fft_along_dim interleaved bug (kw2l). tensor_hermitian_extend read complex input via view_as_real then split it as vals[..half]=re / vals[half..]=im, but view_as_real is INTERLEAVED ([re0,im0,...]). This scrambled the reconstructed full spectrum, breaking EVERY irfftn (which calls hermitian_extend) — even 1-D: irfftn(rfftn([1,2,3,4]))[0] gave 2 instead of 1; 2-D gave 2.577 vs 0. Fix: interleaved read. irfftn now round-trips rfftn bit/tolerance in 1-D and N-D; full ft-api 1964/0. Found by cross-checking rfftn/irfftn vs torch while adding their norm modes.
close_reason: Fixed: interleaved read in hermitian_extend. irfftn round-trips in 1-D and N-D. ft-api 1964/0.

title: ft-data WeightedRandomSampler accepts invalid weight distributions
description: Fresh parity audit: ft-data WeightedRandomSampler currently sanitizes negative/NaN/Inf weights to zero and treats all-zero weights as uniform. PyTorch WeightedRandomSampler delegates to torch.multinomial semantics; official torch.multinomial docs require weights to be non-negative, finite, and have a non-zero sum. Acceptance: make WeightedRandomSampler fail closed for empty weights with positive samples, negative weights, non-finite weights, and zero-sum weights; preserve valid positive weighted sampling; add regression tests; validate with rch exec -- cargo test -p ft-data weighted_random_sampler and rch exec -- cargo check -p ft-data --all-targets.
close_reason: Implemented fail-closed WeightedRandomSampler validation in ft-data: empty weights with positive samples, zero num_samples, non-finite weights, negative weights, and zero-sum weights now return transform configuration errors instead of silently sanitizing/falling back to uniform sampling. Valid positive weighted sampling remains covered. title: ft-api: hfft/ihfft lack norm= modes — final fft norm family ops + correctness cross-check
description: Last fft ops without norm=. torch.fft.hfft/ihfft accept norm=backward/forward/ortho. hfft is FORWARD (Hermitian complex -> real len n) so rescale uses N=n (out_len); ihfft is INVERSE (real len n -> Hermitian n/2+1) so N=n (input len). Added tensor_hfft_norm/tensor_ihfft_norm reusing fft_norm_factor; defaults delegate to backward. Also added hfft_ihfft_match_torch_golden cross-check (the technique that found 3 FFT bugs) — hfft/ihfft + round-trip + norm all verified correct vs torch 2.12, NO bug found (module clean). hfft(c,n=4)=[8,0,0,-4] fwd/4 ortho/2; ihfft fwd x4 ortho x2. fft norm family + FFT module verification now fully COMPLETE. ft-api 1966/0.
close_reason: shipped hfft/ihfft norm + cross-check (no bug)

title: conv2d dilation= (functional + nn Conv2d) — dilated/à-trous convolution parity
description: functional_conv2d had no dilation; nn Conv2d had no dilation field. torch F.conv2d/nn.Conv2d support dilation (DeepLab/dilated-ResNet segmentation, WaveNet). Implemented by composition (ft-api only, NO conv-kernel change): zero-stuff the kernel taps (insert dilation-1 zeros between taps along H,W via autograd-aware reshape/pad/narrow -> [out,in,dH*(kh-1)+1,dW*(kw-1)+1]) then plain conv; bit-exact since zeros contribute 0 (verified torch.allclose). pad targets inner dims so no permute. Added dilate_conv_weight helper + functional_conv2d_dilated(input,w,b,stride,pad,dilation,groups) threading groups (so dilation+groups compose) + tensor alias; nn Conv2d gains .dilation((dh,dw)) builder + forward routing. Verified vs torch 2.12 (dilation=2 golden, asymmetric (2,1)+padding, dilation=1 identical, grad to all taps, module==functional). Follow-up: conv1d/3d dilation+groups.
close_reason: done

title: ft-data weighted sampler skips zero-weight prefix at boundary sample

Match multinomial interval semantics by selecting the first cumulative threshold strictly greater than the sample and add regression coverage for fast paths and fallback.

title: Complex elementwise autograd: differentiable mul/add/div/exp on complex tensors (Wirtinger) — unblocks complex-valued networks

Gated by complex_grad_pair (both complex && grad-enabled && a requires grad) — no-grad complex keeps the fast typed-dispatch forward; only the grad path re-routes (the engine's real-elementwise Mul/Div backward reads f64 and is silently wrong for complex). REMAINING: complex UNARY (exp/log/sqrt/abs²) differentiable — exp(z)=e^re·(cos im + i sin im), Wirtinger grad = conj(exp(z)) etc.; these compose through real exp/sin/cos + complex bridge. Gated by complex_grad_pair / requires_grad — no-grad complex keeps the fast typed-dispatch forward; only the grad path re-routes (the engine's real-elementwise backward reads f64 and is silently wrong for complex). title: tensor_matrix_norm has severed-autograd + lacks numpy subprocess conformance
description:
tensor_matrix_norm exists in ft-api/src/lib.rs:11602 supporting fro / 1 / -1 / inf / -inf orderings. It extracts tensor values and rebuilds a requires_grad=false leaf, silently severing the autograd tape (same severed-autograd pattern as the cov / corrcoef / mode / quantile family that was fixed under frankentorch-1tax). PyTorch's torch.linalg.matrix_norm IS differentiable — for 'fro' it's just sqrt of sum of squares (composable through autograd primitives), and for the operator norms it's a max/min over column/row L1 sums (also composable through tensor_abs + tensor_sum_dim + tensor_max/min).

Also missed during the c36b closure: my earlier closure note claimed matrix_norm 'has no separate API in ft-api' — that was wrong, it does. It just wasn't covered by a subprocess conformance harness.

Two fixes needed:
  1. Compose tensor_matrix_norm through autograd primitives (or wrap in tensor_apply_function) so gradients flow.
  2. Add torch_matrix_norm_numpy_subprocess_conformance harness covering all five orderings.

Surfaced during a /reality-check pass scanning for severed-autograd patterns.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Composed tensor_matrix_norm through autograd primitives: 'fro' = sqrt(sum(A*A)) via tensor_mul + tensor_sum + tensor_sqrt; '1'/'-1'/'inf'/'-inf' = max/min over (sum_dim of tensor_abs) via tensor_abs + tensor_sum_dim + tensor_max_dim/min_dim. Output shape preserved as [1] via tensor_unsqueeze when reductions yield 0-D scalars. Added matrix_norm_frobenius_propagates_gradient and matrix_norm_inf_propagates_gradient_to_max_row regression tests in ft-api (1139 passing). Added torch_matrix_norm_numpy_subprocess_conformance covering all five orderings (fro / 1 / -1 / inf / -inf) across 12 matrices. Subprocess conformance count now 32 (was 31).

title: [perf][no-gaps] ft-api KNN staged coordinate-panel distance layout
description: Follow-up from frankentorch-jod6 rejection. Profile-backed target remains knn_search/8192x512_k8. Rejected families: scalar partial-distance threshold pruning (swbh), query-panel width 16->32 (bbgu), and point-major all-query top-k workspace (jod6, ts1 8.1467ms -> 9.6224ms, Score 0.80). Next materially different primitive: staged coordinate-panel / SoA distance layout that improves coordinate load/vectorization locality without increasing the hot top-k workspace footprint or changing per-query point order. Preserve strict-less tie behavior, exact squared-distance expression, sqrt output bits, RNG absence, and KNN full-sort golden digest. Acceptance: fresh rch baseline first, one lever only, focused knn_search full-sort proof plus golden sha256, same-worker rch rebench, keep only Score >= 2.0.
notes: ASSESSMENT (cc, session 6): the materially-different bit-exact lever left is SIMD-across-QUERIES (wide::f64x4, lanes=4 independent queries, each computing the SAME dx²+dy²+dz² so per-query float order is preserved). Two blockers make Score>=2.0 unlikely: (1) AMDAHL — the hot loop is 8192q x 512pts; per pair = 6 cheap FP ops (distance) + the branch-heavy consider_knn_candidate top-k insertion. The full-sort golden + per-query-order constraint FREEZE the top-k insertion (can't use quickselect/heap-only), and it dominates once the buffer fills; SIMD only speeds the small distance fraction. (2) FMA-FUSION RISK — wide f64x4 uses non-FMA mul+add; if the scalar golden path auto-fuses dx*dx+dy*dy+dz*dz to FMA under some target-feature build (or vice-versa), the SIMD path mismatches the frozen sha256 golden -> determinism break (cf the MXCSR/FTZ saga). wide is also not an ft-api dep (would need Cargo add or moving the loop to ft-kernel-cpu). The >2x primitives (kd-tree/ball-tree spatial partition, quickselect top-k) are forbidden by full-sort + per-query-order + exact-expression. Deferring as low-EV; 4th rejection not worth the determinism risk. If revisited: prove FMA-neutrality first (build the scalar baseline digest under the bench's exact target-features, then confirm wide matches) BEFORE benching.
close_reason: Kept panel-only KNN staged coordinate layout: fmd clean baseline 6.4421ms median -> 4.3452ms median, 1.483x; Score 2.05. Focused KNN proof 3/3, golden sha256, and cargo check -p ft-api --all-targets passed; fmt/clippy remain blocked by broad existing ft-api drift; UBS timed out after 300s.
