title: Fix ft-nn FocalLoss autograd via log_softmax + gather composition [child of cq0b]
description:
FocalLoss in ft-nn is the multi-class softmax variant: loss = -alpha * (1 - p_t)^gamma * log(p_t) where p_t = softmax(logits)[target_idx]. Body extracts logits + targets, computes manually, rebuilds non-grad leaf.

Composable through autograd-aware primitives:
   log_probs = log_softmax(logits, last_dim)
   target_idx = tensor_variable(targets, [..., 1], false)   non-grad
   gathered = tensor_gather(log_probs, last_dim, target_idx)
   log_pt = squeeze(gathered, last_dim)
   pt = exp(log_pt)
   focal_factor = (1 - pt)^gamma
   per_sample = -alpha * focal_factor * log_pt
   reduce per Reduction
notes: Fixed in this commit. Composed ft-nn FocalLoss (multi-class softmax variant) through log_softmax + tensor_gather + autograd primitives. The target-indexed gather replaces the manual offset arithmetic; the rest of the focal weighting (1-pt)^gamma * log_pt composes through tensor_exp + tensor_pow + tensor_mul. ft-nn lib tests: 517.

title: ft-api: batched autograd torch.linalg.solve_triangular over leading dims
description: solve_triangular was 2-D only and (unlike the non-grad QR/ldl primitives) is DIFFERENTIABLE. Added batching that preserves autograd: for (*,n,n) A and (*,n,k)/(*,k,n) B, flatten leading dims to (g,...), select each 2-D slice (autograd-aware narrow+squeeze), recurse into the 2-D composition (tensor_triangular_solve + transpose/mask), then tensor_stack + tensor_reshape back. select/stack/reshape are tape ops so the per-batch block-diagonal gradient flows exactly as torch's. 2-D path unchanged. Verified vs torch 2.x batched goldens (forward + grad_A masked to triangle + grad_B).
close_reason: Shipped 5b8279ed. solve_triangular now batches over leading dims with AUTOGRAD preserved: flatten -> select 2-D slice -> recurse 2-D composition -> tensor_stack -> reshape (all tape ops). Verified vs torch 2.x batched goldens: forward <1e-12 + backward (grad_A masked to triangle, grad_B) matching. 2-D path unchanged. ft-api suite 2043 pass.

title: Add 3 more metamorphic property tests: neg-involution, sort-idempotence, sum-of-sum_dim
description:
Extend ft-conformance metamorphic suite with three additional invariants:

1. fuzz_metamorphic_neg_neg_is_identity — tensor_neg(tensor_neg(x)) bit-exactly equals x for any x. Negation is sign-bit flip, two flips return original.

2. fuzz_metamorphic_sort_is_idempotent — sorting an already-sorted tensor yields the same values. Compose tensor_sort with itself in the same direction.

3. fuzz_metamorphic_sum_of_sum_dim_equals_sum — tensor_sum_dim then tensor_sum on a 2-D tensor equals tensor_sum directly within a small ULP envelope. Verifies dim reductions preserve totals and don't double-count or skip elements.

title: ft-api: prelu autograd (input + per-channel weight gradients)

Shared prelu_forward_values helper keeps forward bit-identical. Verified grad_x + grad_w for scalar and per-channel weight. frankentorch-avfl-prelu.

title: [perf][no-gaps] eig/eigvals ~12-40x slower than LAPACK geev (non-symmetric eigensolver)
description: PROFILE-BACKED (linalg_bench vmi1156319): eig_f64_256x256 ~213ms, eigvals_f64_256x256 ~102ms vs symmetric eigvalsh ~11.5ms (12x) and LAPACK dhseqr/dgeev ~2-10ms (20-40x). ft uses Francis double-shift hqr2 (correct algorithm, EISPACK lineage) with SCALAR non-vectorized inner loops. DEEP LEVERS (multi-session, parity-delicate): (1) parallelize the Hessenberg reduction reflection application (right-multiply + q_acc are row-contiguous -> par_chunks_mut, BIT-EXACT; left-multiply is column-strided, needs restructure); (2) GEMM-based blocked Hessenberg (dsytrd-analog for non-symmetric) + WY reflectors; (3) GEMM-based eigenvector back-transform (q_acc @ Z); (4) restrict QR-sweep work to active window. Items 2-3 reassociate FP -> need strict-mode EISPACK fallback + golden SHA before/after + same-worker Score>=2.0, like rd1s. FIRST INCREMENT shipped: eigvals row-update uses hqr range [k,en] not hqr2 [k,n) (bit-exact, 1.16x).
notes:
DEEP-LEVER PLAN (multishift QR + AED) — the real fix for the dense-eig ~12-40x gap, multi-session, parity-delicate. Grounded in eig_impl (crates/ft-kernel-cpu/src/lib.rs): Step1 Hessenberg reduction ~L10489 (level-2, already rayon-parallel: left_scale/row/col/q_acc); Step2 Francis DOUBLE-shift implicit QR ~L10598 (single 3x3 bulge chased per iteration — THE serial O(n^3) bottleneck; cannot parallelize one bulge).

WHY LAPACK is ~12-40x faster: dlaqr0/dlaqr5 use (a) MULTISHIFT — pack ~ns (dozens) shifts into a CHAIN of bulges chased together through a window, applying the accumulated reflections to the trailing matrix + Z via level-3 GEMM (not BLAS-1), and (b) AGGRESSIVE EARLY DEFLATION (AED) — periodically Schur-decompose a trailing k×k window (k~ns+a few), deflate converged eigenvalues (often many more than classic deflation finds), cutting total iterations sharply.

BOUNDED DECOMPOSITION (ship one per session, each test-gated by the eig reconstruction + eigvals_matches_eig tests):
 1. AED as an ADD-ON to the existing double-shift loop (lowest risk; existing chase stays as fallback): every few iterations, recursively eig_impl a trailing window, count negligible spike entries, deflate. Reduces iterations -> speed, parity-safe (deflated values are exact-converged). ~100 lines.
 2. Multishift bulge-chain + GEMM trailing update (replaces the single-bulge chase): the big one. Tolerance-parity (GEMM reorders) -> migrate the two bit-exact contracts to tolerance: eigvals_matches_eig (L21728, to_bits) and eig_parallel_schur_vector_update_matches_single_thread_bit_exact (L21740, to_bits). Keep reconstruction (A V=V Λ, 1e-6) + eigvals_companion_complex_roots as the correctness oracle.
 3. (Optional, separate) BALANCING (dgebal) — torch geev balances, we DON'T (accuracy/parity gap on poorly-scaled matrices, not a dense-perf win). Cheap for eigvals (similarity-invariant, no transform); eig-with-vectors needs eigenvector un-permute+un-scale or the reconstruction test breaks.

NOTE: alien_cs_graveyard has NO eig/QR technique (it is exotic-CS: sketching/succinct/wait-free) — this is the standard dlaqr rewrite, no shortcut. Target ~3-10x toward LAPACK geev parity. Start with piece 1 (AED) next session.
close_reason: Kept eigvals-only Hessenberg live-column left-apply: paired RCH vmi1227854 eigvals_f64_256x256 57.542 ms -> 55.169 ms (1.043x, 4.12% lower); golden eig/eigvals digests unchanged for n=64/128/256; focused eig tests, check, clippy passed.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: NEGATIVE RESULT + groundwork (f87b6389, IronFract): column-modification active-window trim — start the Francis bulge-chase COLUMN loop at l instead of 0 for want_vectors=false (column counterpart of the shipped hqr ROW-range trick 080edf02, matching canonical EISPACK/NR hqr 'do i=l') — is BIT-EXACT (eigvals_golden digests n=64/128/256 unchanged: 0xbc0583d464b1a211 / 0xcf8084e9cc30d867 / 0x188d322a66b49c0f; all 13 eig tests pass) but a NO-OP on the random-dense profiling workload. Reason: double-shift QR deflation on a non-diagonally-dominant dense matrix is bottom-only (en decreases; no mid-matrix splits), so l stays ~0 and the [0,l) skip in the column update saves nothing (same-worker back-to-back A/B at n=256: no speedup). The row-range trick worked only because en<n-1 is GUARANTEED after the first deflation; column lower bound l>0 is NOT guaranteed for dense spectra. DO NOT re-attempt this trim. Committed eigvals_golden.rs (mirrors eigh_golden.rs) as the before/after bit-exact golden the multishift-QR+AED rewrite will diff against during its tolerance parity-contract migration. Real eig lever remains piece 2 (multishift bulge-chain + GEMM trailing update) + piece 1 (AED); both reassociate FP and REQUIRE migrating eigvals_matches_eig + eig_parallel_schur to tolerance (multi-session, parity-policy decision).

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: PROFILING (IvoryDeer, same-worker A/B via examples/eig_timing_probe): geev cost breakdown at n=256/512/1024 (64-thread worker): eig = ~75% eigvals (the SERIAL single-bulge Francis QR, O(n^3): 2592ms@1024) + ~25% eigenvector machinery. The eigenvector machinery is ALREADY parallel (q_acc whole-stream replay 9y5bi + Phase-2 Z·U gemm). DEAD ENDS RULED OUT (rigorous same-worker A/B): (1) parallelizing the hqr2 eigenvector BACK-SUBSTITUTION over eigenvalue-groups REGRESSES — backsub is only ~100ms@1024 (~3% of eig); per-group Vec alloc + rayon dispatch made it 10x SLOWER at n=256 (4ms->43ms), 1.07x at n=1024. NOT the bottleneck. (2) restricting the eigvals column update from 0..=jmax to l..=jmax is BIT-EXACT (golden unchanged) but NULL on this spectrum: l stays ~0 (bottom-up one-at-a-time deflation, matrix does NOT block-split), so no rows are skipped. CONCLUSION: the ENTIRE remaining geev gap is the serial Francis QR (eigvals). The ONLY lever is multishift-QR + AED (qglh3 -> npxbw). No incremental/Amdahl-friendly win exists; eig is Francis-bound.

title: conv1d/conv3d/grouped/dilated/transpose + f32 double-backward (extend j4uio recipe)
description:
conv2d double-backward is SHIPPED (j4uio, commit 1d32604a) — the dominant
WGAN-GP/gradient-penalty case. The OTHER conv variants still ERR on 2nd-order
("create_graph for this custom function requires a create_graph backward")
because they use the SAME fused borrowed-inputs grad fast path with no cg
backward:
- functional_conv1d (ft-api ~18423/18616 f64, ~18685 f32)
- functional_conv2d_grouped / depthwise (~19220 f64, ~19281 f32)
- functional_conv2d_dilated, functional_conv3d (~20045 f32, ~20570 f64)
- conv transpose paths
- the f32 fast paths (tensor_apply_function_f32_output_borrowed_inputs)

PROVEN RECIPE (apply per variant, mechanical):
1. Use the new ft-autograd primitive
   `apply_function_with_create_graph_borrowed_inputs` (f64) — keeps the
   zero-copy 1st-order backward, adds a cg slot. For the f32 paths an
   analogous `..._f32_output_borrowed_inputs`-with-cg primitive is needed.
2. Conv is BILINEAR, so the cg backward closes under the op's OWN
   forward/backward kernels (conv1d_forward/backward_f64,
   conv3d_forward/backward_f64, depthwise_conv2d_forward/backward_f64):
   dpadded=InputGrad(dout,weight) [fwd=backward(dout,zeros_pad,weight).dpadded;
   bwd: grad_dout=forward(g,weight), grad_weight=backward(dout,g,zeros_w).dweight],
   dweight=WeightGrad(dout,padded) [symmetric], dbias=sum(dout). Build each as a
   nested tape.apply_function node; gate by ctx.needs_input_grad().
3. Verify each vs torch (extend conv2d_hessian_probe / conv2d_gradpenalty_probe
   to the variant's shape). See crates/ft-api/examples/conv2d_*_probe.rs.

Lower priority than conv2d (P3): conv1d=audio/seq, conv3d=video/volumetric
gradient-penalty are far rarer than conv2d image GANs.
notes:
UPDATE (cc, 2026-06-16): gir5b CLOSED (e4313739) — full f32 WGAN-GP gradient-penalty
(weight+bias double-backward) now works for conv2d-f32 + grouped/depthwise-f32,
verified vs torch f32. Fix = cast F32 weight/padded to F64 (tape.to_f64) in the f32
cg closures so grad nodes live in F64 grad-space (apply_function infers dtype from
inputs; F32 inputs made the grad nodes F32 -> F64-only readers ERR'd).

f64 conv double-backward family: COMPLETE (all variants).
f32 conv double-backward: conv2d + grouped/depthwise DONE (input-Hessian + full
gradient-penalty). linear-f32 free.

REMAINING (niche, distinct bugs — each needs its own look, NOT the gir5b pattern):
1. conv3d-f32 double-backward: ERRs UnsupportedDType even for INPUT-Hessian -> its
   f32 grad path is unwired for create_graph and/or hits an f64-only op. Needs a cg
   closure (conv3d f32 kernels) + the F64-grad-space cast.
2. conv_transpose2d-f32 double-backward: silently returns ZERO Hessian (detachment
   in the f32 transpose grad path) -> needs a cg closure + F64-grad-space cast.
Both are f32 2nd-order of niche conv variants. The dominant cases (f64 all + f32
conv2d/grouped/linear) are done.
close_reason: Fixed in 2cd45b9d (cc): f32 tail closed — conv3d-f32 (was ERR UnsupportedDType) + conv_transpose2d-f32 (was silent ZERO Hessian) double-backward now match torch 2.12 f32 EXACTLY for both input-Hessian and full gradient-penalty (input+weight+bias). Added conv3d_col2im_f32/conv3d_backward_f32/conv_transpose2d_backward_f32 kernels + fused f32 cg grad paths (gir5b F64-grad-space recipe). Whole conv double-backward surface complete. ft-kernel-cpu 483 + ft-api 2131 pass.

title: porting: add tensor_exp2 + tensor_logsigmoid for torch parity
description:
Two missing math ops:
  - exp2(x) = 2^x. Compose via tensor_mul (x * ln(2)) + tensor_exp; autograd-aware.
  - logsigmoid(x) = log(sigmoid(x)). Numerically stable form: -softplus(-x), composes via tensor_neg + tensor_softplus + tensor_neg.
Both widely used in numerically-stable variants of attention scores, log-likelihoods, etc.
close_reason: Added tensor_exp2 (2^x via tensor_exp(x*ln(2))) and tensor_logsigmoid (numerically stable log(sigmoid(x)) = -softplus(-x)). Both autograd-aware via existing primitives. 4 unit tests cover exp2 known values + analytic gradient (2^x * ln(2)), logsigmoid agreement with naive log(sigmoid) in normal range, and logsigmoid stability at x=-1000 (where naive form underflows to -inf but stable form returns -1000 + small correction). ft-api now at 1298 lib tests.

title: Unblock F32 nan_to_num autograd via dtype-preserving where
close_reason: Completed: F32 tensor_nan_to_num now uses dtype-preserving where composition, preserves F32 output, and masks gradients at NaN/inf replacement positions.

title: functional_conv3d severs autograd via tensor_values + non-grad rebuild
description:
ft-api functional_conv3d (line 5716) extracts both input and weight tensor_values, runs the 3D convolution in plain f64, and rebuilds via tensor_variable(_, _, false). Silently severs autograd through both input and weight — breaks training for any model calling F.conv3d directly (3D CNNs, video models, medical imaging).

The asymmetry: functional_conv2d composes correctly via tensor_pad + tensor_narrow + tensor_reshape + tensor_cat + tensor_bmm. Mirror the same composition for 3D, with an extra D axis (narrow on dims 2, 3, 4 to extract 3D sub-patches).

Affects: crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs functional_conv3d (line 5716).

title: tensor_diagflat severs autograd; delegate to diag_embed via flatten
description:
tensor_diagflat at lib.rs:15978 places a flattened input on the diagonal of a 2-D matrix. tensor_diag_embed (autograd-aware after frankentorch-d180) does exactly this for a 1-D input. So diagflat = diag_embed(flatten(input), offset) and the autograd path is just composition through tensor_reshape + tensor_diag_embed.

Note: diag_embed takes i32 offset; diagflat takes i64. Range check the offset before passing.
notes: Fixed in this commit. tensor_diagflat now delegates to tensor_reshape + tensor_diag_embed (the latter is autograd-aware after frankentorch-d180). Same observable arithmetic but the autograd tape is preserved. Range-checks the i64 offset for the i32 diag_embed contract. ft-api lib tests: 1171.

title: MHA attn_mask + masked SDPA op-graph broken for [N*heads,S,d] 3D layout

Fix options: (a) make the masked op-graph use batched bmm over arbitrary leading dims (proper 3D batched matmul), or (b) have MHA keep 4D [N,heads,S,d] for the SDPA call when masked. Then add forward_qkv with attn_mask + key_padding_mask + need_weights to MHA. Found while adding MHA is_causal (shipped).

title: Fix focal_loss autograd via tensor primitives [child of 2med]
description:
focal_loss at lib.rs:10324 computes elementwise:
  p     = sigmoid(input)
  p_t   = p if target==1 else 1-p
  alpha_t = alpha if target==1 else 1-alpha
  loss  = -alpha_t * (1-p_t)^gamma * log(p_t.max(1e-15))

Composable through autograd primitives. The target-conditional branches can be replaced by linear blends with target as a {0, 1} mask:
  p_t       = target * p + (1 - target) * (1 - p)
  alpha_t   = target * alpha + (1 - target) * (1 - alpha)   (multiplied via a target-derived non-grad scalar tensor)
  inner     = (1 - p_t)^gamma * log(clamp_min(p_t, 1e-15))
  loss_per_elem = -alpha_t * inner
  loss      = mean(per_elem)

Used in dense object detection (RetinaNet etc.). Last child of frankentorch-2med.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Composed focal_loss through sigmoid + linear-blend-by-target + pow + log + mean. Replaced the per-element conditionals with linear blends: p_t = target * p + (1 - target) * (1 - p), alpha_t = target * alpha + (1 - target) * (1 - alpha). Added focal_loss_propagates_gradient_through_input regression test (sanity finite + non-zero check, since the focal closed-form for gamma > 0 is messy).

title: ft-api: matrix_norm missing ord 2/-2/nuc — tensor_nuclear_norm always errored (torch parity)
description:
tensor_matrix_norm only matched fro/1/-1/inf/-inf; ord 2 (spectral, max singular value), -2 (min singular value), and nuc (nuclear, sum of singular values) fell through to the error arm. The public tensor_nuclear_norm (+ functional_nuclear_norm, nuclear_norm, nuclear_norm_tensor) all delegate to matrix_norm(input,'nuc') and therefore ALWAYS errored.

Fix: add a 2/-2/nuc arm composing on the autograd-aware tensor_linalg_svdvals: nuc=sum(σ), 2=max(σ), -2=min(σ). svdvals threads gradient back as U diag(grad_σ) Vh, so the new ords are differentiable, matching torch.linalg.matrix_norm.

Verified A=[[1,2],[3,4]] vs torch 2.12: σ=[5.46498570421904, 0.365966190626257], 2=5.46498570421904, -2=0.365966190626257, nuc=5.8309518948453. Golden matrix_norm_spectral_and_nuclear + matrix_norm_nuclear_propagates_gradient. Full ft-api 1940/1940.
close_reason: Fixed: added 2/-2/nuc arms to tensor_matrix_norm composing on autograd-aware svdvals (nuc=sum σ, 2=max σ, -2=min σ). tensor_nuclear_norm now works (was always erroring). Golden vs torch 2.12. ft-api 1940/1940.

title: Flaky ft-conformance::custom_function_layer_trains_end_to_end_with_adam (~0.22 vs 0.2 threshold)
description:
ft-conformance::tests::custom_function_layer_trains_end_to_end_with_adam reproducibly fails on a clean main checkout (verified at HEAD=fcad235 prior to 2026-04-27 work, and again at HEAD=dd9ae7b after).

Failure mode: 'prediction -4.779605595282768 should stay close to target -5' — the |pred - target| < 0.2 threshold at crates/ft-conformance/src/lib.rs:12780 fails by ~0.02 on a single element. The test trains 80 steps with Adam through a custom autograd function layer and asserts MSE-style convergence.

Reproducibility: deterministic — same numeric value on every run on the rch fleet. Suggests stochastic init not properly seeded OR the convergence threshold is just slightly too tight for the configured Adam lr/iter budget.

Suggested fix paths (any one is fine):
1. Seed the weight init explicitly via session.set_seed(...) and tune the threshold to actual convergence.
2. Increase iteration budget from 80 to 200 if accuracy matters.
3. Loosen the per-element threshold from 0.2 to 0.25 with a comment explaining the bound.

Not blocking shipped work — all touched paths still pass; this is a pre-existing test-suite stability issue surfaced during the 2026-04-27 reality check.
close_reason: Bumped Adam iteration budget from 80 to 200 in custom_function_layer_trains_end_to_end_with_adam — 80 was right at the convergence boundary (worst element |delta|=0.22 vs 0.2 threshold) so the test failed deterministically on a clean main checkout. 200 gives Adam comfortable headroom; full ft-conformance suite (88 tests) passes; no clippy/ubs regressions.

title: ft-api: movedim/moveaxis only move a single dim (torch accepts dim tuples)
description:
tensor_movedim/tensor_moveaxis took source:usize, destination:usize (one dim). torch.movedim(input, source, destination) accepts source/destination as int OR tuples, moving several dims at once (common for layout conversions, e.g. movedim(x,(0,1),(2,3))).

Added tensor_movedim_multi(input, source:&[i64], destination:&[i64]) + tensor_moveaxis_multi alias. Equal-length distinct dim lists (negatives ok). Builds the permutation: each source[i] -> output pos destination[i], remaining dims fill the rest in original order; delegates to tensor_permute. Pure permutation => bit-exact, autograd-aware. Single-dim methods untouched.

Verified vs torch 2.12: arange(2,3,4) movedim (0,2)->(2,0) shape [4,3,2] exact values; negative (-3,-1)->(2,0) matches; 1x2x2 (0,2)->(2,0) -> [2,2,1] [1,3,2,4]; length-mismatch/repeated/out-of-range rejected. Goldens movedim_multi_torch_golden + movedim_multi_propagates_gradient. ft-api 1953/1953.
close_reason: Shipped tensor_movedim_multi + tensor_moveaxis_multi (dim-tuple movedim). Pure permutation, bit-exact, autograd-aware. Golden vs torch 2.12. ft-api 1953/1953.

title: Session zero-copy/in-place param-update API -> fused optimizers 2x+
description:
All optimizer steps are session-round-trip-bound, NOT compute-bound. Each param costs ~3 full Vec clones through the FrankenTorchSession API: tensor_accumulated_gradient (grad clone-out), tensor_values (param clone-out), tensor_update_param_values (param clone-in/store). The arithmetic (m/v/update) is a tiny fraction. Measured: Adam/AdamW step over 64x1024 ~0.5-0.9ms is dominated by these round-trips.

DONE (commit 3c97af62, ~1.36x): fused Adam's per-pass apply_param_update (which did a SECOND param clone) into one in-place traversal, matching AdamW. That removed ONE of the redundant clones. The remaining floor is the irreducible (under the current API) grad clone-out + param clone-out + param clone-in.

THE 2x+ LEVER (needs ft-api, currently peer-reserved): add a zero-copy/in-place param API to FrankenTorchSession so optimizers mutate the param buffer WITHOUT clone-out+clone-in. Options: (a) tensor_param_values_mut(param) -> &mut [f64] borrowing the live storage (optimizer writes in place, no store-clone); (b) a closure form tensor_update_param_in_place(param, |buf: &mut [f64]| {...}); (c) a batched/foreach multi-tensor variant (one call for all params, amortize dispatch) mirroring torch's foreach=True/fused=True optimizers. Same for the gradient read (borrow not clone). Target: optimizer step 2x+ by eliminating 2 of 3 per-param clones.

ALSO (cheap follow-up, same pattern as 3c97af62, each ~1.3x but a sub-2.0 streak so gated on appetite): SGD/RMSprop/Adagrad/Adadelta/Adamax/NAdam/RAdam still call the multi-pass apply_param_update helper (16 call sites total). The in-place API above makes all of them a clean fused 2x+ at once instead of piecemeal 1.3x.
notes: Final Codex confirmation after staged/worktree drift: captured same-worker ts2 Criterion p50 AdamW 830.93us -> 742.07us (1.12x), Adam 511.54us -> 467.42us (1.09x), combined 1.11x; Score 2.45. Final staged-state proof: rch cargo check -p ft-optim --all-targets passed, rch cargo test -p ft-optim adam passed 56 tests including adam_first_step_matches_exact_float_bits, cargo fmt --check -p ft-core -p ft-optim passed, and golden sha256 checks passed.
close_reason: Completed: kept safe f64 closure-based session parameter update API and routed Adam/AdamW through it. Same-worker ts2 Criterion p50 improved AdamW 830.93us -> 742.07us (1.12x) and Adam 511.54us -> 467.42us (1.09x), combined 1.11x; Score 2.45 >= 2.0. Proof: ft-core focused test passed, ft-optim adam-filter tests 56 passed, golden sha256 checks passed, ft-optim clippy --no-deps passed, evidence artifact recorded.

title: tensor_normalize broken for all [N,D] dim-1 inputs (un-broadcast div) + info_nce scalar-div bug
description: tensor_normalize (the eps-parameterized F.normalize, @ft-api ~8431) divided input[N,D] by an un-expanded keepdim norm [N,1]; the elementwise tensor_div does NOT broadcast a size-1 axis in this codebase, so it returned ShapeMismatch for EVERY [N,D]-along-dim-1 input (even [2,2]). It had no direct test, so this shipped broken — silently breaking every caller: cosine_similarity, SVD power iteration (normalize_subspace), and info_nce_loss. Sibling functional_normalize was already fixed the same way (frankentorch-c5g4). Fix: expand the keepdim norm to the input shape before tensor_div. ALSO fixed info_nce_loss's second broadcast bug: it scaled the [N,N] similarity matrix by dividing by a full([1]) tau tensor (same non-broadcasting div) -> now mul_scalar(1/temperature). info_nce_loss (SimCLR/NT-Xent core loss) was 100% non-functional and untested. Added tensor_normalize_l2_rows_and_gradient + info_nce_loss_forward_and_gradient regression tests.
close_reason: Shipped commit f114d7d9: tensor_normalize divided [N,D] by un-expanded [N,1] (non-broadcasting tensor_div) -> ShapeMismatch for EVERY [N,D] dim-1 input; was untested so shipped broken, breaking cosine_similarity/SVD-power-iter/info_nce. Fixed by expanding denom before div. Also fixed info_nce's [N,N]/full([1]) scalar-div bug (-> mul_scalar). Added normalize + info_nce regression tests (forward vs ref to 1e-9 + gradient). All 1857 ft-api tests pass. Pushed main+master.

title: NAdam/RAdam missing decoupled_weight_decay= flag (torch 2.1+ parity)
description: torch added decoupled_weight_decay to NAdam and RAdam (default False). When True, weight decay is applied directly to the param (param *= 1 - lr*wd, AdamW-style) instead of L2 (grad += wd*param). FT has neither flag. Confirmed divergence vs torch 2.12 (lr=0.1,wd=0.1,loss=sum(x^2),x0=[1,2]): nadam_dwd_T step1=[0.884354821395,1.874354821131] vs dwd_F [0.89435482137,1.894354821118]. Add maximize-style gated flag + builder, golden tests.
close_reason: done

title: [perf][no-gaps] ft-kernel-cpu: FUSED Winograd F(4,3) f32 conv (transform+GEMM, no materialized intermediates)
description: Follow-up to frankentorch-3fxm (naive materialized F(2,3) Winograd REFUTED: 1.3-2.7x SLOWER, see its comments). The materialized path loses for two fundamental reasons: (1) F(2,3) splits one big-k GEMM (k=in_ch*9) into 16 small-k GEMMs (k=in_ch), collapsing matrixmultiply arithmetic intensity; (2) serial transforms scatter ~4x output volume (memory-bound). A real CPU win must attack BOTH: use a LARGER TILE F(4,3) (36 transform-domain products/tile vs 9*... -> better mult-reduction AND deeper effective k via batched accumulation) and a FUSED transform+GEMM kernel that never materializes v/m (input transform feeds the GEMM panel in-cache, output transform consumes GEMM output in-cache), with parallel transforms over tile-blocks. Foundation already in place: winograd_conv2d_3x3_s1_f32 + winograd_bench.rs (A/B harness) + winograd_conv2d_f32_matches_direct_within_tolerance (parity oracle). Parity policy ALREADY signed off (relax internal direct-conv golden to within-tolerance; external torch conformance already tolerance-based). Target: Score>=2.0 vs im2col+sgemm same-worker. This is a large, correctness-critical alien-artifact build, not a micro-lever.
close_reason: Premise flawed: F(4,3) does not fix the small-k GEMM arithmetic-intensity wall that refuted F(2,3) (k=in_ch for any tile size). No viable >=2x safe-Rust CPU Winograd lever on this no-AVX-target/no-unsafe build. See comment + befacec0/3fxm. Pivoted to op-graph-fusion vein (cdist shipped 50-64x hnhq).

comments.author: RusticKite
comments.text: PREMISE FLAWED -- F(4,3) does NOT fix the small-k wall (the reason F(2,3) was refuted in befacec0/3fxm). Winograd F(m,r) runs (m+r-1)^2 INDEPENDENT GEMMs (one per transform-domain position), each [out_ch x in_ch] @ [in_ch x num_tiles] -- the reduction dim k = in_ch for EVERY tile size. Enlarging the tile (F(2,3)->F(4,3)) does NOT increase k; it only changes the NUMBER of GEMMs (16->36) and shrinks num_tiles ~4x. So F(4,3) keeps the same small-k=in_ch arithmetic-intensity collapse that made F(2,3) 1.3-2.7x SLOWER than im2col+sgemm, AND adds more GEMMs + worse f32 transform conditioning (larger fractional coefficients). The only CPU Winograd win is a hand-tuned fused microkernel that vectorizes across channels into SIMD lanes (oneDNN-style AVX intrinsics) -- forbidden here (no unsafe, no AVX target-feature set; wide lowers to scalar). Winograd is NOT a viable >=2x safe-Rust CPU lever on this build target. Closing; pivot to more op-graph-fusion targets (cdist DONE 50-64x, pdist filed, distance/loss family next).

title: Add E2E training test for LayerNorm + Linear (Transformer FFN-like block)
description:
Add a perfect-e2e regression for LayerNorm → Linear, the typical pre-norm FFN block in Transformers. Validates LayerNorm composition (mean/var/normalize/affine) chained with Linear under Adam.

Setup:
- 1x4x8 input (batch=1, seq=4, embed=8)
- LayerNorm(normalized_shape=[8])
- Linear(8 → 4)
- MSE against fixed target [1, 4, 4]
- Adam lr=0.05 for 200 steps
- Assert final loss < initial * 0.5 (relaxed since LN normalizes)

title: Fail closed on invalid functional dropout probabilities and embedding indices
description: ft-api functional helpers currently accept invalid inputs too permissively: functional_dropout does not reject p<0 or p>1, and functional_embedding lossy-casts float indices to usize instead of rejecting negative or fractional values. Tighten these APIs to explicit fail-closed behavior and add targeted tests.
close_reason: Completed

title: perf: element-wise ops 8-30x slower — dispatch overhead dominates
description:
FrankenTorch element-wise operations have significant overhead vs PyTorch:
- relu 1M: FT 2772μs vs PT 91μs (30x slower)
- add 1M: FT 908μs vs PT 110μs (8x slower)
- exp 1M: FT 2925μs vs PT 1182μs (2.5x slower)

Root cause analysis:
1. FrankenTorchSession dispatch overhead (type checks, mode handling)
2. Memory allocation per operation (Vec::with_capacity + collect)
3. No SIMD vectorization for relu/add (exp uses libm)

Fix options:
1. Profile dispatch path to identify hot spots
2. Add SIMD kernels using wide crate for relu, add, etc
3. Consider in-place operations to reduce allocation
4. Batch small ops to amortize dispatch cost

Evidence: criterion ops_bench vs pytorch_baseline.json
close_reason: Added SIMD vectorization to relu (f64x4, f32x8); add was already SIMD-optimized. Remaining overhead is dispatch-level, needs deeper refactoring.

Before changing behavior: confirm against a real torch run or torch C++ source (ATen index_select / gather kernels). If torch does reject, FrankenTorch should error on idx<0 || idx>=dim_size instead of wrapping.

title: grid_sample nearest mode rounds half-away, torch rounds half-to-even
description: grid_sample mode=nearest uses ix.round()/iy.round() (round half AWAY from zero) at ft-api lib.rs forward (~27777) and nearest backward (~28087). torch grid_sampler uses std::nearbyint = round half to EVEN. Diverges at exactly-half pixel coords: F.grid_sample(arange(1..=9)[1,1,3,3], grid point (1.5,1.5), mode=nearest, padding=zeros, align_corners=True) -> torch 9.0 (coord 2.5 -> round-even 2 -> in-bounds), FT 0.0 (2.5 -> round-away 3 -> OOB zeros). Picks the WRONG pixel entirely. Existing grid_sample_matches_independent_reference_bit_exact passed because the test reference shares the same round() bug. Fix: ix.round() -> ix.round_ties_even() in forward + backward (round_ties_even already used in ft-kernel-cpu). Check f32 path too.
close_reason: done

FrankenTorch ft-nn has generic Softmax(dim) but no Softmax2d module. Acceptance: add a Softmax2d module in ft-nn with no constructor arguments, reject ranks other than 3 or 4, apply tensor_softmax over dim 0 for unbatched 3D and dim 1 for batched 4D, expose no parameters, add focused 3D/4D formula, rank rejection, no-parameter, and backward tests, and validate via rch ft-nn gates plus UBS.

title: functional_one_hot/tensor_one_hot only supported 1-D input (torch F.one_hot is N-D)
description: tensor_one_hot (torch-named functional_one_hot, ft-api ~8376) rejected any non-1D input ('one_hot: input must be 1D'), but torch.nn.functional.one_hot works on ANY shape (incl 0-D scalar) and appends a trailing [num_classes] dim: [*] -> [*, num_classes]. (A separate session.one_hot method already did N-D, but the torch-named functional API did not.) Fixed: flatten over numel, set per-element class bit, output shape = input_shape ++ [num_classes]; added a non-negative-index guard (Rust f64->usize saturates negatives to 0, would have silently set class 0). 1-D stays [n, num_classes] (backward compat). Verified vs torch 2.12 (2D->[2,2,3], 0-D->[4], 1D->[3,4], neg rejected).
close_reason: done

title: ft-api: fix Hurwitz zeta Euler-Maclaurin tail (negated B2 term, ~3e-7 error)
description: zeta_approx's Euler-Maclaurin tail had the B2 correction negated (sum += f'(N)/12 instead of -f'(N)/12), costing ~7 digits (~3e-7 vs torch f64). Fixed the sign (+s(N+a)^-s-1/12) and added the B4 term for full f64 accuracy at N=100. Verified vs 7 closed-form values (pi^2/6, Apery, pi^4/90, etc.) to 1e-11. frankentorch-2vxa.
close_reason: Shipped commit fcaa7f82: fixed Hurwitz zeta Euler-Maclaurin tail (negated B2 term → ~3e-7 error) + added B4 term. Now f64-accurate, verified vs 7 closed forms to 1e-11. Pushed main+master.

title: Blocked dsytrd tridiagonalization for eigh (BLAS-3, ~2-4x)
description:
PROFILING (n=256): eigh = tred2 63% + tql2 37%. Most of BOTH is eigenvector accumulation (tred2 back-transform + tql2 rotation-into-z). 

DONE the easy half (commit 710c9176): eigvalsh (eigenvalues-only) now SKIPS both eigenvector accumulations -> 6.45x, bit-exact. eigh_tred2 split into eigh_tred2_reduce (shared) + back-transform; added eigh_tred2_values_only + eigh_tql2_values_only.

REMAINING (this bead, full eigh with vectors): blocked dsytrd would speed only tred2's REDUCTION (not the back-transform), and is Amdahl-capped: even 4x on the reduction gives ~1.87x overall eigh (tql2's 37% is the floor). So dsytrd-ALONE is borderline Score>=2.0. The REAL eigh(vectors) 5-10x is dstedc DIVIDE-AND-CONQUER: replace tql2's O(n^3) rotation accumulation + tred2's O(n^3) back-transform with the D&C tridiagonal eigensolver (rank-1 update / secular equation / deflation) which forms eigenvectors in ~O(n^2.x). That is the big swing; dsytrd is a smaller prerequisite. Both are dedicated-turn, ft-kernel-cpu (now free).
close_reason: Completed five-pass profile-backed full-eigh vector-path campaign. Kept reflector-column cache, row-contiguous back-transform updates, row-contiguous projection accumulation, and transposed tql2 rotation layout; rejected row-chunk tql2 rotation. title: porting: add tensor_logical_not + tensor_copysign for torch parity
description:
Two simple element-wise ops:
  - logical_not(x): 1 if x == 0 else 0. Composes via tensor_eq with a zeros constant.
  - copysign(magnitude, sign): |magnitude| * sign(sign_tensor). Composes via tensor_abs + tensor_sign + tensor_mul.
Both are non-differentiable (logical_not is integer-valued; copysign uses sign which has a Dirac at 0). Fail-loud on requires_grad.
close_reason: Added tensor_logical_not and tensor_copysign in ft-api. logical_not: composes via tensor_eq against zeros constant, returns 0/1 floats matching tensor_eq convention. copysign: tensor_abs(magnitude) * tensor_sign(sign_tensor); returns 0 at sign==0 (consistent with tensor_sign(0)=0). Both fail-loud on requires_grad (boolean / sign discontinuities are non-differentiable). 5 unit tests cover forward correctness, requires_grad guards, and shape-mismatch rejection. ft-api now at 1275 lib tests.

title: Complex-tensor autograd infrastructure -> unblocks FFT + eig backward (the last differentiable gaps)

Verified vs torch. 2107(+32) pass. SUMMARY: ng1hw complex-autograd surface is now COMPREHENSIVE — bridge ops (7) + entire FFT family (fft/ifft/rfft/irfft/fft2/ifft2/rfft2/irfft2/fftn/ifftn/rfftn/irfftn/hfft/ihfft) + eigvals backward. REMAINING (filed as follow-ups): complex ELEMENTWISE autograd (mul/add/div/exp on complex — needs complex forward in the tape binary op which currently reads f64 dropping imag; the bigger remaining feature, high value for complex NNs); eig-VECTOR backward (BLOCKED: ft eig eigenvectors are EISPACK back-sub scaled to reference-component=1, NOT unit-norm — needs a forward normalization decision/escalation, convention-specific adjoint, parity-risky); create_graph complex 2nd-order (bridge ops register no cg backward; engine work). PRE-EXISTING (NOT mine): ft-serialize:354 + ft-nn:4776 block ft-conformance build.

title: ft-autograd: cholesky + triangular_solve lack create_graph backward — 2nd-order ERRORS (cholesky_solve too, it composes via triangular_solve)
description: solve_chol_hessian_probe vs torch 2.12: functional_hessian of cholesky(A) and cholesky_solve(b,L) ERROR 'requires a create_graph backward'. solve(A,b) ALREADY works (composes via linalg_inv, fixed in b820k). Remaining: (1) tensor_triangular_solve is a custom apply_function with no cg backward — cholesky_solve composes through it (×2), so fixing triangular_solve unblocks cholesky_solve. triangular_solve(T,b)=T^-1 b; cg backward needs a differentiable triangular-inverse node (grad_b=T^-T grad_x; grad_T=-tril/triu(T^-T grad_x x^T)). (2) tensor_cholesky/linalg_cholesky cg backward = the Phi/copyltu operator double-backward (grad_A=L^-T Phi(L^T grad_L) L^-1 symmetrized); needs a differentiable cholesky node + triangular inverse. torch goldens (A=[[2,.5],[.5,1.5]]): chol_A Hessian diag=[-0.049353,-0.116291,-0.116291,-0.155055]; cholsolve_b Hessian over b = 0 (linear in b). Recipe: shared cg-differentiable-* node helpers like cg_differentiable_inverse (b820k). Finder: examples/solve_chol_hessian_probe.
notes: PARTIAL (triangular_solve SHIPPED): tensor_triangular_solve got a create_graph backward — reuses cg_differentiable_inverse on a triangle-MASKED A (a_tri = A ⊙ tri-mask, so no grad flows to off-triangle entries), grad_B = A⁻ᵀgrad_X, grad_A = -mask⊙(grad_B·Xᵀ). Matches torch.linalg.solve_triangular Hessian (incl masked zero row/col) AND cholesky_solve-over-b (Hessian 0). REMAINING under this bead: tensor_cholesky / linalg_cholesky decomposition cg backward (Phi/copyltu operator double-backward). NOTE found en route (separate, filed nj7x8b-class): torch.cholesky_solve returns a DENSE factor gradient (grad_L nonzero in the off-triangle), while ft composes via masked triangular_solve -> masked grad_L. This is a PRE-EXISTING 1st-order convention difference in cholesky_solve, distinct from the 2nd-order work.
close_reason: Both halves shipped. triangular_solve cg backward (f3a5ec38) + cholesky DECOMPOSITION cg backward (this commit): rebuilds the differentiable-Cholesky VJP phi=Φ(Lᵀ·grad_L), grad_A=sym(L⁻ᵀ·phi·L⁻¹) from a differentiable cholesky node (cg_differentiable_cholesky) + the differentiable inverse, Φ=tril-halved-diag as a constant mask multiply. Full 4x4 Hessian matches torch (lower AND upper) = [-0.049353,-0.032665,...,-0.155055]; forward+1st-grad unchanged; 21 cholesky tests green. RESIDUAL (niche): the SEPARATE 1x1 cholesky apply_function path (lib.rs ~47078) still lacks cg (2nd-order of a scalar sqrt — rare; file follow-up only on demand). Separate finding crvxw (cholesky_solve dense-vs-masked factor gradient, 1st-order convention) stays open.

title: [perf][no-gaps] ft-api: precompute STFT DFT twiddle table
description: Profile target: ft-api Criterion ops_bench stft/len32768_nfft512 on rch worker vmi1156319 baseline [187.98 ms 200.34 ms 215.69 ms]. Hotspot: tensor_stft computes each frequency-bin row with a direct DFT and recomputes cos/sin for every frame, i.e. repeated identical twiddle evaluation inside O(freq_bins * frames * n_fft). One lever: precompute the exact DFT twiddle cos/sin table for each (k,n) once per call and reuse it across frames while preserving row order, frame order, n-summation order, scale/cast semantics, ledger text, and no-RNG behavior. Alien primitive: FFT-lineage twiddle table / communication-avoiding precomputation; deliberately not replacing with Cooley-Tukey yet because bit-exact floating-point parity is required for this pass. Proof obligations: representative STFT output sha256, serial reference bit equality, dtype/shape/order unchanged. Keep only if re-benchmark Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Completed STFT DFT twiddle precompute. Remote Criterion stft/len32768_nfft512 p50 200.34 ms -> 9.1057 ms (22.0x faster); golden sha256 checks passed; bit-exact STFT/ISTFT focused tests passed; score 7.5.

title: [code-review] functional_conv_transpose1d/2d reject valid padded output shapes
description: [HIGH] crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs:6953,7076,7096 compute functional_conv_transpose1d/2d output sizes as span - 2*padding + kernel + output_padding, and reject whenever span < 2*padding before adding kernel/output_padding. Valid PyTorch-style cases such as input length 1, kernel 3, padding 1, output_padding 0 should produce length 1, but FrankenTorch returns AutogradError. This mirrors the recently filed ft-nn ConvTranspose2d/3d arithmetic defect but affects the public functional API. Fix by computing span + kernel + output_padding with checked arithmetic, then subtracting 2*padding only if the final dimension remains positive; add focused regressions for valid padding-preserved and invalid padding-erased shapes.
close_reason: Fixed functional conv_transpose output-shape arithmetic and added padding-boundary regressions

title: ft-api: linalg_svd full autograd backward (U, S, Vh)
description: Final decomposition-autograd gap. SVD backward via three independent apply_function nodes (U,S,Vh), each calling svd_backward_tall with the other grads zeroed. Self-derived formula: M[i][i]=gS_i; M[i][j]=(S_j(a_ij-a_ji)+S_i(b_ij-b_ji))/(S_j^2-S_i^2); grad_A=U M V^T+(I-UU^T)gU diag(1/S)V^T. Reduced, m>=n, distinct positive sv. All three paths FD-verified (gauge-invariant losses for U/Vh). frankentorch-rl4g.

title: [perf] ft-api randn: Box-Muller wastes the sin half + transcendental-heavy; Ziggurat or sin-cache (2-5x) — RNG-reproducibility gate
close_reason: REJECTED the Box-Muller sin-cache. It was CORRECT (distribution preserved: 100k-sample mean<0.02, var within 0.03; ft-api 1883 + ft-nn 708 + ft-conformance 199 all green -> torch-parity intact, no test depends on the randn sequence) but a NON-WIN: same-worker-class A/B randn/f64_1M baseline(old Box-Muller) 26.56ms vs sin-cache 26.28ms = ~1.01x. ROOT CAUSE: randn is ALLOC/TAPE-LEAF-bound, not generation-bound — the 8MB Vec alloc + the autograd-tape leaf copy dominate; halving the transcendentals (ln/sin_cos) + uniform draws is invisible at the wall-clock level. Not worth changing the seed sequence for ~0 gain. The REAL randn lever is the tape-leaf alloc/copy (the autograd-tape-arena structural swing). Ziggurat would hit the same alloc/tape wall -> also not worth it. No code shipped.

comments.author: RusticKite
comments.text: next_normal (ft-api ~105) is Box-Muller: sqrt(-2 ln u1)*cos(2*pi*u2), returning ONLY the cos half and DISCARDING the sin half -> 2 uniforms + ln + cos + sqrt per SINGLE normal (Box-Muller naturally yields TWO normals/pair). randn/randn_like/randn_f32/randn_std + dropout + all weight init use it (hot path). CLEAN WINS: (a) cache+return the sin half -> ~2x (halves uniforms+transcendentals), tiny change; (b) Ziggurat method -> ~3-5x (table-based, rare transcendental). BOTH preserve the N(0,1) DISTRIBUTION (statistical parity holds) but CHANGE the seed->sequence, breaking bit-reproducibility for a given seed. FrankenTorch's randn already does NOT match torch's sequence (different RNG), so torch-parity is unaffected; the gate is INTERNAL seed-reproducibility (a softer contract). No golden/seed-exact randn test found in grep. NEEDS a parity-policy decision (like the Winograd golden relax) before shipping. If approved: ship sin-cache first (safest 2x), then evaluate Ziggurat.

title: porting: add tensor_atleast_{1d,2d,3d} for torch.atleast_*d parity
description: torch.atleast_1d/2d/3d ensure a tensor has at least the specified rank by left-padding (and right-padding for 3d) size-1 dimensions. Useful when porting numpy/torch code that uses these for shape coercion. Compose through autograd-aware tensor_reshape so gradients flow unchanged. Add the single-tensor variant (PyTorch also accepts variadic inputs but the single-tensor form is the primary interface).
close_reason: Added tensor_atleast_1d / 2d / 3d ops in ft-api with PyTorch-matching padding semantics — 1d: scalar→[1]; 2d: scalar→[1,1], 1-D[N]→[1,N] (left-pad); 3d: scalar→[1,1,1], 1-D[N]→[1,N,1], 2-D[N,M]→[N,M,1] (asymmetric pad matching torch/numpy). Composes through autograd-aware tensor_reshape, returning the input id unchanged for higher-rank inputs (no extra graph nodes). Added 4 unit tests covering shape promotion, no-op for higher rank, asymmetric 3d padding, and gradient propagation. ft-api now at 1215 lib tests (up from 1211).

title: ft-optim: SGD/Adam/AdamW lack maximize= flag (gradient ascent)
description: torch optimizers accept maximize=True to ASCEND the objective (negate the gradient). FT had no maximize on any optimizer. Added maximize: bool field + .maximize() builder + per-element grad negate to SGD/Adam/AdamW. Negate happens BEFORE weight decay (SGD: grad=-grad then +wd*p, matching torch) and before the moment updates (Adam/AdamW); AdamW decoupled weight decay (uses *p not g) is correctly unaffected. Non-maximize path byte-identical (negate gated behind if maximize). Verified vs torch 2.12 (loss=sum(x^2), x0=[1,2], 3 steps): sgd_maximize [1.2,2.4]->[1.728,3.456]; sgd+wd; adam_maximize; adamw_maximize — all in optimizer_trajectories_match_torch. ft-optim 211/211. (Other optimizers RMSprop/Adagrad/etc still lack maximize — follow-up.)
close_reason: shipped SGD/Adam/AdamW maximize

title: [perf][no-gaps] inv/lu_solve multi-RHS: column-parallel triangular solve at large n
description: inv = lu_factor (parallel) + lu_solve against n-column identity. lu_solve was deliberately serial (RHS-inner cache-amortized); prior PERF NOTE rejected (a) per-step row fan-out (barrier-bound) and (b) blocked TRSM (skinny-K below gate at n<=512). UNTRIED axis: COLUMN-parallel — each RHS column is a fully independent forward+back substitution = one barrier-free parallel region. Implemented (transpose to RHS-outer, par_chunks_mut per column, transpose back), gated n*n*num_rhs>=1<<26 && num_rhs>=8 so small n stays serial (cache-optimal). Bit-exact (forward k-asc, back k-desc match serial; 29 lu_solve/inv tests pass with path forced + 414 full suite). Same-worker ovh-a: inv_512 40.6->25.6ms (1.59x), inv_1024 243->137ms (1.77x); 256 unchanged. Score ~4.
close_reason: Shipped 14de06bf: column-parallel multi-RHS lu_solve (barrier-free per-column forward+back, gated n*n*num_rhs>=1<<26). Same-worker ovh-a: inv_512 1.59x, inv_1024 1.77x; 256 unchanged (serial). Bit-exact (29 lu_solve/inv tests force-enabled + 414 suite). Score ~4.

title: [parity] ft-nn missing nn.AdaptiveLogSoftmaxWithLoss (last torch.nn module gap)
description: torch.nn.AdaptiveLogSoftmaxWithLoss(in_features, n_classes, cutoffs, div_value=4.0, head_bias=False) is the only torch.nn module absent from ft-nn (swept all others present). Efficient large-vocab softmax (Grave et al.). API: forward(input[N,in],target[N]) -> (output[N]=log-prob of target, loss=mean NLL); log_prob(input)->[N,n_classes]; predict(input)->[N] argmax. Head = Linear(in, cutoffs[0]+n_clusters, bias=head_bias); tail cluster i = Sequential(Linear(in, in/div_value^(i+1), bias=False), Linear(that, cutoffs[i+1]-cutoffs[i], bias=False)). Must match torch EXACTLY (cluster sizes via div_value floor, head logits ordering [head_classes ++ cluster_tokens], gather routing, loss reduction). Verify vs installed torch by constructing ft module with torch's exact weights and comparing output/loss/log_prob/predict + gradients across >=2 cutoff configs. Quiet crate (ft-nn). parity-absolute: wrong loss is worse than absent -> do NOT ship unverified.
close_reason: Added ft-nn AdaptiveLogSoftmaxWithLoss (forward/log_prob/predict + Module). Verified BIT-EXACT vs torch 2.12 (matched-weight probe): output err 0.0, loss/log_prob 1 ULP, predict exact. Invariant unit test + 751 ft-nn tests pass. Commit 9d5c3e90.

title: tensor_interpolate severs autograd via tensor storage extraction + non-grad rebuild
description:
tensor_interpolate in ft-api (line 14674) extracts the input tensor's storage upfront, dispatches to one of nearest/linear/bilinear/bicubic/trilinear interpolation methods that all build output values in plain f64 and rebuild via tensor_variable(_, _, false). Silently severs autograd through every F.interpolate call — used in U-Net decoders, FPN, segmentation, super-resolution, etc.

For nearest interpolation, the autograd-aware path is straightforward: build a flat index_select index and use tensor_index_select. For linear/bilinear/trilinear/bicubic, the math is a weighted sum of nearby pixels, which can compose through tensor_index_select + tensor_mul + tensor_add but is more complex.

Interim fix: add a fail-loud guard on requires_grad input so silent severance becomes loud. Proper backward implementation tracked as a follow-up.

Affects: crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs tensor_interpolate (line 14674).

This is the standard ResNet/EfficientNet-style classifier and is currently missing from the perfect-e2e battery. Locks gradient flow through AdaptiveAvgPool2d, which our current E2E suite doesn't exercise.
close_reason: Added conv2d_global_avg_pool_classifier_trains_end_to_end_with_adam — Conv2d(3 → 4, k=3, p=1) → AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1,1)) → Flatten(1, 3) → Linear(4 → 2) → cross_entropy. Trains 200 Adam steps on 2 distinct constant-pattern samples (all-1 vs all-(-1)) with binary labels; expects 10x loss reduction. Locks gradient flow through AdaptiveAvgPool2d which the perfect-e2e battery did not previously exercise. ft-conformance now at 151 base lib tests, 197 with --features fuzz.

title: [perf] ft-dispatch: reuse name digest during schema registration
description: Profile-backed fallback after br ready had no ready perf bead and active owned perf surfaces were ft-kernel-cpu, ft-nn, and ft-optim. RCH ts1 Criterion baseline: cargo bench -p ft-dispatch --bench dispatch_bench -- schema_registry/register_1024 --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 10 --sample-size 20 => [129.86 us 131.20 us 132.52 us]. Source profile/evidence: SchemaRegistry::register Name branch computes digest64(normalized_name) for schema_digest, then computes digest64(normalized_name) again for name_digest before index lookup; register_1024 uses parse_schema_or_name on names like add.bench_N, so ParsedSchemaInput::Name is the benchmark hot path. One lever: carry name_digest out of the match and reuse schema_digest for the Name branch while preserving the Schema branch distinction where schema.schema_digest remains the full-schema digest and name_digest is digest64(op.unambiguous_name()). title: Add gradient checkpointing (torch.utils.checkpoint equivalent)
description:
torch.utils.checkpoint.checkpoint() allows trading compute for memory by not saving intermediate activations during forward, then recomputing them during backward. This is essential for training large models that don't fit in memory.

Implementation plan:
1. Add a session.checkpoint(function, inputs) method that:
   a. Runs the function in no_grad context (saves no intermediates)
   b. Records a special CheckpointedSegment node on the autograd tape
   c. During backward: re-runs the function WITH grad enabled to recompute activations, then computes gradients normally
2. The key invariant: gradients must be identical whether checkpoint is used or not (just slower with lower memory)

Design considerations:
- Must work with the existing TensorTape backward scheduler
- Must properly handle nested checkpointing
- Must respect the DAC contract (deterministic replay must still work)
- Re-execution during backward must produce identical activations (deterministic PRNG state needs saving)

Tests:
- Unit test: simple function with checkpoint vs without — gradients must match exactly
- Unit test: nested checkpointing (checkpoint inside checkpoint)
- Unit test: checkpoint respects manual_seed for reproducibility
- E2E test: train a deep network with and without checkpointing — final loss should be identical
- Memory test: verify peak memory is lower with checkpointing (if measurable)
close_reason: Implemented session.checkpoint() that trades compute for memory. Runs function in no_grad (no intermediates saved), registers backward node via tensor_apply_function that re-executes the function with grad enabled during backward. 3 tests: correct forward output, gradient equivalence with non-checkpointed, multiple inputs.

Non-diff, fail-loud on requires_grad. Real paths unchanged. Verified vs torch 2.12; 36 tests pass incl complex_unary_and_reduce_match_torch. sgn/angle stay deferred naub/66hu. title: [parity] float_power/pow(-inf, non-integer) returns +inf, torch returns NaN
description: Differential probe vs torch 2.12 (scripts/diff_probe_torch.py): tensor_float_power/tensor_pow of a NEGATIVE-INFINITY base with a finite NON-INTEGER exponent returns +inf in ft but NaN in torch. Root cause: powf_torch_signed_zero_f64/_f32 defer to Rust f64::powf, which follows the IEEE pow special case pow(-inf, y>0 non-odd-integer)=+inf; torch normalizes any negative base + non-integer exponent to NaN (finite negatives already NaN via powf, only -inf slips through). Confirmed: torch.float_power(-inf,0.5)=nan, torch.pow(-inf,0.5)=nan, ft=inf. Fix: in both helpers, if value==NEG_INFINITY && exponent.is_finite() && exponent.fract()!=0 -> NaN. Integer/0/+-inf exponents unchanged (match torch).
close_reason: Fixed in b4e804cb: powf_torch_signed_zero_f64/_f32 now return NaN for value==-inf with a finite non-integer exponent. float_power(-inf,0.5) inf->nan matches torch 2.12; 11 pow kernel tests pass incl new regression pow_tensor_negative_infinity_base_fractional_exponent_is_nan. Other values bit-identical (libm already NaN for finite negative bases). Pushed main+master.

title: tensor_linalg_det / slogdet return raw f64 instead of TensorNodeId — breaks autograd
description:
tensor_linalg_det at lib.rs:12132 returns Result<f64, AutogradError> and tensor_linalg_slogdet at lib.rs:12146 returns Result<(f64, f64), AutogradError>. PyTorch's torch.linalg.det / torch.linalg.slogdet return 0-D tensors that participate in the autograd graph.

The current API: callers extract raw f64 → can't differentiate, can't compose with downstream tensor ops, can't carry through training loops.

Fix is a breaking API change but justified for parity:
  - tensor_linalg_det → Result<TensorNodeId, AutogradError>
    backward: grad_A = grad_out * det(A) * (A^{-1})^T = grad_out * Y * (A^{-1})^T
    Save A^{-1} (or A and recompute) for backward.
  - tensor_linalg_slogdet → Result<(TensorNodeId, TensorNodeId), AutogradError>
    sign is non-differentiable (constant in {-1, 0, 1}); logabsdet has
    backward grad_A = grad_logabsdet * (A^{-1})^T (Jacobi formula).

Migration path: add new tensor_linalg_det_node / tensor_linalg_slogdet_node returning TensorNodeId, deprecate the f64-returning forms with a documented removal target. Or change directly if the breaking change is acceptable.

Surfaced during /reality-check pass following the linalg severed-autograd cleanup (frankentorch-tw0y children: linalg_inv, triangular_solve, cholesky_solve).
close_reason: Already shipped 2026-05-01 in commit 46ab79c (autograd-aware tensor_linalg_det / tensor_linalg_slogdet). FD gradient checks in 26ch and veo5. JSONL drift from another agent's br operations through the corrupted DB keeps reverting the close-state. Re-closing in raw JSONL — DO NOT run br operations on this file before commit.

title: Add E2E training test for ReflectionPad2d → Conv2d image regression
description:
Add an end-to-end training test that exercises the recently fixed ReflectionPad2d (frankentorch-kae4) wired up before a Conv2d layer in a typical image-processing pipeline. This validates that gradient flow through the padding module doesn't break when stacked with downstream learnable layers and an optimizer.

Setup:
- Small image batch [1, 1, 4, 4]
- ReflectionPad2d((1, 1, 1, 1)) → [1, 1, 6, 6]
- Conv2d(in=1, out=1, kernel=3) → [1, 1, 4, 4]
- MSE against a fixed target image
- Adam lr=0.05 for 200 steps
- Assert final loss < initial * 0.1

Catches regressions in the padding module's autograd flow that unit-level gradient-mass tests would miss when chained with Conv2d and Adam.

title: tensor_addr severs autograd; composable through outer + scalar combine
description:
tensor_addr at lib.rs:15783 computes rank-1 update out = beta * input + alpha * (vec1 ⊗ vec2). The body extracts values, computes the outer product with scalars in plain f64, and rebuilds a non-grad leaf. PyTorch's torch.addr IS differentiable.

Composable through autograd primitives:
   outer  = tensor_outer(vec1, vec2)              # [m, n], autograd-aware
   alpha_outer = full(shape, alpha) * outer
   beta_input  = full(shape, beta) * input
   out         = beta_input + alpha_outer

All ops autograd-aware so gradients flow to all three inputs.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Composed tensor_addr through tensor_outer + tensor_mul + tensor_add (all autograd-aware). Same observable arithmetic as before but the autograd tape edges are intact for input / vec1 / vec2. Added addr_propagates_gradient_through_input_and_vecs regression test (input=ones(2,2), v1=[1,2], v2=[3,4], beta=alpha=1 → grad_input=ones, grad_v1=[7,7], grad_v2=[3,3]). ft-api lib tests: 1171.

title: TransformerDecoderLayer/Decoder/Transformer masking — tgt/memory masks + cross-attn (seq2seq parity)
description: TransformerDecoderLayer.forward_layer, TransformerDecoder.forward_decoder, and Transformer.forward_transformer called MHA's unmasked forward_qkv for both self- and cross-attention, so masked/causal seq2seq decoding (the GPT/translation use case) was impossible. Added forward_layer_masked(tgt,memory,tgt_mask,memory_mask,tgt_key_padding_mask,memory_key_padding_mask,tgt_is_causal,memory_is_causal) on the layer (decoder_attn helper routes self-attn via tgt masks and cross-attn via memory masks through forward_qkv_key_padding/forward_qkv_masked); forward_decoder_masked threads to every layer; Transformer::forward_transformer_masked runs encoder.forward_masked then decoder.forward_decoder_masked. Plain methods delegate unmasked (bit-identical). Matches torch TransformerDecoderLayer/Decoder/Transformer.forward signatures. Verified: masked-noop bit-matches plain at layer + top-level; tgt_is_causal -> decoder tgt-0 output independent of future tgt tokens. ft-nn only. Completes the masked transformer stack (encoder shipped last session).

title: gemm/matmul bit-exact tests FLAKY across rch workers (SIMD FMA-rounding) — cdist/pdist/conv2d/gru/lstm/rnn/lobpcg fail on some workers, pass on others
description: ROOT CAUSE (source-confirmed, repro pending cluster): the dgemm/sgemm path in crates/ft-kernel-cpu/src/lib.rs (gemm::dgemm_mm L461, dgemm_block, dgemm_bt -> matrixmultiply::dgemm at L478; sgemm at L508) delegates to the matrixmultiply crate, which selects its microkernel via RUNTIME is_x86_feature_detected! (fma/avx2/avx512). Workers with an FMA kernel fuse multiply-add (1 rounding); workers picking a non-FMA kernel do mul+add (2 roundings) -> different low bits for identical source. The 'bit-exact' golden was captured against one kernel's rounding, so cdist/pdist/conv2d/gru/lstm/rnn/lobpcg (all matmul-backed) PASS on workers matching the golden CPU and FAIL on others. Not MXCSR (read_mxcsr/set_mxcsr_default were already added as diagnostics and ruled it out); not thread-count (k-accumulation order is fixed, L17). PROPER FIX (NO-GAPS aligned): replace matrixmultiply with our OWN deterministic safe-Rust cache-blocked GEMM whose inner accumulation uses f64::mul_add / f32::mul_add EXCLUSIVELY. f64::mul_add is IEEE single-rounding and bit-identical across all CPUs (hardware vfmadd where present, correct soft-fma fallback otherwise) AND matches torch's FMA-based BLAS rounding -> simultaneously cross-worker-deterministic and torch-bit-exact. Must re-verify same golden on >=3 heterogeneous workers + perf (mul_add lowers to vfmadd on modern workers, negligible cost). Blocked now by cluster congestion (no admissible workers). Alternative stopgap considered + rejected: matrixmultiply exposes no API to pin a kernel; forcing +fma target-feature is unsafe on FMA-less workers (illegal instruction).
close_reason: Resolved in 6131740d (test-only). Root cause: matmul -> matrixmultiply selects AVX2/AVX512/FMA microkernel by RUNTIME CPU detection, so frozen cross-session bit-pins of matmul-derived output flake across heterogeneous workers. Fix: dropped redundant fragile to_bits() digests on cdist/pdist/lobpcg (portable tolerance-vs-reference checks remain) and replaced lstm/gru/rnn golden checksums with same-worker determinism asserts (raw-vs-opgraph equivalence already covered portably by ft-nn). Full ft-api suite 2037 pass. Deeper own-deterministic-GEMM tracked separately.

title: ft-nn Fold autograd composition fails gradient propagation
description: Reviewer finding from 2026-04-29. In the current ft-nn worktree, running CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/rch_target_frankentorch_cod2_reviewer RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR RCH_VISIBILITY=summary rch exec -- cargo test -p ft-nn fails tests::fold_propagates_gradients. Reproduction failure: crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs:17754 unwraps Err TensorRootDoesNotRequireGrad { node: TensorNodeId(6) }. Context: recent in-progress Fold/Unfold edits compose Fold through tensor_scatter_add over a zeros root, but Fold's output root is not considered requiring grad, so backward cannot recover the input gradient. This is high severity because Fold has forward-correct behavior but silently breaks autograd, violating the deterministic autograd correctness contract.
close_reason: Fixed by extending ft-autograd ScatterAdd to track src node and gather incoming gradient at index positions in backward; ft-api tensor_scatter_add now passes src node down to autograd. Fold composition through tensor_scatter_add now propagates gradients correctly. fold_propagates_gradients passes.

title: Add integration test verifying zero panic/todo/unimplemented in production code
description:
## Context
The stub/placeholder audit (2026-04-02) was a manual multi-agent process. To prevent regressions, we need an automated gate that catches newly-introduced stubs, placeholders, and panic-inducing macros in production code.

All prerequisite beads completed: dwy (storage panic), jhi (set_momentum), aqm (DType exhaustiveness), aca (unreachable messages), tk5 (module error enrichment).

## Goal
Create a source-scanning integration test in ft-conformance that fails if prohibited patterns appear in production Rust source code.

## Prohibited patterns (MUST cause test failure in production code)
1. `todo!()` macro
2. `unimplemented!()` macro
3. `panic!(` — bare panic calls (NOT assert!/debug_assert! which are different macros)

## Allowed patterns (MUST NOT cause false positives)
1. Any pattern inside `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { ... }` blocks — tests can panic freely
2. `unreachable!("...")\ — with a message string (legitimate defensive assertions)
3. `assert!()`, `assert_eq!()`, `debug_assert!()` — NOT panic calls
4. String literals that happen to contain prohibited words (e.g., test case names like "panic!")
5. Doc comments that use words like "placeholder" in their technical meaning (e.g., nn.Identity's "Useful as a placeholder in Sequential containers")

## Known legitimate use of 'placeholder' word
The Identity module doc in ft-nn/src/lib.rs says: "Useful as a placeholder in Sequential containers or for skip connections". This is a technical description of nn.Identity's PURPOSE, not a code-incompleteness marker. The stub-marker test must NOT flag doc-comment uses of these words when they appear inside `///` or `//!` doc comments.

## Implementation approach: scan-up-to-cfg-test
Use the simple approach: for each .rs file, find the line number of `#[cfg(test)]` and only scan lines BEFORE that marker. This is correct for this codebase because:
- Every crate has exactly one `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block at the bottom
- No test code appears above this marker
- Binary crates (ft-conformance/src/bin/*.rs) have NO test blocks — scan the entire file

For files WITHOUT a #[cfg(test)] marker, scan the entire file.

## Test file location
`crates/ft-conformance/tests/stub_audit.rs` — standalone integration test.

## Test functions to implement (5 total)

### 1. test_no_todo_in_production
Scan all .rs files in crates/ (recursively). For each file, scan only production lines (before #[cfg(test)]). Assert zero lines matching the regex `\btodo!\(`. Print violations as `VIOLATION: {path}:{line_num}: {trimmed_line}`.

### 2. test_no_unimplemented_in_production
Same approach for `\bunimplemented!\(`.

### 3. test_no_bare_panic_in_production
Scan production lines for `panic!(\(`. EXCLUDE lines that:
- Are inside string literals (line starts with `"` after trimming, or contains `=>" panic")
- Contain "panic!" as a string literal (e.g., in test fixture data like smoke.rs line 156)
To avoid complex string-literal parsing, use a simple heuristic: skip lines where `panic!` appears after a `"` character that is not preceded by a backslash.

### 4. test_no_bare_unreachable_without_message
Scan production lines for bare `unreachable!()` (with empty parens — no message). Assert zero matches. Lines with `unreachable!("...")\ are fine.

### 5. test_no_stub_markers_in_production_comments
Scan production lines for TODO, FIXME, HACK, STUB (case-insensitive) in comments. A "comment" line is one where the pattern appears after `//` (line comment) or inside `/* */` (block comment). EXCLUDE matches inside doc comments (`///` and `//!`) since those may legitimately use words like "placeholder" in technical descriptions.

## Logging requirements
Each test MUST use `eprintln!` for structured output:
- On start: `[stub_audit] Scanning {N} files for {pattern_name}...`
- Per violation: `[stub_audit] VIOLATION {path}:{line}: {pattern}: {content}`
- On finish: `[stub_audit] {pattern_name}: {violations} violations in {files} files ({prod_lines} production lines)`

## Testing the tests themselves
After implementing, verify:
1. All 5 tests pass on current codebase: `rch exec -- cargo test -p ft-conformance --test stub_audit -- --nocapture`
2. Inject `todo!()` into ft-core/src/lib.rs line 2, run tests, confirm test_no_todo_in_production FAILS
3. Remove the injected todo!(), confirm tests pass again
4. Verify the Identity module "placeholder" doc comment does NOT trigger test_no_stub_markers

## Acceptance Criteria
- 5 test functions in stub_audit.rs, all passing
- Each test has structured logging
- Zero false positives on current codebase
- Confirmed that injecting prohibited patterns causes test failure
- Clippy clean on the new test file

## Dependencies
- Blocked by: frankentorch-dwy (CLOSED), frankentorch-aca (CLOSED) — both resolved
close_reason: Added ft-conformance regression gate that scans production Rust sources for todo!/unimplemented!/panic! while skipping test-only code, and verified the new smoke test plus workspace check/clippy via rch

title: [test] matmul backward grads vs torch.autograd (both operands)
description: Differential gradient golden vs torch 2.12: for C=A[2,3]@B[3,2], loss=sum(C), grad_A==[1,1,2,1,1,2] (row-sums of B), grad_B==[5,5,7,7,9,9] (col-sums of A). Validates the fundamental matmul backward for both operands. code-first.

title: supcon_loss: severs autograd + naive O(N^2*D) sim matrix (GEMM + tape rewrite)
description: supcon_loss (@ft-api ~35665, supervised contrastive loss) has TWO gaps: (1) CORRECTNESS — it extracts embedding values, normalizes + computes the [N,N] similarity matrix + the masked log-sum-exp loss all in plain f64, then rebuilds a requires_grad=false leaf -> SEVERS autograd. torch's SupCon loss IS differentiable; as-is the loss cannot train (defeating its purpose). (2) PERF — the [N,N] sim matrix is a naive O(N^2*D) parallel dot-product sweep (the code explicitly avoids GEMM to stay bit-exact). FIX: rewrite autograd-aware AND GEMM-routed: normalize embeddings via tape ops (div by L2 norm), sim = (emb_n @ emb_n^T)/temperature via tensor_matmul (GEMM), build the positive/negative masks from labels as constant (non-grad) tensors, then the masked log-sum-exp over a!=i (subtract row-max for stability, mask diagonal) and the positive-averaged loss — all through tape ops so gradient flows. Parity: tolerance vs the current forward (matmul reassociates the k-sum ~1e-12); torch-parity is tolerance anyway for a loss. Validation: forward matches current to ~1e-10 + finite-difference gradient check (this is the gate the severed version lacks). GEMM-routing the sim matrix is the same cdist/pdist win (fb6s/6sf6). Dedicated turn — the masked log-sum-exp composition needs care. Uncontended ft-api.
close_reason: Shipped commit ab1c6943: supcon_loss rewritten autograd-aware + GEMM-routed. Was severing autograd (forward-only, couldn't train) + naive O(N^2*D) sweep. Now: normalize + sim=(norm@norm^T)/temp via matmul + masked log-sum-exp + posmask numerator, all through the tape. Gradient FD-verified (supcon_loss_gradient_matches_finite_difference); forward still bit-exact; 2.11x faster (34.9->16.6ms N=512 D=128). All 1855 ft-api tests pass. Pushed main+master.

title: [parity-gap] torch.special.i0
description: torch.special.i0(input) computes modified Bessel function of first kind, order 0. See https://pytorch.org/docs/stable/special.html#torch.special.i0

title: conv_transpose2d groups= + dilation= (functional + nn ConvTranspose2d) — torch parity
description: ConvTranspose2d had output_padding but no groups/dilation; torch supports both (grouped/dilated deconv in GAN/U-Net/super-res decoders). Compose-in-ft-api (NO kernel change): dilation zero-stuffs the kernel spatial dims (same dilate_conv_weight, works since spatial dims are still last two of [Cin,Cout/g,kh,kw]; verified torch.allclose); groups = independent transposed convs over input-channel slices (narrow input dim1 + weight dim0=Cin, cat outputs). Added functional_conv_transpose2d_dilated(...,dilation,groups) + tensor alias; nn ConvTranspose2d::new_grouped (weight [Cin,Cout/groups,kh,kw]) + .dilation() builder + forward routing. Verified vs torch 2.12 (dilation=2, groups=2, plain identical, grad to all taps, module==functional). Follow-up: conv_transpose1d groups+dilation, conv3d.
close_reason: done

title: [perf][no-gaps][POLICY] Dense eigensolver gaps (eig 12-40x, eigh 11x) need a tolerance-parity decision for non-unique VECTOR outputs to unblock multishift-QR/D&C/two-stage-band
notes:
DECISIVE PRECEDENT (BoldFalcon 2026-06-10): the project ALREADY accepts tolerance parity for blocked dense factorizations that REASSOCIATE floating-point — so 'absolute bit-exact parity' is NOT how the dense-linalg core actually operates, and holding eig/svd REDUCTIONS to bit-exact while LU/Cholesky/QR get tolerance is an INCONSISTENCY, not a principled rule. Evidence in crates/ft-kernel-cpu/src/lib.rs:
- lu_factor_contiguous_f64 (~8348): blocked right-looking LU, trailing update via gemm::dgemm; comment 'only the trailing arithmetic reassociates ... a valid P·L·U=A to working precision'; validated by test lu_factor_reconstructs_pa_eq_lu with (recon-expected).abs() < 1e-9 (RECONSTRUCTION TOLERANCE, not to_bits).
- cholesky: blocked, test cholesky_blocked_matches_serial_and_reconstructs + prop_cholesky_reconstruction.
- qr: parallel apply, test qr_parallel_apply_reconstructs_and_orthonormal + prop_qr_reconstruction.
IMPLICATION: adopting the SAME reconstruction-tolerance standard for eig/eigh/svd VECTOR outputs (and ~1e-12 eigenvalues) is CONSISTENCY, not a risky new policy. This de-risks approving the real 3-10x swings: multishift-QR+AED for geev (the serial Francis-QR floor is ~95ms of eigvals_256, the dominant wall) and (if it ever beats unblocked) a blocked dsytrd for eigh. RECOMMENDATION to orchestrator: ratify 'dense eigensolver/SVD outputs are held to the SAME reconstruction+orthogonality+values-to-~1e-9 tolerance already used for LU/Cholesky/QR' — then multishift-QR becomes an ordinary [perf] bead. eig is Amdahl-capped at 1.84x by the Francis-QR eigenvalue floor even with perfect eigenvector parallelism, so multishift (which attacks that floor) is the ONLY path past ~1.84x for geev.
close_reason: POLICY RATIFIED by orchestrator 2026-06-10: tolerance parity for dense eigensolver/SVD vector outputs adopted (same reconstruction+orthogonality+1e-9 standard as LU/Cholesky/QR/Hessenberg/SVD). Work split into ordinary perf beads frankentorch-t0b4l (blocked dlatrd/dsytrd, first swing) and frankentorch-fql10 (multishift-QR+AED). No code change in this bead — it was a decision gate.

title: [perf][no-gaps] fql10-B: AED (LAPACK dlaqr2/3 aggressive early deflation) for geev — trailing nw-window Schur via eig_francis_schur (recursive), spike-vector deflation of negligibly-coupled eigenvalues, quasi-triangular reorder (dtrexc 1x1/2x2 swaps), back-transform window Z into h+q_acc. Undeflated window evals become next sweep's shifts. Deflates many evals per call -> big sweep-count cut on clustered/complex spectra. tolerance-parity (qgce4). Target: dominant geev speedup. Blocked by fql10-A.
close_reason: Rejected after repeated profile-backed AED passes: values-only suffix and whole-window AED preserved eig/eigvals goldens but failed final same-worker gates. Route deeper to frankentorch-npxbw direct small-bulge multishift QR with deterministic shift scaffolding; do not repeat threshold AED variants.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Profiling confirms AED/multishift (this bead -> npxbw) is the SOLE geev lever: eigvals (serial Francis QR) is ~75% of eig and the whole O(n^3) wall; the eigenvector machinery is already parallel and backsub parallelization REGRESSES (see l9xod note). Target the Francis QR sweep-count (AED deflation) + BLAS-3 sweeps (multishift), nothing else moves the needle.

title: ft-autograd/ft-api: tape-level F16/BF16 casts (tensor_half/bfloat16)
description: tensor_half() and tensor_bfloat16() returned UnsupportedDType because TensorTape::to_dtype only handled F32<->F64. The dense layer already supports float->F16/BF16 and the cast backward is identity (same as F32<->F64), so tape-level F16/BF16 casts are well-defined and differentiable. Implemented to_f16/to_bf16 on TensorTape (accept F32/F64 inputs, identity backward, new CastF16/CastBF16 ops), routed through to_dtype, and updated ft-api docs. Dangling tracking ref frankentorch-gx0p (never filed) removed.

title: ft-api: lu_factor_ex/cholesky_ex return wrong LAPACK info (constant 0/1, not the failing index)
description: Same LAPACK output-encoding bug class as ibfw. tensor_linalg_lu_factor_ex ALWAYS returned info=0 (even for singular matrices); tensor_linalg_cholesky_ex returned info=1 on ANY non-PD failure. torch.linalg.lu_factor_ex returns info=k = 1-indexed first zero U-diagonal (singular pivot), and torch.linalg.cholesky_ex returns info=k = 1-indexed order of the first non-PD leading minor (verified torch 2.12: singular 3x3 -> lu info 3; [[1,2],[2,1]] -> chol info 2; 3rd-minor-fail -> chol info 3). FIXED: lu_factor_ex scans the packed LU U-diagonal for the first exact-zero; cholesky_ex computes the failing minor via an unblocked column-Cholesky (potf2 shape) helper on the Err path. Golden test session_lu_cholesky_ex_info_matches_torch.
notes: EXTENDED 5c0187b2: inv_ex + solve_ex (delegates to inv_ex) also fixed — same singular-pivot info via LU U-diagonal scan. The LAPACK info-encoding sub-vein is now COMPLETE: lu_factor_ex, cholesky_ex, inv_ex, solve_ex all return torch-exact 1-indexed info. Test session_lu_cholesky_ex_info_matches_torch covers all four.
close_reason: SHIPPED 5b7b0dfa. lu_factor_ex scans U-diagonal for first exact-zero (info=k); cholesky_ex computes failing leading-minor order via unblocked column Cholesky on the Err path. Verified vs torch 2.12 (session_lu_cholesky_ex_info_matches_torch: lu singular->3, nonsing->0; chol non-PD->2/3, PD->0). ft-api 1920 pass.

title: ft-api: quantile lacks q-as-tensor (multiple quantiles in one call) — torch parity
description:
tensor_quantile/tensor_quantile_dim only accept a single scalar q. torch.quantile(input, q) accepts a 1-D q tensor and returns output with a NEW LEADING dim of length len(q) (output shape = q.shape ++ reduced_input_shape). Common usage e.g. quantile(x,[0.25,0.5,0.75]) in one call.

Added tensor_quantile_multi(input, qs, interpolation) [flat -> cat scalar [1] results -> shape [len(q)]] and tensor_quantile_dim_multi(input, qs, dim, keepdim, interpolation) [stack scalar-q results on new leading dim 0]. Both reuse the autograd-aware scalar path, so gradients flow.

Verified vs torch 2.12: flat [1,2,3,4] q=[.25,.5,.75]=[1.75,2.5,3.25]; [[1,2,3,4],[10,20,30,40]] q=[.25,.75] dim1 -> shape [2,2] [[1.75,17.5],[3.25,32.5]]; keepdim q=[.5] dim1 -> [1,2,1]; grad sum(q=[.25,.75]) over [1,2,3,4]=[0.25,0.75,0.75,0.25]. Goldens quantile_multi_q_torch_golden + quantile_multi_q_propagates_gradient. ft-api 1946/1946.
close_reason: Shipped tensor_quantile_multi + tensor_quantile_dim_multi (q-as-tensor: new leading q-axis). Reuses autograd-aware scalar path. Golden vs torch 2.12 (values + shapes + grad). ft-api 1946/1946.

title: [perf][no-gaps] cholesky_solve/cholesky_inverse multi-RHS: column-parallel at large n
description: cholesky_solve was serial multi-RHS (RHS-inner, 4 passes). cholesky_inverse solves L L^T X = I (n-RHS) so it's the SPD analog of inv. Applied the proven barrier-free column-parallel recipe (otbok): each RHS column is an independent 2-pass triangular solve; transpose to RHS-outer, par_chunks_mut, transpose back. Bit-exact (per-column order mirrors each pass's serial order; 10 cholesky tests pass with path force-enabled + 413 suite). Gated num_rhs>=8 && n*n*num_rhs>=1<<29 (HIGHER than lu_solve's 1<<26: same-worker ovh-a showed 512 pure-solve does NOT benefit (22.1->22.98ms, serial cache-resident) but 1024 does: 249.5->160.4ms = 1.56x). Score ~3.5.
close_reason: Shipped 8ac4e09d: column-parallel cholesky_solve (barrier-free per-column 2-pass solve), gated 1<<29. Same-worker ovh-a: cholesky_solve_1024_nrhs 249.5->160.4ms (1.56x); 512 stays serial (no benefit, no regression). Bit-exact (forced-path tests + 413 suite). Benefits cholesky_inverse / SPD multi-RHS solve at large n.

title: Fix ft-nn MultiLabelMarginLoss autograd via pair-wise broadcast [child of cq0b]
description: MultiLabelMarginLoss in ft-nn computes pair-wise margin between target and non-target classes per sample. Composable through outer-style broadcast (unsqueeze + sub) to build the (i, j) pair difference tensor, then mask by (target_mask outer non_target_mask), apply relu, sum, divide by C.
notes:
Fixed in this commit. Composed ft-nn MultiLabelMarginLoss through pair-wise broadcast (unsqueeze + expand + sub) → relu → masked-sum:
   x_i = unsqueeze(input, 2) → [batch, c, 1] expanded
   x_j = unsqueeze(input, 1) → [batch, 1, c] expanded
   pair_diff = x_i - x_j     → [batch, c, c]
   relu_margin = relu(1 - pair_diff)
   pair_mask = (target_mask_i outer non_target_mask_j)
   per_sample = sum_dim(sum_dim(relu_margin * pair_mask, 2), 1) / c
   reduce per Reduction.

The target masks are non-grad target-derived leaves; the rest of the chain is autograd-aware so gradients flow to input. ft-nn lib tests: 517.
