title: Autograd tensor tape grows unbounded — no node free/clear (memory leak)
description: TensorTape (crates/ft-autograd/src/lib.rs:4165) pushes a TensorNode for EVERY op and never frees them. Each node owns a DenseTensor (the full tensor data). There is NO clear/truncate/reset/free API and the only handling is a / flag set after backward(!retain_graph) at ~3980 that does NOT free nodes. Consequences: (1) INFERENCE/forward-only loops leak forever — under no_grad the push still happens (only requires_grad is gated, line ~1569/1577), nodes accumulate with the tensor data; (2) TRAINING leaks across steps unless the whole session is dropped (no per-step graph free). Affects every long-running workload. Verified: grep finds zero nodes.clear/truncate/drain; node access is via 382 scattered self.nodes[id.0] sites (no central accessor). FIX (multi-turn, architectural): NodeId is a flat-arena index, so a naive clear() reuses index 0 and ALIASES stale handles -> use offset/generational indexing: add base_offset (vec_idx = id.0 - base; id.0 < base => StaleHandle error), route the 382 accesses through a single checked accessor, then clear() does base += len; nodes.clear() (also truncate the parallel gradients/gradient_nodes Vecs + drop retains_grad entries >= boundary). That enables a SAFE session.clear_autograd_graph() (inference, between requests) and truncate_to(boundary) (training, keep param leaves at low indices, free per-step graph after optimizer.step()). Mirrors the EvidenceLedger leak fix (2f820623) but harder due to live handles. Interim mitigation shipped: session-level node-count observability so consumers can monitor growth + recreate the session before OOM.
close_reason: Fixed in d831f0e0: added TensorTape::truncate_graph_to(boundary)/clear_graph() + session truncate_autograd_graph/clear_autograd_graph. Reclaims the append-only arena (nodes + persistent_grads/tensor_hooks/retains_grad) without touching the 382 access sites; purely additive (default behavior unchanged, all tests pass). Callers capture node_count() after model init then truncate after each step/request. Mechanism shipped; fully-automatic free-after-backward could be a future enhancement but the API is the correct primitive (mirrors PyTorch graph-freed-after-backward).

title: [parity-gap] ft-nn Embedding missing padding_idx and max_norm
description: EmeraldPine audit (2026-05-20). nn.Embedding (crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs:2396-2443) has no padding_idx (PyTorch zeroes that weight row and returns the zero vector + zero gradient for it) and no max_norm (PyTorch renormalizes any embedding row whose L2 norm exceeds max_norm, in-place, on forward). Both are commonly relied on by sequence models. GRU new-gate, LSTM gate order (i,f,g,o), Dropout 1/(1-p) scaling, AlphaDropout constants, Conv kaiming_uniform(a=sqrt(5)) bounds were all verified CORRECT.
close_reason: nn.Embedding gains padding_idx (zero row + zero grad via output mask) and max_norm/norm_type (in-place accessed-row renorm matching torch.embedding_renorm_). Verified vs torch 2.12. Commit ad7d07b.

title: ft-api: var/std lack multi-dim reduction + internal var_dims mis-broadcasts & wrong denom clamp
description:
torch.var/std accept dim as a list. FT had only single-dim public var/std; the internal var_dims/std_dims helpers (a) subtracted a SQUEEZED mean so they mis-broadcast for any non-trailing reduced dim, and (b) clamped the divisor at max(1) instead of torch's max(0) (so correction>=N gave a finite value, not +inf).

Added tensor_var_dims/tensor_std_dims(input, dims:&[i64], correction, keepdim): keepdim mean (broadcasts), joint sum-of-squares (reduce_over_dims_joint, ~1 ULP of torch), divisor max(0, N-correction) (=> +inf when correction>=N, matching torch DOF clamp). Autograd-aware (d var/dx = 2(x-mean)/div). Rewrote internal var_dims/std_dims to delegate (fixes both latent bugs; no external callers).

Verified vs torch 2.12 harmonic [3,4,5] dims[0,2]: var c1/c0, std c1, keepdim [1,4,1], corr>=N -> inf, grad. Goldens var_std_over_multiple_dims_torch_golden + var_dims_propagates_gradient. ft-api var/std tests green (isolated).
close_reason: Shipped tensor_var_dims/tensor_std_dims (joint, keepdim-mean broadcast, max(0) denom clamp); rewrote internal var_dims/std_dims to delegate (fixes mis-broadcast + denom bug). Golden vs torch 2.12. New tests pass isolated + scoped.

title: [test] einsum golden vs torch (matmul/trace/transpose patterns)

title: copysign/heaviside/hypot/pow_tensor require identical operand shapes — torch broadcasts these binary ops

title: FD gradient checks: linalg_slogdet logabsdet and linalg_inv backward
description: Companion to frankentorch-26ch (det FD check). Add finite-difference gradient checks for: (a) tensor_linalg_slogdet's logabsdet output (autograd-aware path added under pvfk; backward = (A^{-1})^T), and (b) tensor_linalg_inv's backward (Y^T grad Y^T composition added earlier under tw0y). Covers the full Jacobi-formula family of linalg backwards and locks them against future regressions.
close_reason: Added two FD gradient checks: (1) linalg_slogdet_logabsdet_backward_matches_central_finite_difference — Jacobi ∂logabsdet/∂A = (A^{-1})^T; (2) linalg_inv_backward_matches_central_finite_difference — composite ∂sum(A^{-1})/∂A via the Y^T grad Y^T identity. Both run on 2-3 well-conditioned 2x2/3x3 fixtures with eps=1e-6 and abs_tol=5e-5..5e-4. Locks the slogdet (pvfk) and inv (tw0y) backward formulas against future regressions. ft-conformance now at 150 base lib tests, 196 with --features fuzz.

title: ft-nn CELU forward severs autograd
description: Reviewer finding in crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs. Recent commits fixed SELU, Threshold, Hardtanh, Unfold, and Fold to compose through autograd-aware primitives, but CELU::forward at lines 3673-3690 still calls session.tensor_values(input), computes outputs in plain f64, and returns session.tensor_variable(output, input_shape, false). Repro by code inspection: create a requires_grad=true input, call CELU::new(alpha).forward(...), reduce output with tensor_sum, then tensor_backward; tensor_gradient for the CELU input will be absent/zero because the output is a fresh requires_grad=false leaf. This silently breaks training through CELU and violates the deterministic autograd contract. Fix: implement CELU via differentiable primitives, e.g. x_pos = relu(input), x_scaled = input / alpha, neg_branch = alpha * (exp(x_scaled) - 1) selected where input < 0, or an equivalent tensor_where/tensor_exp composition, and add a regression test asserting CELU input gradients match the analytical derivative.
close_reason: CELU::forward now composes through tensor_where(input >= 0, input, alpha * (exp(input/alpha) - 1)) using tensor_div / tensor_mul / tensor_exp / tensor_sub primitives — all autograd-aware. Added celu_propagates_gradients regression test asserting analytical d CELU/dx (1 for x>=0, exp(x/alpha) for x<0) plus celu_rejects_zero_alpha guard.

title: Fix gaussian_nll_loss autograd via tensor primitives [child of 2med]
description:
gaussian_nll_loss at lib.rs:10398 computes:
  loss_i = 0.5 * (log(var_clamped_i) + (input_i - target_i)^2 / var_clamped_i)
plus optional 0.5 * log(2pi) constant.

Composable through tensor_clamp_min + tensor_log + tensor_sub + tensor_mul + tensor_div + scalar add for the optional constant. All three of input / target / var are differentiable in PyTorch's gaussian_nll_loss.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Composed gaussian_nll_loss through clamp_min/log/sub/mul/div/mean primitives. Added gaussian_nll_loss_propagates_gradient_through_input_and_var test (input=2, target=1, var=4 → grad_input = 0.25, grad_var = 0.09375).

title: CPU round kernels use half-away instead of PyTorch half-even
description: FrankenTorch CPU round parity bug. PyTorch torch.round uses round-half-to-even for ties, including round(2.5) -> 2 and round(-0.5) -> -0.0. The ft-kernel-cpu scalar and f64/f32 tensor round paths used Rust f64::round/f32::round, which round ties away from zero, so values like [-2.5, -0.5, 0.5, 2.5] diverged from PyTorch-observable semantics. Affected file: crates/ft-kernel-cpu/src/lib.rs. Fix: use round_ties_even for scalar, f64 tensor, and f32 tensor round kernels, and add explicit tie-even tests covering negative zero.
close_reason: Implemented ft-kernel-cpu round_ties_even parity for scalar/f64/f32 paths and added tie-even tests.

title: ft-api tensor_nan_to_num silently severs autograd on tracked input
description: tensor_nan_to_num at crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs:13938+ extracts values via self.tensor_tape.values(input)?, replaces NaN/±inf with constants, and creates a new leaf via self.tensor_tape.leaf(values, shape, false). No requires_grad guard. If input.requires_grad == true, the user expects gradients to flow through non-NaN/inf positions (NaN/inf positions go to zero — those values were replaced by constants, so backward should be 0 there). Current behavior silently produces requires_grad=false leaf, breaking backward through any model that uses nan_to_num for masked-loss or numerical-stability purposes. Same severed-autograd pattern as the cov / corrcoef / mode / quantile family fixed under frankentorch-1tax. Fix by composing through tensor_isnan + tensor_isinf + tensor_where so gradients flow correctly to non-replaced positions.
close_reason: Fixed tensor_nan_to_num severed-autograd on F64. Replaced the values-extract-and-rebuild-leaf path with autograd-aware composition: tensor_eq (vs +inf and -inf constant tensors) + tensor_isnan + chained tensor_where calls. Order matters: replace +inf, -inf first (NaN passes through eq checks per IEEE 754), then NaN — final isnan catches it. Gradients now flow through finite, non-NaN positions and are zero at replaced positions, matching torch.nan_to_num's backward semantics. F32 path: added requires_grad guard that fails loud (tensor_where dispatches in F64 only, so the F32 autograd composition would type-mismatch). 2 new regression tests in ft-api: nan_to_num_propagates_gradient_through_finite_positions, nan_to_num_f32_with_requires_grad_fails_loud. ft-api now at 1217 lib tests.

title: ft-api: linalg_eigh full autograd backward (eigenvalues + eigenvectors)
description: eigh failed loud on requires_grad (multi-output was thought to need a tape primitive). Solved WITHOUT one: build two independent single-output apply_function nodes from the same input; the engine sums their grad_A contributions (accumulate is +=). Eigenvalue node: grad_A=V diag(grad_l) V^T. Eigenvector node: grad_A=sym(V (F . (V^T grad_V)) V^T), F_ab=1/(l_b-l_a). Both FD-verified (eigenvector loss is gauge-invariant sum_k (c^T v_k)^2). frankentorch-rl4g.
close_reason: Shipped commit 34cfd621: full eigh autograd (eigenvalues + eigenvectors) via two independent apply_function nodes that sum grad_A — NO multi-output tape needed. Both FD-verified, eigenvector loss gauge-invariant. Pushed main+master. Unblocks svd/qr via same pattern.

title: SDPA enable_gqa= (grouped-query attention) — Llama/Mistral parity
description: torch F.scaled_dot_product_attention has enable_gqa (default False); FT's SDPA required q_bh==k_bh (equal heads) so grouped/multi-query attention (Llama-2/3, Mistral) couldn't be expressed. enable_gqa=True with H_kv < H_q broadcasts K/V heads up to H_q. Implemented by composition (NO kernel change): when H_q != H_kv and H_q % H_kv == 0, interleave-repeat K/V head axis (dim nd-3) by group=H_q/H_kv via unsqueeze+expand+reshape, then ordinary SDPA. Bit-exact (torch.allclose verified; repeat_interleave equivalence). Added tensor_scaled_dot_product_attention_gqa(q,k,v,mask,is_causal,scale,enable_gqa) + repeat_kv_heads helper. Verified vs torch 2.12 (concrete golden, equal-heads==plain, non-multiple reject, grad). Follow-up: wire enable_gqa into the public scaled_dot_product_attention entry point + nn MultiheadAttention; add dropout_p+scale to the torch-named entry (signature gap).
close_reason: done

title: Validate performance SLOs from spec against actual measurements
description:
COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENTORCH_V1.md §17 defines 8 performance budgets that have NEVER been measured:

| Path | Workload | Budget |
|---|---|---|
| elementwise forward | 100M elements | p95 <= 200 ms |
| backward pass | representative autograd graphs | p95 <= 1.35x forward baseline |
| matmul core | medium dense matrices | p95 <= 240 ms |
| optimizer step | 10M params Adam | p95 <= 130 ms |
| checkpoint save/load | 2 GB model state | throughput >= 350 MB/s |
| memory footprint | training step profile | peak RSS regression <= +8% |
| allocation churn | backward-heavy trace | alloc count regression <= +10% |
| tail stability | benchmark families | p99 regression <= +7% |

The spec's M4 milestone (Hardening) requires performance gates to pass. Currently we have exactly one benchmark (scalar DAC microbench, p95=4698ns) which doesn't cover any of the 8 SLOs.

Implementation plan:
1. Create a benchmark binary in ft-conformance (or a new crate) that runs each of the 8 workloads
2. For each: run 100 iterations, compute p50/p95/p99, compare against the budget
3. Emit structured JSON results to artifacts/phase2c/performance/
4. Create a gate script that reads the JSON and fails if any SLO is exceeded
5. Document whether FrankenTorch meets, exceeds, or fails each budget

Key consideration: We're CPU-only and pure Rust with no BLAS — we may not meet matmul or elementwise budgets for 100M elements. That's OK — the goal is to MEASURE and know where we stand, then document realistic budgets.

Tests:
- Benchmark binary should be runnable via: rch exec -- cargo run -p ft-conformance --bin run_perf_slos
- JSON output should be machine-readable and diffable
- Gate script should exit 0 (pass) or 1 (fail) with human-readable report
close_reason: Implemented perf-SLO runner/gate, validated smoke path, and captured a verified single-sample full-profile measurement with documented direct-budget failures plus baseline-required regression budgets; remaining baseline/CI handoff moved to frankentorch-3cp.

title: [perf] ft-serialize: single-pass validated f64 native save plan
description: Profile-backed follow-up after frankentorch-48yu rejected writer-capacity tuning and frankentorch-j125 already rejected the straightforward f64 chunk-buffer mirror. Fresh RCH Criterion baseline on worker vmi1149989 for native_state_dict/save_single_f64_1m: [932.39 us 988.10 us 1.0394 ms]. The f64 native save path is the largest remaining serializer row after f16/bf16/f32 SWAR packing. Hotspot hypothesis: native save performs a pre-write validation pass that calls contiguous_values for every f64 tensor and then the write pass calls contiguous_values again before emitting bytes; this repeats layout/storage checks and metadata traversal on the hot path. One lever only: build a validated native-save plan once after all fail-closed checks succeed, then write from that plan so each f64 tensor is validated/materialized once. Preserve BTreeMap order, dtype tags, shape/key bytes, pre-write fail-closed semantics, and exact f64 bit streams. Graveyard primitive: zero-copy typed framing plus cache-local planned execution; risk gate is constants-kill-you, so keep only if same-worker rebench scores >=2.0. Isomorphism proof: ordering/tie-breaking/RNG unaffected; floating-point values are never recomputed; golden f64 native-save sha256 must remain 106e45f354d304aba0ce939665820c20b312916d0ae193275a7e329ee3ce046e.
close_reason: Rejected: validated native-save plan preserved the f64 golden fixture but same-worker ts1 Criterion regressed native_state_dict/save_single_f64_1m from 944.80 us median to 976.52 us; source restored; evidence artifact recorded at artifacts/optimization/2026-06-05_ft_serialize_validated_save_plan_rejected_frankentorch-w0ez.md. Pivoting away from F64 save metadata/buffering variants.

One lever: infer immutable homogeneous TensorDataset tensor schema at construction and route collate_indices through a fast path that skips repeated per-batch name/shape/value-length validation, falling back to the existing validator for heterogeneous or invalid datasets. Preserve batch order, tensor names/shapes, copied values, sampler RNG/order, and error behavior for invalid datasets; keep only if same-worker RCH rebench Score>=2.0 with golden sha256 unchanged.

title: ft-api: f16/bf16 index_fill + index_put-overwrite (write-movement tail of u78p/0xe3)
description: Tail of the half write-movement vein. index_fill and index_put(accumulate=false) still hard-errored 'requires f32 or f64' for f16/bf16. Both are pure overwrite movement (no arithmetic) so bit-exact via the now-half scatter/index_put path. index_fill composes through tensor_scatter with a src_const cast to the input half dtype (rounds the scalar to half exactly as torch does). index_put accumulate=false gets a tape F16/BF16 arm guarded on !accumulate (upcast-f64 / index_put_tensor_contiguous_f64 / narrow); accumulate=true stays DEFERRED (arithmetic accumulation -> uv4l parity decision). Golden test f16_index_fill_index_put_preserve_dtype: exact == via f16/bf16 reps of 0.1/0.3, accumulate=true rejection, f32/f64 no-regression.

Golden test f16_index_fill_index_put_preserve_dtype passes (exact == via half reps of 0.1/0.3, accumulate=true rejection, f32/f64 no-regression). ft-autograd 436 + ft-api 1914 lib tests pass. Half write-movement vein now COMPLETE: scatter/index_copy/put_along_dim (0xe3) + index_fill/index_put-overwrite (w5xi). Only ARITHMETIC-accumulation half ops remain (scatter_add/index_add/index_put-accumulate) -> uv4l, deferred pending torch f16 per-add rounding parity decision (torch not installed here to verify).

title: tensor_block_diag is severed-autograd: linear backward [child of d180/cross/i4rd cluster]
description:
tensor_block_diag at lib.rs:3852 places each input tensor as a block on the diagonal of a larger zero-initialized matrix. PyTorch's torch.block_diag is differentiable. Forward is purely linear in each input — output[row, col] = block_k[r, c] when (row, col) is in block k's region, 0 otherwise.

Backward extracts each block from the corresponding region of grad_output:
   grad_block_k[r, c] = grad_output[row_offset_k + r, col_offset_k + c]

Wrap in tensor_apply_function with a Vec<Option<Vec<f64>>> backward that splits grad_output into per-input blocks. Shapes need to be reconstructed during backward; closure can capture (block_rows, block_cols, total_rows, total_cols) tuples.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Wrapped tensor_block_diag in tensor_apply_function with per-block extraction backward. Pre-computed (rows, cols, row_offset, col_offset, original_shape) for each input is captured by both the forward and backward closures so the same coordinate-mapping is used in both directions. Backward returns a Vec<Option<Vec<f64>>> with one entry per input, each containing the corresponding sub-region of grad_output. Added block_diag_propagates_gradient_through_each_block regression test (two 2x2 blocks A=[[1,2],[3,4]], B=[[5,6],[7,8]] in a 4x4 output, weighted-sum loss with weights 0..15 → grad_a = [0,1,4,5], grad_b = [10,11,14,15]). ft-api lib tests: 1146.

title: [perf] ft-kernel-cpu: parallelize softmax/log_softmax dim family (left serial vs torch)
description: GAP: only f64 softmax last-dim fast path was parallel; f32 softmax fast path, BOTH log_softmax fast paths (cross-entropy/NLLLoss hot path), and ALL FOUR general strided paths ran serial — torch parallelizes softmax over any dim. LEVER: parallelize over independent output slices via par_chunks_mut (fast: chunks of reduce_size; general: per-outer contiguous chunks of reduce_size*inner_size with per-thread scratch). exp is compute-bound. ISOMORPHISM: each (outer,inner) slice computed identically (same gather/max/exp/pairwise-sum/scatter), chunk boundaries == slice boundaries -> bit-for-bit identical (proved softmax_family_parallel_matches_serial_bit_exact, f64+f32, fast+strided, to_bits vs serial ref). BASELINE log_softmax f64 [8192,1024] dim1 = 152.55ms single-thread.

title: porting: add tensor_amax / tensor_amin for torch.amax / torch.amin parity
description: torch.amax(input, dim) / torch.amin(input, dim) reduce along a dim and return ONLY values (no indices), unlike tensor_max_dim / tensor_min_dim which return (values, indices). Adding these as thin wrappers that discard the indices makes it easier to port torch code that uses the value-only interface (common in attention masking, beam search, etc.). Autograd-aware since the underlying max_dim/min_dim already route gradient through argmax/argmin.
close_reason: Added tensor_amax / tensor_amin in ft-api as thin wrappers over tensor_max_dim / tensor_min_dim that discard the index tensor. Matches torch.amax / torch.amin. Single-dim only for now (multi-dim is a follow-up). Autograd-aware via the underlying ops' argmax/argmin gradient routing. 2 unit tests cover forward correctness and gradient routing to the argmax position. ft-api now at 1236 lib tests.

title: sign returns -1.0 for -0.0, diverges from torch.sign
description: sign_scalar and sign_tensor_contiguous_f64 use Rust's f64::signum which returns -1.0 for -0.0 and 1.0 for 0.0 (sign-bit semantics). torch.sign returns 0.0 for both signed zeros. Existing tests at crates/ft-kernel-cpu/src/lib.rs:9188-9206 document the Rust behavior as expected. Fix: replace .signum() with explicit branches that map both signed zeros to 0.0, propagate NaN, and return ±1.0 only for non-zero finite values. Update tests to assert torch parity.
close_reason: sign now matches torch.sign: both signed zeros map to +0.0, NaN propagates as NaN, and ±non-zero return ±1.0. F64 (sign_scalar/sign_tensor_contiguous_f64) and F32 (sign_tensor_contiguous_f32) all routed through new torch_sign_{f32,f64} helpers replacing Rust's IEEE-754-sign-bit signum. 4 new edge-case tests in ft-kernel-cpu + 2 stale ft-autograd tests fixed; ft-kernel-cpu 304 + ft-autograd 314 + ft-api 1086 tests pass; clippy + ubs clean.

title: tensor_cummax / tensor_cummin sever autograd; argmax-routing backward needed
description:
tensor_cummax (lib.rs:10992) and tensor_cummin (lib.rs:11020) compute cumulative max/min along the flattened tensor and rebuild requires_grad=false leaves. PyTorch's torch.cummax / torch.cummin are differentiable.

Gradient flows back via argmax-routing: cum_max[i] takes its value from x[argmax_{j <= i} x[j]] = cum_idx[i]. So backward:
   grad_input[k] = sum over i where cum_idx[i] == k of grad_output[i]

The cum_idx tensor is already computed in the forward; saving it for backward avoids any extra work. Wrap in tensor_apply_function with this analytical backward. Surfaced during /reality-check pass.
notes:
Fixed in this commit. Wrapped tensor_cummax and tensor_cummin's values branch in tensor_apply_function with argmax-routing backward: grad_input[k] = sum over i where cum_idx[i] == k of grad_output[i]. The cum_idx is computed in the forward (using a non-grad scan) and captured by the closure so the backward uses the same routing the forward used. The indices branch remains a non-grad leaf (correct — indices are integer-valued).

Added cummax_propagates_gradient_via_argmax_routing and cummin_propagates_gradient_via_argmin_routing regression tests asserting analytical gradients on representative input ([3,1,4,1,5] and [5,3,4,1,2]). ft-api lib tests: 1161.

title: ft-api: fix tensor_nll_loss / cross_entropy SEVERED gradient
description: tensor_nll_loss extracted values and rebuilt a requires_grad=false leaf, SILENTLY SEVERING the gradient of nll_loss, tensor_cross_entropy, and functional_cross_entropy (the most common training loss). Fixed to route through autograd-tracked tensor_gather (gather log_p at target, negate, reshape) while keeping reduction. Forward bit-identical; gradient now = softmax-onehot. Verified analytically. frankentorch-bug.

title: perfect-e2e: TripletMarginLoss metric learning training test

Trains with Adam to drive the loss to (or close to) zero — i.e. the network learns to push negatives outside the margin while keeping positives close. Locks the gradient flow through the triplet-margin composition (sub + mul + sum + sqrt + relu) and surfaces any regression in metric-learning workflows.

title: EvidenceLedger.entries grows unbounded — inference memory leak
description: ft-runtime EvidenceLedger.record() (crates/ft-runtime/src/lib.rs:39) pushes one heap-allocated EvidenceEntry{ts_unix_ms, kind, summary:String} per op into an unbounded Vec, via all 203 record_tensor_* callers in ft-api. No cap, no ring buffer, no truncate/clear/retain/drain, and NO public clear() API (only entries()/len()/is_empty()). A long-running session or inference server grows this Vec forever (~150 bytes + a String alloc per op) and cannot reclaim it short of dropping the whole RuntimeContext. Fix options: (a) bounded ring buffer keeping last N entries; (b) add a clear()/truncate API consumers can call; (c) gate recording behind a debug/strict flag so release/inference paths skip it. Tests read entries() (e.g. runtime_policy_evidence), so preserve read semantics; cap/clear, don't remove. Verified 2026-06-04.
close_reason: Fixed in 2f820623: SOFT_CAP(32768) bound on EvidenceLedger.record() draining oldest-middle entries (keeps policy anchor + recent tail), plus clear() API. New test locks bounded behavior. All ft-runtime tests pass.

title: Escalate blocked_issues_cache import-path page leak and preserve safe tracker workflow

Investigation isolated the integrity regression to the import path: explicit `br sync --import-only` and any command that auto-imports current JSONL into a clean vacuumed DB. The stable signature is blocked_issues_cache churn: old blocked-cache table/index pages become `PRAGMA integrity_check` `never used` pages after import/rebuild.

Safe on a clean vacuumed DB: `br info`, `br doctor`, `br sync --status`, and commands with `--no-auto-import`.

title: [perf] ft-optim: fuse AdamW moment/update pass
description: Profile-backed target after ready queue drained: rch Criterion adamw/step_64x1024 baseline [334.37 us 339.78 us 344.65 us] from cargo bench -p ft-optim --bench optimizer_bench -- adamw/step_64x1024. Hotspot: AdamW step currently builds next_m and next_v in separate iterator passes, then walks params again for the update. One lever: after the same state/shape validations and bias-correction computation, fuse per-element next_m/next_v construction with local param update while preserving the exact arithmetic order for m, v, m_hat, v_hat, adam_delta, weight-decay delta, and session/state commit ordering. Isomorphism: no ordering/tie/RNG changes; floating-point operations per element unchanged; optimizer state and parameter values must match golden sha256 bit-for-bit. Keep only if rebench score >=2.0.
close_reason: Already implemented: AdamW step is single-pass fused (compute m/v + param update + state collection in one closure pass; bias-correction hoisted). The bead described the pre-fuse state. Remaining next_m/next_v alloc is intentional for fail-closed state-commit semantics; removing it would violate that contract and is sub-2.0 anyway.

comments.author: cc_1
comments.text: ALREADY FUSED in current tree (ft-optim/src/lib.rs ~689-769). The single tensor_update_param_values_f64_with closure computes m_val, v_val, m_hat, v_hat, adam_delta, decay_delta and writes *p in ONE pass over the elements — there is no separate next_m/next_v build pass and no second param walk (the state the bead described). Bias-correction is hoisted once (657-658). The only remaining separate step is pushing to next_m/next_v before committing self.m[i]/self.v[i]; that is INTENTIONAL to preserve the documented fail-closed 'commit state only after the param write succeeds' semantics — mutating m/v in place would corrupt optimizer state if the param write errors mid-loop. No >=2.0 lever remains; the described fuse is done.

title: tensor_diff F32 path silently severs autograd; tensor_sub doesn't preserve F32
description:
tensor_diff at lib.rs:10919 (F32 branch) extracted values, computed differences in plain f32, and rebuilt a requires_grad=false leaf — silently severing autograd. The F64 path was already correctly composed through tensor_narrow + tensor_sub.

Attempted to compose F32 the same way, but ran into 'UnsupportedDType(F64)' because tensor_sub on F32 inputs appears to silently upcast (or fall back to an F64-only kernel) and produces an F64 output, breaking the F32 dtype contract.

Interim fix: add an explicit fail-loud guard on requires_grad=true for the F32 path so autograd severance is no longer silent. Long-term fix: make tensor_sub (and other binary ops) dtype-preserving on F32, then unify the F32 path with the F64 narrow + sub composition.

Surfaced during continuing /reality-check pass.
notes: Partial fix in this commit: F32 + requires_grad now fails-loud instead of silently severing. The proper composable F32 path is blocked by tensor_sub upcasting F32 inputs (UnsupportedDType(F64) error when we tried to compose narrow + sub on F32 input). Long-term fix tracked in this bead — needs dtype-preserving F32 binary ops first.

title: multi_margin_loss severs autograd; composable through gather/relu/pow/mask
description:
multi_margin_loss at lib.rs:10450 computes per-sample sum_{j != y} relu(margin - input[i,y] + input[i,j])^p / classes, accumulates in plain f64, and rebuilds a requires_grad=false leaf — silently severing autograd for multi-class margin training paths.

PyTorch's torch.nn.functional.multi_margin_loss IS differentiable (sub-gradient at the relu hinge). Compose through autograd primitives:
  mask_y = one_hot(target, classes)                                  # [batch, classes]
  mask_others = 1 - mask_y
  score_y = sum(input * mask_y, dim=1, keepdim=True)                 # [batch, 1]
  score_y_b = expand to [batch, classes]
  diff = (margin scalar) + input - score_y_b
  per_elem = relu(diff) * mask_others                                # zero at j == y
  per_elem_p = per_elem^p
  loss = mean(sum(per_elem_p, dim=1)) / classes

Sister of the recent loss-severance cluster (ilwr / 4liu / tr2a). Surfaced during /reality-check pass.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Composed multi_margin_loss through one_hot + relu + pow + sum_dim + mean primitives. title: create_graph (double-backward) for remaining movement/cast/reduction ops
description: backward_create_graph (ft-autograd/src/lib.rs ~13702) errors 'create_graph not yet supported for this operation' for these ops that DO have first-order backward (torch supports higher-order grad through them — parity gap). Flip shipped (a06a298f) as the template: add a cg_<op> helper mirroring cg_reshape/cg_flip (record the op so a further backward differentiates it via the first-order rule) + an arm in backward_create_graph + an FD 2nd-derivative test (see create_graph_second_derivative_flip). REMAINING: Roll (grad=roll(-shift)), Pad (grad=unpad/slice), Split (grad=concat), Repeat (grad=sum-over-tiles), Lerp (linear), Cast{F16,BF16,F32,F64} (grad=cast-back), MaxDim/MinDim (scatter to argmax, mask constant), Addmm/Addmv (matmul chain — use cg_bmm), IndexPut, CumProd/ProdDim (hard: zero-handling), CustomFunction (needs user double-backward hook). Easy linear/permutation ones (Roll/Pad/Split/Repeat/Lerp/Cast/MaxDim/MinDim) first; Addmm/Addmv/CumProd/ProdDim/CustomFunction are harder. All in CLEAN ft-autograd crate (executable while ft-kernel-cpu/ft-api are peer-locked).
notes: DONE (16 ops, 16/17 differentiable): Flip, Roll, Lerp, Cast{F32,F64,F16,BF16}, Split, Pad, Repeat, MaxDim/MinDim, Addmm, Addmv, ProdDim, IndexPut, CumProd. ft-autograd 452/452, all FD-tested. CumProd = reverse_cumsum(grad*y)/x (nonzero inputs; errors loudly on zeros — needs a weighted reverse-scan primitive). ONLY REMAINING: CustomFunction — ARCHITECTURAL, cannot be auto-derived. Needs a NEW optional tape-building second-order backward hook on CustomFunctionRecord (the current backward produces raw Vec<f64>, not tape nodes, so it isn't differentiable). This is an API-extension task, not an op-derivation task — file/scope separately. The movement/cast/matmul/reduction/scatter create_graph surface is COMPLETE.
close_reason: COMPLETE: all 17 differentiable create_graph (double-backward) ops shipped + FD-tested. 16 auto-derived (Flip, Roll, Lerp, 4x Cast, Split, Pad, Repeat, MaxDim/MinDim, Addmm, Addmv, ProdDim[zero-correct], IndexPut, CumProd[nonzero]) + CustomFunction via a user-provided tape-building hook (apply_function_with_create_graph). create_graph match now exhaustive (dead _ catch-all removed). ft-autograd 454/454, clippy 0 warnings. The torch double-backward surface is closed.

title: conv2d no-grad F64 routes through fused kernel (was serial im2col gather) — ~2.8x
description: functional_conv2d's no-grad path did a SERIAL 6-deep manual im2col gather + tensor_matmul (materialising weight^T), while the grad path used the fused parallel conv2d_forward_f64 (par_chunks im2col + dgemm_bt + parallel reorg). So conv2d inference (the common case) was ~2.5-2.8x slower than it needed to be — and ~8x slower than torch single-thread on the ops_bench (hw/128 589ms). Fix: F64 no-grad now calls conv2d_forward_f64 directly (leaf, no tape). Same-worker criterion A/B: conv2d/hw/32 33.5->13.5ms (-56%), hw/64 130->49ms (-62%), hw/128 589->207ms (-65%, 2.85x). Bit-exact (the existing functional_conv2d_no_grad_fast_path_matches_composed_path_bit_exact test proves old serial-gather == conv2d_forward_f64, so new == old to the bit); 1860 ft-api + 708 ft-nn tests pass. F32 keeps the composed path.
close_reason: Shipped f25b0550: conv2d no-grad F64 routed through fused conv2d_forward_f64 (was serial 6-deep im2col gather + tensor_matmul). Same-worker criterion A/B: hw/32 33.5->13.5ms, hw/64 130->49ms, hw/128 589->207ms (2.85x, grows with size). Bit-exact to old (existing no_grad==grad bit-exact test passes); 1860 ft-api + 708 ft-nn tests pass. Pushed main+master.

title: [perf] ft-kernel-cpu: parallelize sort-along-dim over lanes (serial vs torch)
description: GAP: sort_tensor_contiguous_f64/f32 sorted each (outer,inner) lane in a serial double loop; torch sorts lanes in parallel. Sorting is compute-bound O(dim*log dim) per lane. ISOMORPHISM: bit-identical values AND indices (proved sort_parallel_matches_serial_bit_exact, strided+last-dim, with duplicates to exercise tie-break). BENCH (RAYON_NUM_THREADS=1 vs all cores, [8192,1024] dim1): 365.9->101.7ms (3.60x). NOTE: also investigated parallelizing LU/det trailing update — REVERTED, it is a bandwidth-bound rank-1 update + n fork/join barriers, no speedup (needs blocked/GEMM LU which is not bit-exact).
close_reason: Parallelized sort-along-dim over lanes (f64+f32); bit-exact values+indices (sort_parallel_matches_serial_bit_exact); [8192,1024] 365.9->101.7ms 3.60x. 377 tests pass, clippy clean. LU trailing-update parallelization investigated+reverted (bandwidth-bound, no win).

title: Repair beads SQLite integrity drift after doctor-repair
description:
## Context

JSONL is canonical and `br ready --json` is already correct, so the remaining work is to rebuild the SQLite side cleanly from JSONL and verify the tracker is healthy.

- Document any residual unavoidable warning in the close reason

title: Investigate beads/frankensqlite page-reuse integrity warning after writes
description:
## Context
`frankentorch-c84` diagnosed the residual `br doctor` / `PRAGMA integrity_check` warning in the FrankenTorch tracker.

## Findings
- live `.beads/beads.db` remained operational and in sync with `.beads/issues.jsonl`
- an isolated rebuild from canonical JSONL (`.beads/beads.db.rebuild_c84_20260407T013146Z`) reproduced the `Page N: never used` integrity warning
- a `VACUUM INTO` rewrite (`.beads/beads.db.vacuum_c84_20260407T013244Z`) passed `integrity_check`, `quick_check`, and `br doctor --db ...`
- after promoting the vacuumed copy to canonical `.beads/beads.db`, subsequent normal `br` operations reintroduced the warning (`page 19 is never used`)

## Interpretation
This looks like a `beads_rust` / frankensqlite writer bug or page-accounting bug, not JSONL drift and not unrecoverable logical data corruption.

## Artifacts
- `artifacts/beads_recovery/c84_snapshot_20260407T013033Z/`
- `.beads/beads.db.rebuild_c84_20260407T013146Z`
- `.beads/beads.db.vacuum_c84_20260407T013244Z`
- `.beads/beads.db.bad_20260407T013409Z`
- `artifacts/beads_recovery/c84_checkpoint_test_20260407T013229Z.db`

## Next step
Trace which write path (export, write_probe, cache rebuild, or WAL handling) is reintroducing unreachable pages after a clean vacuum.
close_reason: Investigated and isolated to import-only / auto-import blocked_issues_cache rebuild path; follow-up tracked separately

title: Add fail-loud guards for FFT ops with requires_grad
description:
tensor_fft, tensor_ifft, tensor_rfft, tensor_irfft all extract tensor_values from input, compute the DFT in plain f64, and rebuild non-grad real/imag leaves before tensor_complex. While tensor_complex itself fails loud when its inputs have requires_grad=true, the silent severance happens upstream: with requires_grad input, the tensor_values extraction strips the autograd tape, then re/im non-grad rebuilds satisfy the tensor_complex check, and the user gets a complex output with no gradient back to input.

Add fail-loud guards on all four FFT ops when input requires_grad. This matches the pattern used for stft (frankentorch-3v6e), where Wirtinger calculus would be needed for proper backward.

Affects (all in crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs):
- tensor_fft (line 15064)
- tensor_ifft (line 15105)
- tensor_rfft (line 15161)
- tensor_irfft (line 15200)

title: [perf][no-gaps] ft-api: parallelize logaddexp/logaddexp2 forward + skip dead no-grad saves
description: tensor_logaddexp_custom calls apply_function unconditionally: serial .iter().zip().map() transcendental forward (powf/log2 or exp/ln per elem = compute-bound) PLUS two unconditional save_for_backward clones (a_vals.to_vec()+b_vals.to_vec()=2x numel). Same anti-pattern as logcumsumexp (d96cda0a, 4.04x). Two levers, bit-exact: (1) par_zip_map_f64 over elements (order-preserving, gated PARALLEL_ELEMENTWISE_MIN); (2) gate the 2 saves on ctx.needs_input_grad().any() so no-grad inference skips the dead tape clones. Grad path byte-for-byte unchanged.
close_reason: Shipped 6299d597: par_zip_map_f64 over elements + gated save_for_backward on needs_input_grad().any(). A/B n=4e6 64t: 86.7ms->37.3ms = 2.32x (2.29-2.45x), bit-for-bit equal to serial reference; ft-api --lib logaddexp 9/9 pass incl grad-edge tests. Grad path byte-for-byte unchanged.

title: EmbeddingBag.forward_with_offsets severs autograd
description:
EmbeddingBag::forward_with_offsets in ft-nn (line 2366) extracts tensor_values from indices, offsets, weight, and per_sample_weights, computes the bag aggregation in plain f64, and rebuilds via session.tensor_variable(output, vec![num_bags, dim], false). This silently severs autograd through the embedding weight AND through per_sample_weights — breaking training for any model using nn.EmbeddingBag (NLP classification, recommendation systems, fastText-style models).

Fix: compose via tensor_index_select + tensor_scatter_add (or matmul with a precomputed indicator matrix). Strategy:
1. Look up rows: emb = tensor_index_select(weight, 0, indices) → [N, D]
2. If per_sample_weights: emb = emb * psw_broadcast (autograd-aware)
3. Per-bag aggregation:
   - Build a [N] non-grad bag_assignment tensor: bag_assignment[i] = which bag i belongs to
   - For Sum: scatter_add into [num_bags, D] zeros. Mean: divide by bag_size after.
   - For Max: needs different routing (gather max per bag) — defer or composed via softmax-based approximation.
4. Handle padding_idx by zeroing out those rows pre-aggregation via mask.

Affects: crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs EmbeddingBag::forward_with_offsets (line 2366).

title: [parity-gap] Add nn.CircularPad3d module parity

Acceptance: add a CircularPad3d module with PyTorch padding order (left, right, top, bottom, front, back), wrap indices along W/H/D using modular circular padding, compose through tensor_index_select along W/H/D so gradients flow to the input, reject rank < 3 and zero spatial dimensions, add forward/error/gradient/no-parameter tests, and validate via focused ft-nn rch gates.
close_reason: Implemented CircularPad3d parity with PyTorch padding order, modular W/H/D wrapping, autograd-aware index_select composition, and forward/error/gradient/no-parameter tests.

title: [code-review] ConvTranspose2d/3d output-shape arithmetic rejects valid padding or underflows
description: [HIGH] Review of recent ConvTranspose1d bias work exposed the same output-length surface in adjacent transposed-conv implementations. In crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs, ConvTranspose2d computes h_span/w_span, rejects whenever span < 2*padding, then adds kernel/output_padding. That over-rejects valid PyTorch-style cases such as H_in=1, kernel=3, padding=1, output_padding=0 where output size should be 1. ConvTranspose3d still computes (dim-1)*stride - 2*padding + kernel + output_padding directly with usize arithmetic, so padding-erased outputs can panic/underflow instead of returning AutogradError. Fix should use checked arithmetic in mathematical order: span + kernel + output_padding, then subtract 2*padding only if the result stays positive, with focused tests for valid padding-preserved and invalid padding-erased shapes.
close_reason: Fixed ConvTranspose2d/3d output-shape arithmetic and singleton padded-output regressions

Add this narrow F64 autograd path while keeping larger Cholesky shapes fail-loud.

title: ft-nn Bilinear severs autograd; composable through outer + reshape + matmul
description:
Bilinear at lib.rs:987 (forward_bilinear) computes y[b, k] = sum_{i,j} x1[b, i] * W[k, i, j] * x2[b, j] manually in plain f64, then rebuilds requires_grad=false leaf and adds optional bias on top. Severs autograd through x1/x2/W.

Composable via outer-product + matmul:
   x1_3d = unsqueeze(x1, 2)             # [batch, in1, 1]
   x2_3d = unsqueeze(x2, 1)             # [batch, 1, in2]
   outer = x1_3d * x2_3d                # [batch, in1, in2]
   flat  = reshape(outer, [batch, in1*in2])
   W_flat_t = transpose(reshape(W, [out, in1*in2]), 0, 1)
   y = matmul(flat, W_flat_t)           # [batch, out]
   reshape to original output shape, then add bias.

All ops autograd-aware so gradients flow to x1 / x2 / W / bias.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Composed Bilinear through reshape + unsqueeze + expand + mul + reshape + transpose + matmul + add (bias). The bilinear formula y_k = x1^T W_k x2 + b_k is reformulated as y = vec(x1 outer x2) @ vec(W).T + b, which composes through autograd-aware primitives. ft-nn lib tests: 517.

title: ft-api: implement torch.linalg.ldl_factor / ldl_solve (Bunch-Kaufman LDL^T)
description: torch.linalg.ldl_factor and ldl_solve (the LDL^T symmetric-indefinite factorization, LAPACK dsytrf/dsytrs) were the LAST missing torch.linalg primitive. Implemented our own safe-Rust LAPACK dsytf2 (lower, partial Bunch-Kaufman pivoting with the alpha=(1+sqrt17)/8 threshold, 1x1 and 2x2 pivot blocks, symmetric interchanges) + dsytrs solve, as non-grad F64 2-D session ops + functional aliases. Returns (LD packed, IPIV pivots) bit-matching LAPACK/torch convention (positive=1x1 with interchange, paired negatives=2x2 block). Prototyped in Python vs torch first (matches piv+LD exactly across SPD/diagonal/indefinite-2x2 cases), then translated. Verified vs torch 2.x goldens + A@X==B reconstruction.

Own safe-Rust Bunch-Kaufman LDL^T: tensor_linalg_ldl_factor (dsytf2 lower, partial pivoting, 1x1+2x2 blocks, LAPACK IPIV) + tensor_linalg_ldl_solve (dsytrs) + functional aliases. Prototyped vs torch first (piv+LD exact across SPD/diag/indefinite-2x2), translated, verified vs torch 2.x goldens + A@X==B recon to <1e-11. Last missing torch.linalg primitive. ft-api suite 2040 pass.

title: tensor_kron generic path allocates a Vec per inner iteration (perf regression)
description:
fa1cf3d generalized tensor_kron to arbitrary ranks but the new generic path calls decode_coords inside the doubly-nested loop over a_vals x b_vals, allocating a fresh Vec<usize> of length rank for EACH iteration. For a 2D x 2D kron of 100x100 inputs that is 100M Vec allocations replacing what was a constant-time inner loop in the previous specialized 2D path.

Fix sketch: precompute strides for a_aligned, b_aligned, and out_shape ONCE before the loop. Inside the inner loop iterate over a_linear and b_linear directly, accumulating out_linear via stride math without intermediate coordinate Vecs. The stride for out at dim d is product(out_shape[d+1..]); a_aligned coordinate d contributes a_coord*b_aligned[d]*stride_out[d]+b_coord*stride_out[d] which can be precomputed as a per-(a_linear,b_linear) table.

Severity: MEDIUM. Functionality is correct; this is a hot-loop allocation regression that did not exist in the prior specialized 2D path. Stalls a primary tensor algebra op for any non-trivial input size.

Reproduction: cargo bench (or a hand-rolled timing harness) on tensor_kron of two 64x64 inputs vs the pre-fa1cf3d code.
close_reason: Hoisted decode_coords Vec allocation out of the doubly-nested kron loop by precomputing a_strides and b_strides via contiguous_strides() ONCE before the outer loop. Per-iteration heap allocation eliminated; output coordinates derived in-place from a_linear/b_linear via integer division/modulo. Existing 5 kron tests still pass; new kron_4d_matches_explicit_index_decomposition regression added to lock the stride-based output layout against future drift. ft-api 1087 tests pass; clippy + ubs clean.

title: ft-kernel-cpu: Golub-Kahan bidiagonal SVD to replace one-sided Jacobi
description: svd_tall uses one-sided Jacobi (up-to-100 O(m n^2) sweeps). Baseline bench: 256x128 1.169s, 256x256 ~16s/iter. Two eigensolver-shortcut levers tried and REJECTED: (1) bare Gram A^TA + eigh = 67x but FAILS U orthonormality (U^TU off by 1.8e-2 at 40x24) — condition-number squaring corrupts small-sigma U directions; (2) Gram-warm-started Jacobi = accurate but only 2.1x tall / ~1.4x square (sub-2.0, convergence checks dominate). The correct accurate-and-fast lever is Golub-Kahan-Reinsch: Householder bidiagonalization A->B (upper bidiagonal, accumulate U,V) + implicit-shift QR sweep on the bidiagonal with Givens rotations (no A^TA, no condition squaring). Target >=5x at 256x256 with full accuracy. Parity = structural (U^TU=I, VV^T=I, U diag(s) V^T = A) per the new svd_tall_orthonormal_and_reconstructs_40x24 test. Reuse the Householder machinery from eigh_tred2 + qr_contiguous_f64.
close_reason: done

description: The double-backward finders verified f'' for the special functions and diff_probe verified VALUE parity, but 1st-order gradient (backward) VALUE parity vs torch.autograd was not explicitly pinned for the differentiable torch.special ops. Verified this session: ft gradients match torch.autograd for i0/i1/i0e/i1e/erf/erfc/erfinv/erfcx/ndtr/log_ndtr/gammaln/digamma/entr at x=[0.1,0.3,0.5,0.7,0.9]. Add a regression test pinning torch goldens. title: tensor_inner is severed-autograd: composable through dot/matmul
description:
tensor_inner at lib.rs:15480 contracts the last dim of two tensors:
   c[..a, j_a, ..b, j_b] = sum_l a[..a, l] * b[..b, l]
where a has shape [a_outer..., a_last] and b has shape [b_outer..., b_last] with a_last == b_last. The forward extracts values, computes the contraction in plain f64, and rebuilds a requires_grad=false leaf. PyTorch's torch.inner is differentiable.

Compose through autograd primitives:
- 1-D case: delegate to tensor_dot (already autograd-aware).
- Higher-D: reshape both inputs to 2-D (a_outer, a_last) and (b_outer, b_last), compute matmul(a_flat, b_flat.T), reshape to the inner output shape. matmul + transpose + reshape are all autograd-aware.

Surfaced during /reality-check pass continuing from sirh fix.
notes:
Fixed in this commit. Composed tensor_inner through autograd primitives:
  - 1-D × 1-D: delegates to tensor_dot (autograd-aware via tape).
  - Higher-D: reshape both inputs to 2-D (outer, last), transpose b, matmul, reshape to inner output shape. matmul + transpose + reshape are all autograd-aware.
The output shape contract ([1] for 1-D inputs that previously returned shape [1]) is preserved via tensor_unsqueeze on the 0-D scalar tensor_dot returns. Added inner_1d_propagates_gradient_via_dot regression test ([1,2,3] · [4,5,6] = 32 → grad_a = b, grad_b = a). ft-api lib tests: 1150.

Fixed with a scatter-add backward (grad to each looked-up row, accumulating repeated indices, padding rows excluded). Forward bit-identical. Verified scatter + padding. frankentorch-bug.

title: [perf][no-gaps] svd_f64_256x256 ~1.58 SECONDS — ~100x slower than LAPACK gesdd
description: PROFILE-BACKED (linalg_bench vmi1227854, reproduced 1.578s/1.632s): svd_f64_256x256 ~1.58s vs LAPACK dgesdd ~10-20ms = ~80-150x gap — the BIGGEST vs-upstream perf gap found. (Contrast svd_f64_256x128 ~3-7ms, so the SQUARE/large case explodes.) ft uses Golub-Kahan bidiagonalization + implicit-QR shifts (svd_contiguous_f64, ft-kernel-cpu ~lib.rs 10881). Likely O(n^4) or pathological iteration/convergence on square matrices, or full U/V accumulation with naive O(n^3) loops per sweep. INVESTIGATE: (1) is the implicit-QR shift converging (iteration count)? (2) are the U/V rotation accumulations serial naive loops (parallelizable bit-exact like the eig Hessenberg win c7973178)? (3) is bidiagonalization blocked? Deep levers parallel to l9xod (eig). Profile iteration counts first; a bit-exact parallelization of the rotation-accumulation may be a large quick win, then GEMM-blocked bidiag for the rest.
notes: DIAGNOSTIC (release, 256x256): degenerate bench matrix (rank<=97) = 1.55s; WELL-CONDITIONED full-rank = 341ms (bench)/227ms (single-run). So TWO gaps: (A) GENERAL ~15-20x vs LAPACK even well-conditioned (Golub-Reinsch BLAS-1 rotation stream; needs D&C or BLAS-3 back-transform — multi-session); (B) ~5-12x convergence pathology on rank-deficient/clustered spectra (the bench's degenerate input). Added representative bench svd_f64_256x256_wellcond (6a70c46e) so the fast-path/parallelization win is measurable on real input (the degenerate bench masks the bidiag fraction — which is why the PERF NOTE found bidiag-parallel 'regressed': measured on the pathological-sweep input). NOTE: a peer (RubyLotus) has svd_normal_equations_reduced (A^T A eigenproblem fast path, n>=64, residual+orthogonality validity gate) IN FLIGHT in lib.rs — that directly targets gap (A) for well-conditioned reduced SVD; my wellcond bench measures exactly it.

Score 0.0. Source hunk removed; evidence in artifacts/perf/frankentorch-yyylo/report.md. Follow-up deeper primitive filed as frankentorch-91dva.

title: ft-autograd: enable double-backward (create_graph) for Sort, TopK, Fmod, Remainder
description: backward_create_graph fail-loud errored for Sort, TopK, Fmod, Remainder, blocking second-order autograd through ranking/sorting and modular ops. Sort/TopK backward is a pure linear scatter of the incoming gradient by stored indices -> route through differentiable scatter_add(zeros, incoming, dim, indices, output_shape) (same pattern as Gather/IndexSelect), so double-backward routes through the permutation/selection. Fmod/Remainder: d/da=1 passthrough (route incoming_id), d/db=-trunc(a/b) [fmod] / -floor(a/b) [remainder] piecewise-constant factor gated via cg_mul. Tests: create_graph_sort_second_derivative_routes_through_permutation (L=sum(sort(x)^2), grad=2x, d2=2), create_graph_topk_second_derivative_routes_through_selection, create_graph_fmod_gradients, create_graph_remainder_gradients.

title: [test] autograd multi-path gradient accumulation + detach stops-gradient invariants
description: Metamorphic autograd invariants (no torch goldens): a variable used on multiple paths accumulates the SUM of path gradients — d/dx[sum(x)+sum(x*x)] = 1+2x; detach(x) contributes zero gradient — d/dx[sum(x*x)+sum(detach(x)*detach(x))] = 2x. Pins gradient accumulation + detach semantics.

Do not retry key materialization, sorted-map construction, delayed materialization, rank-1 metadata, or scalar payload collect micro-levers. Attack a fundamentally different primitive: safe-Rust owned batch/slab native decode storage for many small tensors, likely spanning ft-core DenseTensor storage representation and ft-serialize native decode. Preserve public BTreeMap<String, DenseTensor> ordering, duplicate-key failure behavior, malformed diagnostics, exact f64 raw bits, dtype/shape contracts, and golden SHA. Baseline via RCH first, one lever only, same-worker rebench, keep only Score >=2.0 / target >=2.0x.

title: [parity] sign(NaN)=NaN but torch returns 0.0 (corrects prior mis-verification)
description: Differential probe vs torch 2.12 (bulletproof: torch.isnan(sign(nan))=False, sign(nan)==0 True, f64+f32): torch.sign/sgn of NaN returns 0.0 (ATen uses (0<x)-(x<0); NaN fails both -> 0). ft's torch_sign_f64/torch_sign_f32 (ft-kernel-cpu ~716/~13495) explicitly force NaN->NaN, and tests sign_scalar_propagates_nan + sign_tensor_contiguous_propagates_nan pin the wrong value. This CORRECTS a prior 'verified correct' note (wfyq got signed-zero right but NaN wrong). Fix: replace the body with if x>0 {1} else if x<0 {-1} else {0} (NaN and +-0 -> 0, matching torch's (x>0)-(x<0)); update the 2 tests to assert sign(NaN)=0. Affects tensor_sign + all callers. Probe: crates/ft-api/examples/diff_probe.rs sign row.
close_reason: Fixed in 6a6e7dd1: torch_sign_f64/_f32 now compute (x>0)?1:(x<0)?-1:0, so sign(NaN)=0 and sign(+-0)=0 matching torch 2.12 (torch.sign=(0<x)-(x<0); bulletproof-verified isnan(sign(nan))=False). Corrects a prior mis-verification (NaN->NaN). 4 sign kernel + 23 ft-api sign/sgn tests pass; diff_probe sign row matches torch. Pushed main+master. Peer eig WIP left untouched (surgical stage).

title: [bug][parity] ft-api isin: fuzzy <EPSILON match (should be exact) + f64-only (errored on f32)
close_reason: Shipped 8d20341c: isin now uses exact == (was fuzzy <EPSILON, mismatching torch + the unique precedent) and reads via tensor_values_lossy_f64 (accepts f32, was f64-only/errored). Kept the vectorizing per-element scan — a hash-set O(n+m) version measured SLOWER (782us vs 355us @ n=m=10000; scan auto-vectorizes, hash pays hashing+cache-miss overhead). Test isin_exact_match_and_f32_support passes; ft-api 1890 green. Pushed main+master.

title: perf(ft-kernel-cpu): packed-panel register-blocked GEMM microkernel (bit-exact, target 1.5-2x)
description:
BOTH GEMM APPROACHES MEASURED BELOW VIABLE — closing 2026-06-16 (cc):
(1) CUSTOM packed-panel micro-kernel: a safe-Rust register-blocked kernel is ~12x
    slower per-core than matrixmultiply (4.5 vs 54.2 GF/s, gemm_microkernel_probe.rs
    471fbf19). Even perfect scaling nets ~6x slower overall. DEAD.
(2) RE-TILING around matrixmultiply (improve the ~54% multi-core scaling, keep the
    fast AVX2 micro-kernel): the scaling probe measured 5.4x of 10 threads, so even
    PERFECT scaling caps the improvement at 10/5.4 = ~1.85x — BELOW the Score>=2.0
    bar, and it risks the dgemm_bt-vs-transpose path-equality test in peer-contended
    ft-kernel-cpu. Sub-threshold micro-tuning (NO-CEILING addendum says don't ship
    these).
CONCLUSION: matrixmultiply (pure-Rust AVX2) is already the optimal per-core GEMM
reachable in #![forbid(unsafe_code)] safe Rust; the residual multi-core-scaling gap
is structural to parallelizing a single-threaded optimal kernel by output-tiling and
cannot reach >=2x. The MKL-parity gap (3-4.5x) needs MKL-class hand-tuned
multi-threaded assembly, which the safe-Rust mandate precludes — and we already use
the best pure-Rust kernel. No >=2.0 GEMM lever exists. Closing wontfix.
close_reason: done

title: ft-api: f32 dtype-parity gaps in quantile/cummax/cummin/take/trapz
description:
Probe batch-2 found more value-reading ops that error or upcast on f32 (torch preserves input float dtype):
- quantile: ERR UnsupportedDType(F32)
- cummax / cummin: ERR UnsupportedDType(F32) (scan-with-indices, like max_dim b3fg; values should be f32, indices int64/F64)
- take: ERR UnsupportedDType(F32) (gather of input elements -> input dtype)
- trapz: OK but returns F64 (should be f32)
count_nonzero returns F64 but that is a COUNT (torch int64) -> codebase int-as-F64 convention, NOT a bug. Same fk5l/b3fg vein; fix via lossy reads + dtype-matched output (cummax/cummin like max_dim values-storage; take via index_select-style gather).
close_reason: Shipped 2735f8d6: cummax/cummin now lossy-read + gather via index_select (dtype+grad preserving, indices stay F64); quantile nan-check lossy + scalar_leaf_matching_dtype early-out; trapz/trapezoid/cumulative_trapezoid use full_like for the 0.5 factor. take was NOT a bug (index_select preserves input dtype; probe passed f32 indices which are correctly unsupported). count_nonzero returns a count (int64->F64 convention), not a dtype bug. Test f32_scan_quantile_trapz_preserve_dtype (dtype + golden + f64 no-regression + f32 grad). ft-api lib 1902 pass.

title: masked_scatter all-false mask skips source validation
description: Reviewer finding in crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs live masked_scatter rewrite. tensor_masked_scatter now returns Ok(input) when n_true == 0 before validating source dtype/device compatibility. Repro from code inspection: call tensor_masked_scatter with an F64 input, an all-zero mask with matching numel, and an incompatible source tensor such as F32. The new fast path returns the input unchanged instead of failing closed; with any true mask the path reaches tensor_where and rejects mismatched dtypes. This makes validation data-dependent on mask contents and can silently accept unsupported source tensors. Fix: validate source dtype/device against input before the n_true == 0 fast path, and add a regression test for all-false mask plus incompatible source.
close_reason: Hoisted dtype + device validation for input/mask/source above the n_true==0 fast path in tensor_masked_scatter, so the fast and slow paths share the same up-front contract regardless of mask contents. Added masked_scatter_all_false_mask_still_validates_source_dtype regression test.

title: functional_conv_transpose1d / 2d sever autograd via tensor_values + non-grad rebuild
description:
ft-api functional_conv_transpose1d (line 5866) and functional_conv_transpose2d (line 5985) both extract input/weight tensor_values, run the transposed convolution in plain f64, and rebuild via tensor_variable(_, _, false). Silently severs autograd through both input and weight — breaks training for upsampling models, U-Net decoders, GAN generators, segmentation networks.

The corresponding ConvTranspose1d / ConvTranspose2d nn.Module impls already compose correctly via tensor_narrow + tensor_squeeze + tensor_matmul + tensor_unsqueeze + tensor_pad + tensor_add (transposed convolution as scatter-and-accumulate). Mirror the same pattern in the functional ops.

Affects: crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs functional_conv_transpose1d (line 5866), functional_conv_transpose2d (line 5985).

Kept lever: borrow f64 input/weight/bias contiguous slices directly from TensorTape in the no-grad Linear fast path, preserving the existing native ft-kernel-cpu dgemm_bt arithmetic and avoiding per-call Vec clones of the activation, weight, and bias. Proof: RCH check -p ft-api --lib --benches passed; focused f64 no-grad Linear test compares fused borrowed path to explicit weight.transpose(0,1)+addmm by f64::to_bits. No ordering/tie/RNG changes. After medians: 198.70us, 438.43us, 477.64us, 900.78us (1.19x, 1.14x, 2.43x, 2.36x).

RCH vmi1227854 Criterion medians linear_forward/hidden: 256 236.40us->198.70us (1.190x), 512 498.36us->438.43us (1.137x), 1024 1.1583ms->477.64us (2.425x), 2048 2.1266ms->900.78us (2.361x). Behavior: same linear_tensor_f64/dgemm_bt arithmetic; focused RCH bit-exact test passed by f64::to_bits().

title: Fix poisson_nll_loss autograd via tensor primitives [child of 2med]
description:
poisson_nll_loss at lib.rs:10357 computes elementwise:
  log_input=True:  loss_i = exp(inp_i) - tgt_i * inp_i
  log_input=False: loss_i = inp_i - tgt_i * log(inp_i + eps)
plus optional Stirling correction tgt*log(tgt) - tgt + 0.5*log(2*pi*tgt) for full=true.

Composable through autograd primitives — the Stirling correction is a function of target only (no input gradient) and can be precomputed as a non-grad scalar tensor and added back in.
notes:
Fixed in this commit. Composed poisson_nll_loss through autograd primitives:
  log_input=true:  exp(input) - target * input → mean
  log_input=false: input - target * log(input + eps) → mean
The Stirling correction (full=true) depends only on target, so we precompute its mean as a non-grad scalar and add via tensor_add. Added poisson_nll_loss_propagates_gradient_through_input regression test (input=[1,2], target=[1,1] → analytical [(e-1)/2, (e^2-1)/2]). ft-api lib tests: 1157.

title: porting: add tensor_sinc with safe-zero handling for torch.sinc parity
description: torch.sinc(x) = sin(pi*x) / (pi*x) for x != 0, 1 at x = 0. Compose via tensor_sin + tensor_div with safe-zero handling: build a mask = (x == 0), use a safe denominator that's pi*x except where x=0 (use 1 to avoid 0/0), then where(mask, ones, sin_term / safe_denom). Gradient at x=0 is 0 (correct limit) since the where routes to the ones constant which has no requires_grad. Useful for signal processing / interpolation.
close_reason: Added tensor_sinc with safe-zero handling matching torch.sinc. Composes through tensor_mul (pi*x) + tensor_sin + tensor_div with a safe-denominator guard (where x=0 the denominator becomes 1 to avoid 0/0; the result is then masked back to 1 via tensor_where). Gradient at x=0 is correctly 0 since the final where routes that position to a no-grad ones constant. 3 unit tests cover known values (0/0.5/1/2), zero-input no-NaN (the safe-denom guard), and analytic gradient regression (0 at x=0, -4/π at x=0.5). ft-api now at 1286 lib tests.

Acceptance: add ft-nn modules ConstantPad3d, ZeroPad1d, and ZeroPad3d using PyTorch F.pad ordering (left, right, top, bottom, front, back), expose zero-pad aliases through constant pad, add forward/no-parameter tests, and validate via focused ft-nn rch gates.

title: Harden unfold/renorm parameter validation

title: tensor_linalg_svd doesn't complete orthonormal basis for zero singular vectors
description: On rank-deficient matrices, FrankenTorch's tensor_linalg_svd returns U columns corresponding to zero singular values as all-zeros instead of orthonormal vectors completing the basis. numpy.linalg.svd (and torch.linalg.svd) always produce a fully orthonormal U with U^T U = I_k, regardless of rank. Repro: 3x3 matrix [[1,2,3],[4,5,6],[7,8,9]] (rank 2). numpy returns U[:,2] as a unit vector orthogonal to col([1,2,3],[4,5,6]); FrankenTorch returns U[:,2] = 0. Surfaced by torch_linalg_svd_numpy_subprocess_conformance during the frankentorch-c36b work; current conformance test trims the rank-deficient case to land. Severity: medium — breaks the U^T U = I invariant that downstream code (e.g., least-squares via SVD) may rely on; doesn't affect the U @ diag(S) @ Vh reconstruction.
notes: Fixed in this commit: unified the orthonormal-basis completion in svd_tall to handle both (a) rank-deficient inputs (zero singular values) and (b) full_matrices mode with m > k. Any column of U that ends up with norm <= tol now gets a unit vector orthogonal to all previously-set columns via Gram-Schmidt against e_0..e_{m-1}, retrying basis vectors as needed. Restored the rank_deficient_3x3 case in both torch_linalg_svd_numpy_subprocess_conformance and torch_linalg_pinv_numpy_subprocess_conformance — both pass.

title: Add metamorphic property tests for index_select identity, relu decomposition, cumsum/diff round-trip
description:
Extend the testing-metamorphic pass with four additional property tests in ft-conformance::tests under #[cfg(feature=fuzz)]:

1. fuzz_metamorphic_index_select_sequential_is_identity — for any 1-D x of length n, index_select(x, dim=0, [0, 1, ..., n-1]) must equal x bit-exactly.

2. fuzz_metamorphic_relu_decomposition — for any x, relu(x) - relu(-x) == x bit-exactly. relu(x) + relu(-x) == abs(x) for x != 0; both are 0 at x = 0.

3. fuzz_metamorphic_cumsum_diff_round_trip — diff(cumsum(x)) == x[1:] bit-exactly (since both are pure addition/subtraction of the same values).

4. fuzz_metamorphic_pow_two_equals_self_mul — pow(x, 2.0) and x * x must agree within a few ULPs (tensor_pow may use exp(2*log(|x|)) for general exponents, but should special-case integer exponents OR at least match within a small ULP envelope).

title: [perf][no-gaps] fql10-C6: blocked/tiled shadow Francis sweep grouping from scalar-complete ledger
description: Successor to frankentorch-7ew8d. Profile evidence remains the non-symmetric Francis QR floor: x9137 hz2 baseline eigvals_f64_256x256 [26.396 ms 26.500 ms 26.737 ms], eig_f64_256x256 [53.720 ms 55.281 ms 57.322 ms], profile n=256 sweeps=319 defl1=14 defl2=121 fallback=0 exceptional=0 and n=1024 sweeps=1132 defl1=18 defl2=503 fallback=0 exceptional=0. 7ew8d completed the scalar-complete private replay ledger: capture after spike cleanup, record/replay h[k,k-1] subdiagonal overwrites, focused shadow replay tests pass, broad eig tests pass, strict eigvals_golden SHA 24ed0e24afc1b41d3b23198f60fc1d06727374bf3551c026941a25785b7c9725. Build the next primitive: blocked/tiled grouping inside the private shadow lane only, using the scalar-complete ledger as oracle. Acceptance: prove identical EigFrancisShiftSample stream, selected-m stream, active-window stream, deflation counters, full Schur buffer, eigenvalue/eigenvector bits, complex-pair slot order, RNG absence, and strict golden SHA before any public dispatch; then immediate same-worker before/after Criterion via rch; keep only Score>=2.0. Do not wire public dispatch until the private grouped lane is bit-exact.
notes: Correction after closeout evidence sweep: artifacts/perf/frankentorch-zu2wo/pass1_rebench_eig_hz2.log contains a comparable hz2 after benchmark. title: [parity-gap] torch math functions: frexp, gcd, lcm
description:
Missing math functions from torch:

- torch.frexp(input): Decomposes input into mantissa and exponent
- torch.gcd(input, other): Element-wise greatest common divisor
- torch.lcm(input, other): Element-wise least common multiple

frexp returns a tuple (mantissa, exponent) where input = mantissa * 2^exponent.
gcd and lcm operate on integer tensors.

Ref: https://pytorch.org/docs/stable/torch.html
close_reason: done

title: ft-kernel-cpu: matrix_exp Taylor[12]->Pade[6/6] to cut matmul count
description: matrix_exp now uses fast dgemm (frankentorch-7bj2) but still does a 13-term Taylor series = 12 matmuls per scaling step. Pade[6/6] (Higham scaling-and-squaring) needs only ~4 matmuls (A2=A*A, A4=A2*A2, A6=A4*A2, U=A*(c1 I+c3 A2+c5 A4)) + 1 LU solve q^-1 p, q=V-U, p=V+U, V=c0 I+c2 A2+c4 A4+c6 A6. Coeffs c=[1,1/2,5/44,1/66,1/792,1/15840,1/665280]. ~2-3x fewer matmuls AND better accuracy than Taylor. Reuse lu_factor_contiguous_f64 + lu_solve_contiguous_f64 for the solve (column by column or matrix solve). Parity tolerance-based; value-parity test compares grad/non-grad (both kernel) so safe. Target additional ~2x on top of the dgemm win.
notes: REJECTED: Pade[6/6] (4 matmuls + LU solve) is SLOWER than the shipped dgemm-Taylor (12 parallel matmuls). A/B 128x128: dgemm-Taylor 1.68ms vs Pade 4.74ms; 256x256 15.0ms vs 21.9ms. Root cause: the naive single-threaded lu_solve dominates; cutting parallel dgemm matmuls does not help when the solve is serial. Real lever is parallelizing the triangular solve (see new bead).
close_reason: Rejected current Padé[6/6] dirty implementation: remote rch Criterion on vmi1227854 measured matrix_exp_f64_128x128 [4.1882 ms 4.7257 ms 5.1411 ms] and matrix_exp_f64_256x256 [22.785 ms 24.146 ms 26.113 ms], regressing against the frankentorch-7bj2 dgemm baseline recorded at 128x128 1.67 ms and 256x256 13.24 ms. Source diff was not committed by BoldOx and is left untouched; next matrix_exp pass should replace the solve-heavy Pade[6/6] path with a deeper primitive or optimized solve strategy, not keep this lever.
